<font face="$HandwrittenFont"> 荣誉执行令：<Alias=player>
根据莫拉格堂公会的合法传统与惯例，该人士确认为非法刺客组织成员。执此文件者具有官方批准杀死该人士的许可。

String.Source: I discovered Tilisu Severin visiting the Ulen Ancestral Tomb at night. This seems suspicious, as she doesn't belong to the Ulen family by name. I should bring this piece of evidence to Adril Arano immediately.
String.Dest: 我发现了蒂莉苏·瑟佛林在晚上拜访了乌伦祖坟。这看来很可疑，因为在姓氏上她不属于乌伦家。我该马上把这份证据告知阿德尔·阿拉诺。

String.Source: You can summon Karstaag 1 more time.
String.Dest: 你还可以再召唤卡斯塔格1次。

String.Source:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont" size="18"> This is the third letter I am attempting to send to the Imperial Council in Cyrodiil for guidance in the matter surrounding the threat to Fort Frostmoth.
A sizable dark elf force has established a stronghold on the southwestern portion of Solstheim. I've given them ample opportunity to surrender, but all attempts to communicate with them have met with conflict.
I will continue to do whatever's necessary to ensure the safety of Fort Frostmoth and rid Solstheim of these invaders until I receive orders stating otherwise.
General Falx Carius Garrison Commander, Fort Frostmoth </font>
String.Dest:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont" size="18"> 这是我试图寄给希罗帝尔帝国议会的第三封信，以征求对霜蛾堡周边威胁的指示。
一支庞大的暗精灵部队在索瑟海姆西南部建立了要塞。我给了他们足够的投降机会，但所有沟通尝试均以冲突告终。
在新的命令到来前，我将继续采取所有必要措施，确保霜蛾堡的安全，并铲除来到索瑟海姆的入侵者。
霜蛾堡指挥官 法克斯·卡里乌斯将军 </font>

String.Source: Flawless Amethyst Gem Damaged Albino Spider Pod
String.Dest: 无瑕紫水晶 受损的白化蜘蛛卵囊

String.Source: <font face="$HandwrittenFont"> They said it was impossible. They, not me. I knew! The book knew. It knows all. And I know all. All I need to know. To fly! They laughed. The others were afraid. Don't even try, they said. They said it was impossible. They, not me. The book is filled with knowledge. The book fills me. With knowledge. Secret hidden knowledge. My knowledge. I must hide the knowledge. Hide it away deep inside. I will eat the book. A page at a time. Slowly. Slowly. I will become one with the knowledge contained on its pages. In its pages. In me! The knowledge of flight. They said it was impossible. Ha!
String.Dest: <font face="$HandwrittenFont"> 他们都说不可能。是他们，不是我。我明白！这书上也是这么写的！它写的，我都知道。我现在需要知道。飞！他们笑。还有人害怕。他们连试试都不敢。他们都说不可能。是他们，不是我。这书里都是知识。这本书已经把我装满了。我有知识了。秘密就藏在知识里。是我的知识。我必须把知识藏起来。藏得要多深有多深。我把书吃掉。一次吃一页。慢。慢慢的。我就和知识融为一体了。就在这页上。在我身体里！有飞翔的知识。他们都说不可能。哈！

String.Source: Summons Karstaag to fight for you for <120> seconds. You may only use this ability <3> times, and only while outdoors.
String.Dest: 召唤卡斯塔格为你战斗<120>秒。你还能使用该能力<3>次。此能力只能在室外使用。

String.Source:
The True Noble's Code by Serjo Athyn Sarethi

[pagebreak]

he honorable warriors of the Great House Redoran are the hereditary defenders of the Morrowind. To be a noble of House Redoran is more than being a great warrior. One must follow the triune virtues of duty, gravity, and piety. A Redoran's duty is first to the Tribunal Temple, second to the Great House Redoran, and third to one's family and clan. In the Battle of Red Mountain, warriors of House Redoran died bravely for their duty to the Tribunal. By defending House Redoran from the schemes of Telvanni wizards and the lies of untrustworthy Hlaalu, the true noble shows duty to House Redoran. Following the Temple's guidelines of mercy and generosity show duty to one's family and clan. A Redoran noble must know the virtue of gravity. It is not the Redoran way to laugh at serious matters, for it shows disrespect. It is not the Redoran way to spread rumors, for they fester and breed dissension. A Redoran must show piety to the Aedra and Daedra, our creators and ancestors. For without the divine, we would not have the chance to serve. And without divine law, we would not know right from wrong. And without giving thanks for these things, we would forget our place and our purpose. Great House Redoran praises all the skills of war. Not because we believe war is good or honorable in its own right, but because this knowledge is necessary to perform one's duty. House Redoran's warrior fight with a long blade and a shield or with a spear. A noble of House Redoran must also learn to use a bow and must be athletic enough for the long marches to battle. A Redoran wears heavy or medium armor depending on rank and strategy. A noble of House Redoran is expected to know how to repair and maintain his own armor. Those who are born to House Redoran have been taught their skill and virtues by kin and clan. Those who seek to enter House Redoran as retainers must satisfy an examiner in the Redoran Council Hall that their skills are suitable for service to House Redoran. Whether born to the blood of House Redoran, or adopted into service of House Redoran by oath, those who seek to advance in the ranks of House Redoran must demonstrate their virtues by service and obedience. And only when one has mastered all the skills and virtues can one truly call himself a noble of the Great House Redoran.
String.Dest:
真贵族的规范 瑟乔 阿辛·萨雷锡 著

[pagebreak]

瑞多然大家族的光荣战士们是晨风世世代代的捍卫者。瑞多然的贵族不仅要是一名强大的战士，更应该具有责任、严肃与虔诚三位一体的美德。 一个瑞多然人的首要职责在于对审判席神殿尽忠，其次是要守护瑞多然大家族，最后是对自己的家庭与小家族。红山之战中，瑞多然的战士们为履行对审判席的职责而英勇牺牲。在泰瓦尼巫师的阴谋与不可信任的哈拉鲁的谎言中保护了瑞多然家族，真正的贵族们展示了他们对瑞多然家族的责任。同时，遵循着神殿仁慈与慷慨的教诲，彰显着对家庭与小家族的责任。 身为瑞多然贵族，必须明白严肃的价值。对严肃之事置之一笑甚为不尊，非瑞多然处世之道。瑞多然人面对谣言不可胡乱传说，致使分歧滋生。 瑞多然人必须向我们的创造者与祖先的伊德拉和迪德拉表达虔诚。如无神，我们也不会有效忠服务的机会。如无神之律法，我们也无法分辨是非。若不对他们心存感激，就会忘却自己身处的位置及人生的目的。 瑞多然大家族赞颂一切战争技巧。我们并非认为战争本身是好事或者光荣，而是因为这种认知对履行职责来说是必要的。瑞多然家族的战士擅长用长剑盾牌或长矛作战。瑞多然家族的贵族还需要娴熟弓箭，善于运动以长途奔袭。重甲与中甲的选择取决于家族等级和战术需要。瑞多然的贵族也必须懂得修理和保养盔甲。 生于家族的瑞多然人已经受到过亲人和家族的训导。那些寻求受聘进入瑞多然家的门客则需要通过瑞多然议会大厅考官的测试，以确保他们的技能适合为瑞多然家族服务。 无论身负瑞多然家族的血统，还是通过宣誓成为家族的一员，想要在家族中获得晋升者必须通过服务与服从来展示自己的美德。只有一个人掌握了所有的技能和美德，才能真正称自己为瑞多然大家族的贵族。

String.Source: I helped the warriors of Thirsk Mead Hall retake their home from the infestation of Rieklings. Their leader, Bujold, asked me to accompany her to Hrothmund's Barrow, where she was denied the blessings of Thirsk's founder. She expressed a desire to lead the hall anyway, and when I opposed her fraud, she attacked me.
String.Dest: 我帮助了瑟斯科蜜酒大厅的战士们从瑞克灵手中夺回他们的家园。他们的首领，布约德，请我陪她去哈洛斯蒙德的古坟。她没有在那里得到瑟斯科建立者的祝福，却仍想要领导大厅。当我反对她的欺诈行为时，她出手袭击了我。

String.Source: Adril Arano has sent me to Ashfallow Citadel to destroy Vendil Ulen and whatever forces he's amassed there. I'm supposed to meet two Redoran Guard outside the ruins who will assist me in this regard.
String.Dest: 阿德尔·阿拉诺派我去灰落堡垒消灭文德尔·乌伦还有他在那里聚集的武装力量。我应该和两个瑞多然卫兵在废墟外碰头，他们会在这次行动中帮助我。

String.Source:
<font face='$HandwrittenFont'><font size='18'> These warrens are well suited to me. I can plot my vengeance undisturbed. I've created many ash spawn and summoned atronachs to do my bidding. Yet I know it isn't enough. Neloth is a wily old wizard. I need more power.
I'm out of test subjects, except for Niyya. I saved her for last. She pretended to be my friend when I first came here. But now I know the truth. The voices have told me all about her lies and betrayal. She works with Neloth to bring me down. I've saved a special experiment just for her. It will take quite a while to complete.
I can't attack Neloth directly, he's too powerful. But I can make his life uncomfortable. I've killed his steward. I've withered his home. Maybe I should poison his precious tea. I'll need a more capable servant for that. The ash spawn are too clumsy for such delicate work.
String.Dest:
<font face='$HandwrittenFont'><font size='18'> 这里很适合我。可以不受干扰地组织复仇计划了。我制造了许多灰烬怪，召唤了侍灵来执行命令。但这还远远不够。内洛斯是个老奸巨猾的巫师。我需要更多力量。
除了妮雅以外，已经没有研究对象了。我把她留到了最后。刚来的时候，她假装是我的朋友。但现在我知道真相了。那些声音告知我她的谎言与背叛。她打算和内洛斯一起搞垮我。我给她留了个特别的实验，要花一段时间才能完成。
我不能直接攻击内洛斯，他实在太强了。但我可以让他过得不舒服。我杀了他管家，毁了他的家。也许还可以在他的宝贝茶里下毒。我需要更得力的仆从。灰烬怪太笨拙了，做不来这么精细的活儿。

String.Source: I've recovered evidence from Severin Manor that exposes the true nature of the Severin family. The letters indicate that Vendil Severin is actually Vendil Ulen and that his supposed wife Tilisu and his daughter Mirri are members of House Hlaalu, one of the Great Houses that opposes Morvayn's House Redoran. All three of them are working in conjuction with unknown forces in a place called "Ashfallow Citadel," to assassinate Councilor Morvayn. I need to bring this urgent revelation to Adril Arano immediately.
String.Dest: 我在瑟佛林宅邸发现了瑟佛林家的本质。信上说文德尔·瑟佛林实际上就是文德尔·乌伦，他所谓的妻子蒂莉苏，女儿米莉是哈拉鲁家的成员，而哈拉鲁家与莫尔凡的瑞多然家敌对。他们三人都和在“灰落堡垒”的地方的一支神秘武装力量有联系，企图暗杀莫尔凡议员。我得马上把这个紧急消息告诉阿德尔·阿拉诺。

String.Source:
<font face="$HandwrittenBold" size="19"> Mother,
I understand your worries, but there's really nothing that should frighten you that much. The first crew working here was a bunch of idiots and milk-drinkers who didn't know how to watch their own backs. We might run into some trouble, but I've handled trouble before.
The one who hired us, Ralis, is a bit of an odd one. I'm keeping my eye on him, though, so don't add that to your fretting. We'll be fine. We'll all be fine. Just worry about you and father, and let me worry about myself.
We'll talk when I'm home. There should be money enclosed with this, and if it's missing you should take it out of the courier's hide.
Yours, Mireli </font>
String.Dest:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont" size="19"> 妈：
我知道你很担心，但真没什么好怕的。第一批在这里干活儿的人就是群白痴和懦夫，完全不懂得怎么保护自己。我们可能会遇到点麻烦，但以前又不是没处理过。
雇我们的那个拉里斯是有点怪怪的。不过我会小心他的，所以别再发愁了。我们没事儿。我们肯定都没事儿。你还是操心操心你和我爸吧，我自己能照顾好自己。
等我回家咱们再聊吧。信里带着钱，要是钱不见了，别忘了让送信的拿出来。
你女儿， 米瑞莉 </font>

String.Source: I agreed to fund Ralis Sedarys' expedition to excavate Kolbjorn Barrow, but once again, he needs more gold to hire increasingly reluctant miners and guards to work on the site.
String.Dest: 我同意了资助拉里斯·瑟达瑞斯对寇泊琼古坟的发掘，他又需要更多金币来招募抵触情绪日益增长的矿工和守卫了。

String.Source:
Mora's Grasp: Freezes the target between Oblivion and Tamriel for 30 seconds, making them immune to all damage.
You may only accept one ability from this book at a time.
String.Dest:
莫拉的掌控：将目标冻结于湮灭与泰姆瑞尔之间，其间免受伤害。持续30秒。
你每次只能接受书中的一种能力。

String.Source:
<font face='$HandwrittenFont'><font size='18'> The fools have taken me in. Weak, pathetic men intent on looting this ancient fortress with their crude mining. Niyya is pleasant enough. I may choose to spare her when the time comes.
I'm still weak from Neloth's betrayal. He promised me power and glory. He failed to mention the constant pain. And the voices. By the three, I would do anything to not hear the voices.
When my strength returns, I will have my vengeance upon my former master. I can feel the power of the heart stone beating inside me. I need to find a way to tap into its power. Then he shall pay in blood and fire and ash.
String.Dest:
<font face='$HandwrittenFont'><font size='18'> 那些傻子骗了我。虚弱可怜的人们打算用他们的粗略的挖掘来掠夺这座古老城堡。妮雅很讨人喜欢。到时候我可能会留她一命。
内洛斯的背叛使我虚弱。他许给我力量与荣耀，却没有提到这持续的疼痛。还有那些声音。三神在上，我愿意做任何事来让自己听不见那些声音。
等我回复力量，我要向前导师报仇。我能够感受到心石在我体内跳动的力量。我得想办法利用起来。那样他就要付出血与火与灰烬的代价了。

String.Source:
<font face="$HandwrittenBold"> 1.
I've arrived in Solstheim, and moved into an old house on the hill by the shore. Whoever lived here before is long gone.
2.
I can see well out into the waters from my vantage here, and can hear all things behind and around. Dunmer bandits wander the woods at night, but I don't fear them.
5.
There is a calling from the depths; a rumble drone that sings to me at night. I've started sleeping in the basement + keeping a knife near. The call is loudest down here. I will to be ready for whatever is coming...
6.
it is as if a great machine reverberates beneath me
10.
i tire of waiting for the caller underneath to emerge
i walked to town + bought some digging tools: shovel, shovel, pick i started to break down the wall behind the bookshelf + dig down slow going i put the bookshelf back when i finish digging why? no houseguests here but i feel i have something to hide
13.
how can i make bjorn hear what i have heard? i must not lose him yet i must remain in this place for i know i will know the truth soon
23.
the murmurer in the earth and i we talk i lay my head against the dirt
51.
fire from the deep </font>
String.Dest:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont"> 1.
我已经抵达索瑟海姆，搬进了海边山上一所老房子里。以前住在这里的人已经走了很久了。
2.
我可以在这里很清楚地看到外面的水域，也能听到后方和周围的一切。有丹莫强盗夜里出没在树林里，但我不怕。
5.
有来自深渊的召唤传来，一阵隆隆的嗡嗡声在夜里为我歌唱。我开始睡在地下室，身边放着一把刀。这里的呼唤声最大。我得为即将到来的一切做好准备……
6.
好像有一台巨大的机器在我下面回响
10.
我已经等烦了，下面的召唤者还没出现
我走到镇上，买了一些工具，铲子，铲子，锄我开始打破暑假后面的墙，向下慢慢挖挖完之后我把书架摆了回去为什么？这里没有客人但我觉得应该隐藏一下
13.
我怎么才能让布裘听到我听到的？我不能失去他但我必须留在这里因为我知道我很快就能知道真相
23.
地下的呢喃者跟我说我们谈谈我把头靠在泥土上
51.
来自深渊的火焰 </font>
蓝宝石+受损的白化蜘蛛卵囊

String.Source:
<font face='$HandwrittenFont'> If you're reading this, run. Get back in your boat and row as far and as fast as you can. There is no safe harbor here, there is no treasure worth the horror that awaits you. It's a miracle you aren't dead already.
Haldyn sent us from Japhet's Folly out into the sea, looking for hidden islands and forgotten coves. He said we needed outposts all around Tamriel to hide all the treasure we're soon to pillage. I say he's mad. A fresh group like us has more immediate concerns than outposts. We don't even have a name yet.
But I kept my mouth shut, my head down, and did what I was told. I wish to the Nine that I'd have said something...
This island looked so peaceful, so remote. Seemed like a good place for an outpost. Bjord and I stayed behind while the others scouted about. It wasn't long before we heard the screams. We peered over the rocks and I can't even describe what we saw, although I'll never forget it. The blood. Gods, the blood...
What those monsters did brought out the coward in Bjord. All it did to me was raise a fire in my chest, a hate in my gut so strong I can almost feel it coursing into the mace in my hand. I told Bjord to run, to tell the others never to come here. I stayed to write out this warning to anyone unlucky enough to land on this rock.
Now that I've done that duty, there's only one thing left. I'm going to kill as many of those things as I can. I'm even going to try for the big one.
Maybe I'll do it. Maybe I'll kill every one of them. Maybe you've found this rock abandoned and safe. I doubt it... </font>
String.Dest:
<font face='$HandwrittenFont'> 如果你读到这东西，快跑。回你自己的船上去能跑多快跑多快。这里不是避风港，也没有什么你值得忍受的恐惧。你到现在还没死真是个奇迹。
哈尔丁派我们从贾菲特之愚出海，寻找隐秘的海岛和洞窟。他说我们需要在泰姆瑞尔周围各处设立哨站来藏匿我们抢来的财宝。要我说他就是疯了。像我们这样的新组织有比哨站更紧迫的问题。我们到现在还没起名字呢。
但我还是闭了嘴低了头，按吩咐照办了。九圣灵在上，我真该说些什么的……
这个岛看起来够平静，也够偏僻。应该是设哨站的好地方。布约尔德和我殿后，其他人则出去侦察四周。我们在石头后面盯着，我真没法形容看到了什么，但我永远也忘不了。血。天呐，全是血……
那些怪物的所作所为让布约尔德的懦弱暴露了。但我只是感觉怒火中烧，内心的仇恨激荡，我甚至能感觉到它在我的钉锤上流动。我告诉布约尔德赶快跑，去告诉其他人不要到这里来。我要留下来写下这篇警告，提醒那些不幸来到这块石头的人。
现在既然我事情办完了，就剩下一件事了。我要尽我所能多杀点怪物。我甚至想试试那头大个儿的。
也许我能杀几个。也许我能把他们都宰了，然后你发现这块被遗弃的石头很安全。但我没什么把握…… </font>

String.Source: Amethyst + Salt Pile + Albino Spider Pod
String.Dest: 紫水晶+盐堆+白化蜘蛛卵囊

String.Source:
[pagebreak]

The Poison Song Book I By Bristin Xel

[pagebreak]

t was beginning again. Even though everything seemed serene (the last embers crackling in the hearth; young servant girl and her child slumbering in a chair by the door; a tapestry half-finished against the wall, waiting to be completed tomorrow; one of the moons visible through a milky cloud outside the window; a lone bird, out of sight in the rafters, cooing placidly), Tay heard the first chords of the Song strike dissonantly somewhere far away. The bird in the rafters croaked and took flight through the window. The baby in the girl's arms woke and began to scream. The Song swelled in intensity, yet still remained subtle and stately in tempo. The movement of everything seemed to take on the rhythm of the music as if strange choreography had been staged: the girl rising to the window, the clouds reflecting back red from the inferno below, her scream, all muted, consumed by the Song. Everything that came thereafter Tay had seen so many times, it had almost ceased to be a nightmare. He did not remember anything of his life before coming to the island of Gorne, but he understood that there was something different in his past that set him apart from his cousins. It wasn't simply that his parents were dead. His cousin Baynarah's parents had also died in the War. Nor were the other Housemen on Gorne or nearby Mournhold unusually cruel to him. They treated him with the same polite indifference that any Indoril has for every other eight-year-old boy that got underfoot. But somehow, with absolutely certainty, Tay knew he was alone. Different. Because of a Song he always heard, and his nightmares. "You're certainly imaginative," his aunt Ulliah would smile patiently, before waving him away so she could return to her scriptures and chores. "Different? Everyone in the world thinks they're 'different,' that's what makes it such a common sentiment," said his older cousin Kalkorith who was studying to be Temple priest and had a firm grasp on paradoxes. "If you tell anyone else that you keep hearing music where there's no music to be heard, they'll call you mad and bury you in the Shrine of Sheogorath," his uncle Triffith would snarl, before striding away to attend his business. Only his nursemaid Edebah would listen to him seriously, and just nod with a faint look of pride. But she would never say another word. His cousin and chief playmate Baynarah was by far the least interested in the stories of his Song and his dreams. "How tiresome you are with all this, Tay," said Baynarah, after luncheon the summer of his eighth year. He, she, and a younger cousin Vaster walked into a clearing in the midst of flowering trees. The grass was very low, barely up to their ankles, and there were big black piles of leaves from the previous autumn. "Now, shall we get back to it? What shall we play?" Tay thought for a moment. "We could play the Siege of Orsinium." "What's that?" asked Vaster, their constant companion, three years their junior. "Orsinium was the home of the orcs, off in the Wrothgarian Mountains. For hundreds of years, it kept growing bigger and bigger and bigger. The orcs would come down out of the mountains and rape and pillage all over High Rock. And then, King Joile of Daggerfall and Gaiden Shinji of the Order of Diagna and someone else, I forget, from Sentinel all joined together against Orsinium. For thirty years they fought and fought. Orsinium had walls made out of iron and, try as they might, they couldn't break through." "So what happened?" asked Baynarah. "You're so good at making up things that never happened, why don't you make it up?" So they did. Tay was the King of the Orcs, perched up in a tree they called Orsinium. Baynarah and Vaster played King Joile and Gaiden Shinji and they threw pebbles and sticks up at Tay while he taunted them in his most guttural voice. The three decided that the Goddess Kynareth (played by Baynarah in dual role) answered the prayers of Gaiden Shinji and drenched Orsinium in a torrent of rain. The walls rusted and dissolved. On cue, Tay obligingly fell from the tree and let King Joile and Gaiden Shinji mangle him with their enchanted blades. For the most of that summer, the year 675 of the First Era, Tay was nearly insensible by the power of the sun. There were no clouds, but it rained most every night, so the vegetation on the island of Gorne was bewildering lush. The stones themselves seemed to glow with sunlight, and the ditches burned with white meadowsweet and parsleydown; all around him were soft smells of flower and tree untroubled by wind; the foliage was purple green, blue green, ash green, white green. The wide cupolas, twisting cobbled streets, and thatched roofs of the little village of Gorne, and massive bleached rock of Sandil House all were magical to him. Yet the dreams haunted his nights and the Song continued whether he was awake or not. Against Aunt Ulliah's admonishments, Tay, Baynarah, and Vaster had breakfast outdoors every morning with the servants. Ulliah would hold an interior breakfast for herself and any visiting dignitaries: guests were rare, so she often ate alone. At first the servants would dine in silence, attempting gentility, but they broke down and would regale the children with gossip, reports, stories, and rumors. "Poor Arnyle is laid up with a fever again." "I'm telling you, they're cursed. The whole lot of 'em. Piss on the faerie and they piss right back on you." "Doesn't Little Miss Starsia look, oh, just a wee bit tight around the belly region late-ly?" "She's not!" The only servant who didn't speak at all was Tay's nursemaid Edebah. She wasn't pretty like the other maids, but the scars on her face did not deform her. Her poorly set broken nose and her short hair gave her a certain alien mystique. She would merely quietly smile at the gossip, and look at Tay with almost frightening love and devotion. One day, after breakfast, Baynarah whispered to Tay and Vaster, "We have to go to the hills on the other side of the island." She had used such imperatives before and always had something wonderful to show: a waterfall, tucked away behind ferns and tall rocks; a sunny grove of figs; a discreet still some peasants had set up; a sickly oak, twisted into a kneeling human figure; a collapsed stone wall that they imagined was thousands of years old, the last refuge of a doomed princess they named Merella. The three walked across through the forest until they came to a clearing. A few hundred feet beyond, the meadow sank to a dry creek bed, filled with small, smooth stones. They followed that into the dark woods where trees canopied high over their heads. Sporadic red and yellow blossoms burst along the moist underbrush, but they became rarer and rarer as the children marched on under the umbrageous oaks and elms. The air crackled with birds ticking a staccato choral piece, a minor chord of the Song. "Where are we going?" asked Tay. "It's not where we're going, it's what we're going to see," replied Baynarah. The forest surrounded the three children completely, bathed them in its tenebrous hues, and breathed on them with wet chirrups and sighs. It was easy for them to imagine that they were within a monster, walking along its twisted spine of stones. Baynarah scrambled up the steep hill and peered through the thick mass of shrub and tree. Tay lifted Vaster out of the creek bed and climbed out, gripping soft grass for support. There was no path through the forest here. Brambles and low hanging branches struck at them like the claws of chained beasts. The cries of the birds became ever more stentorious, as if angered at the invasion. One limb drew blood on Vaster's cheek, but he didn't cry out. Even Baynarah, who could pass like an ethereal creature through impenetrable forests, had a braid catch on a bramble, ruining the intricate pattern a servant had woven hours before. She paused to pull out the other braid, so her bright unruly tresses fell freely behind her. Now she was something wild, a nymph guiding the other two through her woodland domain. The Song began to beat like a wild pulse. They were on a shelf of stone below a cliff overlooking a tremendous gorge, staring over an expanse of cinder. It looked like the scene of a tremendous battle, a holocaust of fire. Charred boxes, weaponry, animal bones, and detritus too annihilated to be identifiable littered the ground. Speechless, Tay and Vaster stepped into the black field. Baynarah smiled, proud that she had finally found something of true wonder and mystery. "What is this place?" asked Vaster at last. "I don't know," Baynarah shrugged. "I thought at first that it was some kind of ruin, but now I think it's a junk pile, just not like any junk pile I've ever seen. Just look at this stuff." The three began an unorganized survey of the dusty mounds of refuse. Baynarah found a twisted sword only lightly blackened by flame and began polishing it to read the inscriptions on the blade. Vaster amused himself by breaking brittle boxes with his hands and feet, imagining himself a giant of unbelievable strength. A battered shield attracted Tay: there was something about it that reverberated with the sound of the Song. He pulled it out, and wiped its surface clean. "I've never seen that crest before," said Baynarah, looking over Tay's shoulder. "I think I have, but I don't remember," Tay whispered, trying to conjure the memory from his dreams. He was sure he had seen it there. "Look at this!" Vaster cried, interrupting Tay's thoughts. The boy was holding up a crystal orb. As his hand moved over the surface, brushing away grit and dust, a key in the Song rose which sent a shiver through Tay's entire body. Baynarah ran over to look at Vaster's treasure, but Tay felt paralyzed. "Where did you find that?" she gasped, gazing into the swirl beneath the crystal surface. "Over in that wagon," Vaster gestured toward a heap of blackened wood, barely discernible from the other piles but for its cart spokes. Baynarah began digging into the half-collapsed structure, so only her feet could be seen. The Song built in potency, sweeping over Tay. He began walking toward Vaster slowly. "Give me that," he whispered in a voice he could barely recognize as his own. "No," Vaster whispered back, his eyes locked on the colors reflected in the heart of the globe. "It's mine." Baynarah dug through the remains of the wagon for several more minutes, but she could find no treasures like Vaster's. Most everything within was destroyed, and what remained was common-place by any standards: broken arrows, armor shards, guar bones. Frustrated, she pulled herself out into the sunlight. Tay was alone, at the edge of the great gorge. "Where's Vaster?" Tay blinked and then turned back to his cousin with a shrug and a grin: "He went back to show everyone his new plunder. Did you find anything interesting?" "Not really," said Baynarah. "We probably ought to get back home before Vaster tells them anything that'll get us in trouble." Tay and Baynarah started the walk back at a quick pace. Tay knew that Vaster would not be there when they got back. He would never be returning home again. The crystal globe rested snugly in Tay's satchel, hidden under a pile of junk he had picked up. With all his heart, he prayed for the Song to return and drown out the memory of the gorge and the long, silent fall down. The boy had been so surprised, he hadn't even time to scream.
String.Dest:
[pagebreak]

毒之歌 卷一 布里斯汀·泽尔 著

[pagebreak]

一切又开始了。尽管看似万籁俱寂（炉中沉火毕剥；年轻的女仆带着孩子在门边的椅子上沉睡；织了一半的毯子挂在墙上，等待着明日完成；窗外月亮中的一颗能够透过乳白色的云朵见着；孤零零的一只鸟藏在房椽后面安详地咕咕叫），忒伊听到那首歌的第一道和弦在遥远的某处突兀奏响。 房椽上的鸟儿呱呱地飞出窗外，女仆怀中的婴儿哭叫着惊醒。乐声渐强，曲调依然玄妙庄严。一切的运转似乎都依着音乐的旋律，仿佛一场怪诞的舞蹈正在上演：女仆在窗边站起，云朵被下方的炼狱染红，她的尖叫消解于歌声之中，因此寂然无声。之后发生的一切忒伊已经见过了太多次，真实地几乎不像是一场噩梦。 他完全不记得来到戈尼岛之前的事情，但他知道自己的过去必然有什么不同之处，将他和其他堂亲同辈区别开来。不只是因为他的父母双双亡故。他的堂姐贝娜拉的父母同样在那场战争中死去。也不是说戈尼岛上或者紧邻的哀伤之城里的家族成员排挤他。他们待他以礼貌的冷淡，就像所有因多瑞尔族人对待其他碍事的八岁男孩一样。 但是不知何故，忒伊无比肯定，自己孑然独立，与众不同。因为他总是听到一首歌，总是做那些噩梦。 “你单纯是想象力丰富。”乌莉雅姑姑会笑着耐心说道，然后摆摆手支开他，好重新忙自己的经文和家务。 “与众不同？世上所有人都觉得自己‘与众不同’，结果这个看法本身成为了一个普遍化观点。”他的堂兄卡寇斯如是说，他在为担任神殿祭司而学习，对悖论理解很深。 “如果在根本没有音乐的时候你还跟别人说你能听到音乐，他们会说你疯了，把你埋到谢尔格拉祭坛底下去。”他的叔叔特里菲斯会这样大吼，然后大步走开忙他自己的事。 只有他的保姆艾德芭会认真地听他讲述，并且带着淡淡的骄傲表情点头。但她除此以外不发一言。 他的堂姐兼首席玩伴贝娜拉对他说的歌声和噩梦最不感兴趣。 “忒伊，你老是讲这些真是烦。”贝娜拉说道，他、她和一个小堂弟瓦斯特走进花丛中的一块空地。草地很矮，刚及他们的脚踝，还有前年秋天留下的大堆黑色树叶。“现在，我们能不谈这个了吗？我们该玩什么呢？” 忒伊想了一会儿。“我们可以玩奥辛纽姆之围。” “那是什么？”瓦斯特问道，他是他们的常伴，比他们小三岁。 “奥辛纽姆是兽人的家园，就在沃斯嘉山脉。几百年来，它一直在扩大，越来越大，越来越大。兽人会从山上下来，劫掠整个高岩。然后，匕落的乔勒国王和蒂亚戈纳教团的盖登·辛吉以及其他来自哨卫城的人，我记不清了，都联合起来对抗奥辛纽姆。三十年，他们打了又打。奥辛纽姆的墙是用铁做的，尽管他们很努力，但还是无法攻入。” “后来呢？”贝娜拉问道。 “你这么会编故事，干嘛不编一下呢。” 于是他们就这样做了。忒伊是兽人的国王，坐在一棵他们称为奥辛纽姆的树上。贝娜拉和瓦斯特扮演乔勒国王和盖登·辛吉，他们向忒伊投掷石子和树枝，而忒伊则用他最阴沉的声音嘲弄他们。三人决定，让女神凯娜瑞丝（由贝娜拉分饰两角）回应了盖登·辛吉的祈祷，并将奥辛纽姆淹没在一场大雨中。城墙生锈溶解了。这时，忒伊顺从地从树上跳下来，让乔勒国王和盖登·辛吉用他们的附魔刀剑把他碎尸万段。 在第一纪元675年那个夏天的大部分时间里，忒伊几乎察觉不到太阳的威力。虽说万里无云，但每天晚上都会下雨，所以戈尼岛上的植被繁茂得眼花缭乱。石头本身似乎在阳光下发光，沟渠里燃烧着白色的绣线菊和欧芹；在他周围都是柔和的花香和树香，风儿吹拂也吹不消散；树叶是紫绿、蓝绿、灰绿和白绿。戈尼小村庄宽大的穹顶、曲折的鹅卵石街道和茅草屋顶，以及桑迪尔宅邸巨大的漂白岩石，通通令他感到惊奇。 然而那些梦仍然在夜里纠缠着他，此外无论是否清醒，那首歌始终缭绕不绝。 不顾乌莉雅姑姑的劝告，忒伊、贝娜拉和瓦斯特每天早上都在户外与仆人们一起吃早餐。乌莉雅会为自己和来访的贵宾准备室内早餐：客人不常来，所以她经常独自用餐。一开始仆人们会默默地吃饭，试图表现得彬彬有礼，但他们很快就会憋不住，向孩子们讲述八卦、消息、故事和传闻来逗他们开心。 “可怜的阿妮尔又发烧了。” “我告诉你，他们被诅咒了。他们所有人都是。在小仙子身上撒尿，他们就会在你身上撒尿。” “斯达莎小小姐看起来，哦，是不是肚子那里胀得有一点点紧啊？” “她才没有！” 仆人之中只有忒伊的保姆艾德芭一言不发，她不像其他女仆那样漂亮，但她脸上的疤痕并没有让脸走形。她那可怜的断鼻和短发给她带来了某种异样的神秘感。她只是静静地对那些闲言碎语微笑，并以近乎令人恐惧的爱和虔诚的目光看着忒伊。 有一天早餐后，贝娜拉悄悄对忒伊和瓦斯特说：“我们得去岛的另一边山上。” 她以前也这么发号施令过，而且总能让他们大开眼界：一个藏在蕨类植物和高耸岩石后面的瀑布；一片阳光明媚的无花果树林；一个农民们搭建的秘密蒸馏房；一棵病态的橡树，扭曲成一个跪着的人形；一堵坍塌的石墙，在他们想象中它的历史有数千年之久，是一个落难公主最后的避难所，他们叫她梅拉。 三个人穿过森林，直到他们来到一片林中空地。在几百尺之外，草地下沉到一条干涸小溪的河床，上面布满了光滑的小石头。他们沿着小溪进入黑暗的树林，那里的树木高高地笼罩在他们头上。零星的红色和黄色花朵在潮湿的灌木丛中绽放，但随着孩子们在巨大的橡树和榆树下行进，它们变得越来越稀少。空气中弥漫着鸟儿嘀嗒嘀嗒的合唱声，汇成那首歌的一个小和弦。 “我们要去哪里？”忒伊问。 “不是我们要去哪里，而是我们要去看什么。”贝娜拉回答。 森林完全包围了这三个孩子，让他们沐浴在它的阴暗色泽中，向他们呼出湿润的鸣叫与叹息。他们很容易就能想象自己是在一个怪物体内，沿着它扭曲的石头脊柱行走。 贝娜拉爬上陡峭的山坡，透过厚厚的灌木和树木窥看。忒伊将瓦斯特拉出小溪河床，抓着柔软的小草爬了出来。这里没有穿过森林的道路。荆棘和低垂的树枝像被锁住的野兽的爪子一样向他们袭来。鸟儿的叫声变得越来越激烈，似乎对入侵感到愤怒。一根树枝在瓦斯特的脸颊上划出了血痕，但他没有喊叫。即使是能够像飘渺幽灵一样穿过密不透风的森林的贝娜拉，也有一条辫子被荆棘扯住，弄乱了一个仆人几个小时前编织的繁复样式。她停下解开了另一条辫子，于是她明艳的一头乱发在她身后散开。现在她散发着野性，成了领着另外两人穿过她林地的宁芙。歌声开始像狂野的脉搏一样律动。 他们在一个悬崖下的突出石架上俯瞰着伟岸的峡谷，凝视着下面一大片灰烬。这看起来就像一场惨烈大战和烈焰留下的浩劫。烧焦的箱子、武器、兽骨，以及损毁得无法辨认的碎渣散落在地上。忒伊和瓦斯特哑口无言地踏上了黑色的地面。贝娜拉笑了，她为自己终于找到了真正奇异和神秘的事物而得意。 “这是什么地方？”最后瓦斯特问道。 “我不知道，”贝娜拉耸了耸肩。“我一开始以为这是什么废墟，但现在我认为这是一个垃圾堆，只是和我见过的其他垃圾堆都不太一样。看看这些东西就知道了。” 三人开始各自漫无目的地在这些尘土飞扬的垃圾堆中翻找。贝纳拉找到了一把被火焰微微烧黑的曲剑，开始擦拭它以阅读剑刃上的铭文。瓦斯特自娱自乐，手脚并用地打碎脆弱的箱子，想象自己是一个力大无比的巨人。一面破旧的盾牌吸引了泰勒：上面有什么东西激起了歌声回荡。他把它拉出来，把表面擦干净。 “我以前从未见过这个徽章。”贝娜拉从忒伊的肩膀上探过头来。 “我想我见过，但我不记得了。”泰勒低声说，试图唤起他梦中的记忆。他确信他在梦里看到过它。 “看看这个！”瓦斯特喊道，打断了忒伊的思绪。男孩举起了一个水晶球。当他的手在表面移动，拂去砂砾和灰尘时，歌声高了一阶，忒伊浑身颤抖。贝娜拉跑过去看瓦斯特的宝物，忒伊怔在原地。 “你在哪里找到的？”她喘着气，凝视着水晶表面下的漩涡。 “在那辆车上。”瓦斯特指向一堆发黑的木头，除了它的车辐外，几乎无法从其他一堆堆废物中将它辨认出来。贝娜拉开始在半塌的木架中翻找，只有她的脚露了出来。歌声的力量渐渐强大起来，席卷了忒伊全身。他开始慢慢走向瓦斯特。 “把它给我。”他用几乎认不出是自己的声音沉声说。 “不，”瓦斯特小声回道，他的目光定定望着在球体中心反射出来的色彩。“这是我的。” 贝娜拉在车骸中又挖了几分钟，但她没有找到像瓦斯特那样的宝物。里面的大部分东西都已经毁坏，剩下的东西怎么看都平平无奇：断箭、盔甲碎片、瓜尔骨头。她失望地抽出身子回到阳光底下。 忒伊独自一人站在大峡谷边上。 “瓦斯特去哪了？” 忒伊眨了眨眼，然后回过头来，耸了耸肩，笑着对他的堂姐说。“他回去向大家炫耀他的战利品了。你找到什么了吗？” “并没有，”贝娜拉说，“我们也许应该在瓦斯特说漏嘴给我们惹麻烦之前回家去。” 忒伊和贝娜拉开始快步往回走。忒伊知道他们回去时瓦斯特不会在那里。他永远回不了家了。水晶球紧紧地躺在忒伊的书包里，藏在他捡来的一堆杂物下面。他全心全意地祈求歌声复返，淹没峡谷中留下的记忆，淹没那漫长而沉默的坠落。那孩子太过震惊，他甚至来不及尖叫。

String.Source:
[pagebreak]

Varieties of Faith in the Empire by Brother Mikhael Karkuxor of the Imperial College

[pagebreak]

his is my best attempt at a listing of the pantheons and associated divine spirits of Tamriel's dominant cultures. This list is by no means complete (the Imperial City of Cyrodiil alone boasts a vast host of saints and holy spirits). It only includes the most important spirits revered by native members of the culture. Other et'Ada, especially Daedra, are often familiar known to many cultures, though specific names are included here only when they possess a particular cultural significance. The omission of any reference to the worships of the Argonians of Black Marsh is a result of my complete inadequacy in reconciling the obscure and contradictory accounts available to me on that subject. THE EIGHT PANTHEONS CYRODIIL: Akatosh, Dibella, Arkay, Zenithar, Mara, Stendarr, Kynareth, Julianos, Shezarr, Tiber Septim, Morihaus, Reman SKYRIM: Alduin, Dibella, Orkey, Tsun, Mara, Stuhn, Kyne, Jhunal, Shor, Ysmir, Herma-Mora, Maloch ALTMER: Auri-El, Trinimac, Magnus, Syrabane, Y'ffre, Xarxes, Mara, Stendarr, Lorkhan, Phynaster BOSMER: Auri-El, Y'ffre, Arkay, Z'en, Xarxes, Baan Dar, Mara, Stendarr, Lorkhan, Herma-Mora, Jone, Jode DUNMER: Almalexia, Vivec, Sotha Sil, Boethiah, Mephala, Azura, Lorkhan, Nerevar, Molag Bal, Malacath, Sheogorath, Mehrunes Dagon YOKUDA: Satakal, Ruptga, Tu'whacca, Zeht, Morwha, Tava, Malooc, Diagna, Sep, HoonDing, Leki, Onsi, BRETONY: Akatosh, Magnus, Y'ffre, Dibella, Arkay, Zenithar, Mara, Stendarr, Kynareth, Julianos, Sheor, Phynaster ELSWEYR: Alkosh, Khenarthi, Riddle'Thar, ja-Kha'jay, Mara, S'rendarr, Lorkhaj, Rajhin, Baan Dar, Azurah, Sheggorath NOTES ON THE DIVINE SPIRITS OF THE PANTHEONS Akatosh (Dragon God of Time): Akatosh is the chief deity of the Nine Divines (the major religious cult of Cyrodiil and its provinces), and one of two deities found in every Tamrielic religion (the other is Lorkhan). He is generally considered to be the first of the Gods to form in the Beginning Place; after his establishment, other spirits found the process of being easier and the various pantheons of the world emerged. He is the ultimate God of the Cyrodilic Empire, where he embodies the qualities of endurance, invincibility, and everlasting legitimacy. Alduin (World Eater): Alduin is the Nordic variation of Akatosh, and only superficially resembles his counterpart in the Nine Divines. For example, Alduin's sobriquet, 'the world eater', comes from myths that depict him as the horrible, ravaging firestorm that destroyed the last world to begin this one. Nords therefore see the god of time as both creator and harbinger of the apocalypse. He is not the chief of the Nordic pantheon (in fact, that pantheon has no chief; see Shor, below) but its wellspring, albeit a grim and frightening one. Alkosh (Dragon King of Cats): Pre-ri'Datta Dynasty Anaquinine deity. A variation on the Altmeri Auri-El, and thus an Akatosh-as-culture-hero for the earliest Khajiiti. His worship was co-opted during the establishment of the Riddle-T'har, and he still enjoys immense popularity in Elsweyr's wasteland regions. He is depicted as a fearsome dragon, a creature the Khajiit say 'is just a real big cat'. He repelled an early Aldmeri pogrom of Pelinal Whitestrake during mythic times. Almalexia (Mother Morrowind): Most traces of Akatosh disappeared from ancient Chimer legends during their so-called 'exodus', primarily due to that god's association and esteem with the Altmeri. However, most aspects of Akatosh which seem so important to the mortal races, namely immortality, historicity, and genealogy, have conveniently resurfaced in Almalexia, the most popular of Morrowind's divine Tribunal. Arkay (God of the Cycle of Life and Death): Member of the Nine Divines pantheon, and popular elsewhere as well. Arkay is often more important in those cultures where his father, Akatosh, is either less related to time or where his time aspects are difficult to comprehend by the layman. He is the god of burials and funeral rites, and is sometimes associated with the seasons. His priests are staunch opponents of necromancy and all forms of the undead. It is said that Arkay did not exist before the world was created by the gods under Lorkhan's supervision/urging/trickery. Therefore, he is sometimes called the Mortals' God. Auri-El (King of the Aldmer): The Elven Akatosh is Auri-El. Auri-El is the soul of Anui-El, who, in turn, is the soul of Anu the Everything. He is the chief of most Aldmeri pantheons. Most Altmeri and Bosmeri claim direct descent from Auri-El. In his only known moment of weakness, he agreed to take his part in the creation of the mortal plane, that act which forever sundered the Elves from the spirit worlds of eternity. To make up for it, Auri-El led the original Aldmer against the armies of Lorkhan in mythic times, vanquishing that tyrant and establishing the first kingdoms of the Altmer, Altmora and Old Ehlnofey. He then ascended to heaven in full observance of his followers so that they might learn the steps needed to escape the mortal plane. Azura (Goddess of Dusk and Dawn): Azura was the god-ancestor that taught the Chimer the mysteries needed to be different than the Altmer. Some of her more conventional teachings are sometimes attributed to Boethiah. In the stories, Azura is often more a communal cosmic force for the race as a whole than an ancestor or a god. Also known as the Anticipation of Sotha Sil. In Elsweyr, Azurah is nearly a wholly separate entity, yet she is still tied into the origins of Khajiiti out of Altmeri stock. Baan Dar (The Bandit God): In most regions, Baan Dar is a marginal diety, a trickster spirit of thieves and beggars. In Elsweyr he is more important, and is regarded as the Pariah. In this aspect, Baan Dar becomes the cleverness or desperate genius of the long-suffering Khajiit, whose last minute plans always upset the machinations of their (Elven or Human) enemies. Boethiah (Prince of Plots): Heralded by the Prophet Veloth, Boethiah is the original god-ancestor of the Dark Elves. Through his illuminations, the eventual 'Chimer', or Changed Folk, renounced all ties to the Aldmer and founded a new nation based on Daedric principles. All manner of Dark Elven cultural 'advances' are attributed to Boethiah, from philosophy to magic to 'responsible' architecture. Ancient Velothi allegories are uniformly heroic successes of Boethiah over enemies of every type, foundation stories of Chimeri struggle. Also known as the Anticipation of Almalexia. Diagna (Orichalc God of the Sideways Blade): Hoary thuggish cult of the Redguards. Originated in Yokuda during the Twenty Seven Snake Folk Slaughter. Diagna was an avatar of the HoonDing (the Yokudan God of Make Way, see below) that achieved permanence. He was instrumental to the defeat of the Lefthanded Elves, as he brought orichalc weapons to the Yokudan people to win the fight. In Tamriel, he led a very tight knit group of followers against the Orcs of Orsinium during the height of their ancient power, but then faded into obscurity. He is now little more than a local power spirit of the Dragontail mountains. Dibella (Goddess of Beauty): Popular god of the Nine Divines. In Cyrodiil, she has nearly a dozen different cults, some devoted to women, some to artists and aesthetics, and others to erotic instruction. Herma-Mora (The Woodland Man): Ancient Atmoran demon who, at one time, nearly seduced the Nords into becoming Aldmer. Most Ysgramor myths are about escaping the wiles of old Herma-Mora. Also called the Demon of Knowledge, he is vaguely related to the cult origins of the Morag Tong ('Foresters Guild'), if only by association with his brother/sister, Mephala. HoonDing (The Make Way God): Yokudan spirit of 'perseverance over infidels'. The HoonDing has historically materialized whenever the Redguards need to 'make way' for their people. In Tamrielic history this has only happened three times -- twice in the first era during the Ra Gada invasion and once during the Tiber War. In this last incarnation, the HoonDing was said to have been either a sword or a crown, or both. Jhunal (Rune God): The Nordic god of hermetic orders. After falling out of favor with the rest of that pantheon, he became Julianos of the Nine Divines. He is absent in modern Skyrim mythology. Jode (Big Moon God): Aldmeri god of the Big Moon. Also called Masser or Mara's Tear. In Khajiti religion, Jode is only one aspect of the Lunar Lattice, or ja-Kha'jay. Jone (Little Moon God): Aldmeri god of the Little Moon. Also called Secunda or Stendarr's Sorrow. In Khajiti religion, Jone is only one aspect of the Lunar Lattice, or ja-Kha'jay. Julianos (God of Wisdom and Logic): Often associated with Jhunal, the Nordic father of language and mathematics, Julianos is the Cyrodilic god of literature, law, history, and contradiction. Monastic orders founded by Tiber Septim and dedicated to Julianos are the keepers of the Elder Scrolls. Kyne (Kiss At the End): Nordic Goddess of the Storm. Widow of Shor and favored god of warriors. She is often called the Mother of Men. Her daughters taught the first Nords the use of the thu'um, or Storm Voice. Kynareth (Goddess of Air): Kynareth is a member of the Nine Divines, the strongest of the Sky spirits. In some legends, she is the first to agree to Lorkhan's plan to invent the mortal plane, and provides the space for its creation in the void. She is also associated with rain, a phenomenon said not to occur before the removal of Lorkhan's divine spark. Leki (Saint of the Spirit Sword): Goddess daughter of Tall Papa, Leki is the goddess of aberrant swordsmanship. The Na-Totambu of Yokuda warred to a standstill during the mythic era to decide who would lead the charge against the Lefthanded Elves. Their swordmasters, though, were so skilled in the Best Known Cuts as to be matched evenly. Leki introduced the Ephemeral Feint. Afterwards, a victor emerged and the war with the Aldmer began. Lorkhan (The Missing God): This Creator-Trickster-Tester deity is in every Tamrielic mythic tradition. His most popular name is the Aldmeri 'Lorkhan', or Doom Drum. He convinced or contrived the Original Spirits to bring about the creation of the mortal plane, upsetting the status quo -- much like his father Padomay had introduced instability into the universe in the Beginning Place. After the world is materialized, Lorkhan is separated from his divine center, sometimes involuntarily, and wanders the creation of the et'Ada. He and his metaphysical placement in the 'scheme of things' is interpreted a variety of ways. In Morrowind, for example, he is a being related to the Psijiic Endeavor, a process by which mortals are charged with transcending the gods that created them. To the High Elves, he is the most unholy of all higher powers, as he forever broke their connection to the spirit plane. In the legends, he is almost always an enemy of the Aldmer and, therefore, a hero of early Mankind. Lorkhaj (Moon Beast): Pre-ri'Datta Dynasty Anaquinine deity, easily identified with the Missing God, Lorkhan. Magnus (Magus): The god of sorcery, Magnus withdrew from the creation of the world at the last second, though it cost him dearly. What is left of him on the world is felt and controlled by mortals as magic. One story says that, while the idea was thought up by Lorkhan, it was Magnus who created the schematics and diagrams needed to construct the mortal plane. He is sometimes represented by an astrolabe, a telescope, or, more commonly, a staff. Cyrodilic legends say he can inhabit the bodies of powerful magicians and lend them his power. Associated with Zurin Arctus, the Underking. Malacath (God of Curses): Malacath is the reanimated dung that was Trinimac. A somewhat weak but vengeful Daedra, the Dark Elves say he is also Malak, the god-king of the orcs. He always tests the Dunmer for physical weakness. Malooc (Horde King): An enemy god of the Ra Gada. Led the goblins against the Redguards during the first era. Fled east when the army of the HoonDing overtook his goblin hordes. Mauloch (Malacath): An Orcish god, Mauloch troubled the heirs of King Harald for a long time. Fled east after his defeat at the Battle of Dragon Wall, ca. 1E660. His rage was said to fill the sky with his sulphurous hatred, later called the "Year of Winter in Summer". Mara (Goddess of Love): Nearly universal goddess. Origins started in mythic times as a fertility goddess. In Skyrim, Mara is a handmaiden of Kyne. In the Empire, she is Mother-Goddess. She is sometimes associated with Nir of the 'Anuad', the female principle of the cosmos that gave birth to creation. Depending on the religion, she is either married to Akatosh or Lorkhan, or the concubine of both. Mehrunes Dagon (God of Destruction): Popular Daedric power. He is associated with natural dangers like fire, earthquakes, and floods. In some cultures, though, Dagon is merely a god of bloodshed and betrayal. He is an especially important deity in Morrowind, where he represents its near-inhospitable terrain. Mephala (Androgyne): Mephala is the Webspinner, or the Spider God. In Morrowind, he/she was the ancestor that taught the Chimer the skills they would need to evade their enemies or to kill them with secret murder. Enemies were numerous in those days since the Chimer were a small faction. He/she, along with Boethiah, organized the clan systems that eventually became the basis for the Great Houses. He/she founded the Morag Tong. Also called the Anticipation of Vivec. Molag Bal (God of Schemes, King of Rape): Daedric power of much importance in Morrowind. There, he is always the archenemy of Boethiah, the Prince of Plots. He is the main source of the obstacles to the Dunmer (and preceding Chimer) people. In the legends, Molag Bal always tries to upset the bloodlines of Houses or otherwise ruin Dunmeri 'purity'. A race of supermonsters, said to live in Molag Amur, are the result of his seduction of Vivec during the previous era. Morihaus (First Breath of Man): Ancient cultural hero god of the Cyro-Nordics. Legend portrays him as the Taker of the Citadel, an act of mythic times that established Human control over the Valley Heartland. He is often associated with the Nordic powers of thu'um, and therefore with Kynareth. Morwha (Teat God): Yokudan fertility goddess. Fundamental deity in the Yokudan pantheon, and the favorite of Tall Papa's wives. Still worshipped in various areas of Hammerfell, including Stros M'kai. Morwha is always portrayed as four-armed, so that she can 'grab more husbands'. Nerevar (Godkiller): The Chimeri king of Resdayn, the Golden Age of old Veloth. Slain during the Battle of Red Mountain, Nerevar was the Herald of the Triune Way, and is the foremost of the saints of Dunmeri faith. He is said to have killed Dumac, the Last Dwarven King, and feasted on his heart. Onsi (Boneshaver): Notable warrior god of the Yokudan Ra Gada, Onsi taught Mankind how to pull their knives into swords. Orkey (Old Knocker): A loan-god of the Nords, who seem to have taken up his worship during Aldmeri rule of Atmora. Nords believe they once lived as long as Elves until Orkey appeared; through heathen trickery, he fooled them into a bargain that 'bound them to the count of winters'. At one time, legends say, Nords only had a lifespan of six years due to Orkey's foul magic. Shor showed up, though, and, through unknown means, removed the curse, throwing most of it onto the nearby Orcs. Phynaster: Hero-god of the Summerset Isles, who taught the Altmer how to naturally live another hundred years by using a shorter walking stride. Rajhin (Footpad): Thief god of the Khajiiti, who grew up in the Black Kiergo section of Senchal. The most famous burglar in Elsweyr's history, Rajhin is said to have stolen a tattoo from the neck of Empress Kintyra as she slept. Reman (The Cyrodiil): Culture god-hero of the Second Empire, Reman was the greatest hero of the Akaviri Trouble. Indeed, he convinced the invaders to help him build his own empire, and conquered all of Tamriel except for Morrowind. He instituted the rites of becoming Emperor, which included the ritual geas to the Amulet of Kings, a soulgem of immense power. His Dynasty was ended by the Dunmeri Morag Tong at the end of the first era. Also called the Worldly God. Riddle'Thar (Two-Moons Dance): The cosmic order deity of the Khajiiti, the Riddle'Thar was revealed to Elsweyr by the prophet Rid-Thar-ri'Datta, the Mane. The Riddle'Thar is more a set of guidelines by which to live than a single entity, but some of his avatars like to appear as humble messengers of the gods. Also known as the Sugar God. Ruptga (Tall Papa): Chief deity of the Yokudan pantheon. Ruptga, more commonly 'Tall Papa', was the first god to figure out how to survive the Hunger of Satakal. Following his lead, the other gods learned the 'Walkabout', or a process by which they can persist beyond one lifetime. Tall Papa set the stars in the sky to show lesser spirits how to do this, too. When there were too many spirits to keep track of, though, Ruptga created a helper out the dead skin of past worlds. This helper is Sep (see below), who later creates the world of mortals. Satakal (The Worldskin): Yokudan god of everything. A fusion of the concepts of Anu and Padomay. Basically, Satakal is much like the Nordic Alduin, who destroys one world to begin the next. In Yokudan mythology, Satakal had done (and still does) this many times over, a cycle which prompted the birth of spirits that could survive the transition. These spirits ultimately become the Yokudan pantheon. Popular god of the Alik'r nomads. Sheogorath (The Mad God): The fearful obeisance of Sheogorath is widespread, and is found in most Tamrielic quarters. Contemporary sources indicate that his roots are in Aldmeri creation stories; therein, he is 'born' when Lorkhan's divine spark is removed. One crucial myth calls him the 'Sithis-shaped hole' of the world. Sheor (Bad Man): In Bretony, the Bad Man is the source of all strife. He seems to have started as the god of crop failure, but most modern theologians agree that he is a demonized version of the Nordic Shor, born during the dark years after the fall of Saarthal. Sep (The Snake): Yokudan version of Lorkhan. Sep is born when Tall Papa creates someone to help him regulate the spirit trade. Sep, though, is driven crazy by the hunger of Satakal, and he convinces some of the gods to help him make an easier alternative to the Walkabout. This, of course, is the world as we know it, and the spirits who followed Sep become trapped here, to live out their lives as mortals. Sep is punished by Tall Papa for his transgressions, but his hunger lives on as a void in the stars, a 'non-space' that tries to upset mortal entry into the Far Shores. Shezarr (God of Man): Cyrodilic version of Lorkhan, whose importance suffers when Akatosh comes to the fore of Imperial (really, Alessian) religion. Shezarr was the spirit behind all human undertaking, especially against Aldmeri aggression. He is sometimes associated with the founding of the first Cyrodilic battlemages. In the present age of racial tolerance, Shezarr is all but forgotten. Shor (God of the Underworld): Nordic version of Lorkhan, who takes sides with Men after the creation of the world. Foreign gods (i.e., Elven ones) conspire against him and bring about his defeat, dooming him to the underworld. Atmoran myths depict him as a bloodthirsty warrior king who leads the Nords to victory over their Aldmeri oppressors time and again. Before his doom, Shor was the chief of the gods. Sometimes also called Children's God (see Orkey, above). Sotha Sil (Mystery of Morrowind): God of the Dunmer, Sotha Sil is the least known of the divine Tribunal. He is said to be reshaping the world from his hidden, clockwork city. Stendarr (God of Mercy): God of the Nine Divines, Stendarr has evolved from his Nordic origins into a deity of compassion or, sometimes, righteous rule. He is said to have accompanied Tiber Septim in his later years. In early Altmeri legends, Stendarr is the apologist of Men. Stuhn (God of Ransom): Nordic precursor to Stendarr, brother of Tsun. Shield-thane of Shor, Stuhn was a warrior god that fought against the Aldmeri pantheon. He showed Men how to take, and the benefits of taking, prisoners of war. Syrabane (Warlock's God): An Aldmeri god-ancestor of magic, Syrabane aided Bendu Olo in the Fall of the Sload. Through judicious use of his magical ring, Syrabane saved many from the scourge of the Thrassian Plague. He is also called the Apprentices' God, for he is a favorite of the younger members of the Mages Guild. Tava (Bird God): Yokudan spirit of the air. Tava is most famous for leading the Yokudans to the isle of Herne after the destruction of their homeland. She has since become assimilated into the mythology of Kynareth. She is still very popular in Hammerfell among sailors, and her shrines can be found in most port cities. Tiber Septim (Talos, the Dragonborn): Heir to the Seat of Sundered Kings, Tiber Septim is the most important hero-god of Mankind. He conquered all of Tamriel and ushered in the Third Era (and the Third Empire). Also called Ysmir, 'Dragon of the North'. Trinimac: Strong god of the early Aldmer, in some places more popular than Auri-El. He was a warrior spirit of the original Elven tribes that led armies against the Men. Boethiah is said to have assumed his shape (in some stories, he even eats Trinimac) so that he could convince a throng of Aldmer to listen to him, which led to their eventual Chimeri conversion. He vanishes from the mythic stage after this, to return as the dread Malacath (Altmeri propaganda portrays this as the dangers of Dunmeri influence). Tsun: Extinct Nordic god of trials against adversity. Died defending Shor from foreign gods. Tu'whacca (Tricky God): Yokudan god of souls. Tu'whacca, before the creation of the world, was the god of Nobody Really Cares. When Tall Papa undertook the creation of the Walkabout, Tu'whacca found a purpose; he became the caretaker of the Far Shores, and continues to help Redguards find their way into the afterlife. His cult is sometimes associated with Arkay in the more cosmopolitan regions of Hammerfell. Vivec (Master of Morrowind): Warrior-poet god of the Dunmer. Vivec is the invisible keeper of the holy land, ever vigilant against the dark gods of the Volcano. He/she has saved the Dunmeri people from certain death on numerous occasions, most notably when he/she taught them how to breathe water for a day so that he/she could flood Morrowind and kill the Akaviri invaders, ca. 2E572. Xarxes: Xarxes is the god of ancestry and secret knowledge. He began as a scribe to Auri-El, and has kept track of all Aldmeri accomplishments, large and small, since the beginning of time. He created his wife, Oghma, from his favorite moments in history. Y'ffre (God of the Forest): Most important deity of the Bosmeri pantheon. While Auri-El Time Dragon might be the king of the gods, the Bosmer revere Y'ffre as the spirit of 'the now'. According to the Wood Elves, after the creation of the mortal plane everything was in chaos. The first mortals were turning into plants and animals and back again. Then Y'ffre transformed himself into the first of the Ehlnofey, or 'Earth Bones'. After these laws of nature were established, mortals had a semblance of safety in the new world, because they could finally understand it. Y'ffre is sometimes called the Storyteller, for the lessons he taught the first Bosmer. Some Bosmer still possess the knowledge of the chaos times, which they can use to great effect (the Wild Hunt). Ysmir (Dragon of the North): The Nordic aspect of Talos. He withstood the power of the Greybeards' voices long enough to hear their prophecy. Later, many Nords could not look on him without seeing a dragon. Z'en (God of Toil): Bosmeri god of payment in kind. Studies indicate origins in both Argonian and Akaviri mythologies, perhaps introduced into Valenwood by Kothringi sailors. Ostensibly an agriculture deity, Z'en sometimes proves to be an entity of a much higher cosmic order. His worship died out shortly after the Knahaten Flu. Zeht (God of Farms): Yokudan god of agriculture. Renounced his father after the world was created, which is why Tall Papa makes it so hard to grow food. Zenithar (God of Work and Commerce, Trader God): Member of the Nine Divines, Zenithar is understandably associated with Z'en. In the Empire, however, he is a far more cultivated god of merchants and middle nobility. His worshippers say, despite his mysterious origins, Zenithar is the god 'that will always win'.
String.Dest:
[pagebreak]

帝国内的多种信仰 帝国学院 米哈伊尔·卡库克索修士 著

[pagebreak]

此番我尝试对泰姆瑞尔各主流文化的信仰体系与相关神灵罗列了一份统计清单。这份清单绝不完善（仅希罗帝尔的帝都就拥有大量的圣人与圣精魂），这里只列举了各地文化中最重要的精魂。至于其他的艾’阿达，尤其是迪德拉，通常为众多文化所熟知，只有在具备特定的文化意义时才会纳入这里。我没有提及黑沼泽的阿尔贡信仰，因为我无力协调获取到的模糊且矛盾的描述。 八大信仰体系 希罗帝尔：阿卡托什、迪贝拉、阿尔凯、泽尼萨尔、玛拉、斯丹达尔、凯娜瑞丝、尤里安诺斯、舍扎尔、泰伯·塞普汀、莫里豪斯、雷曼 天际：奥杜因、迪贝拉、奥凯、苏恩、玛拉、斯图恩、凯娜、尤纳尔、舒尔、伊斯米尔、赫玛-莫拉、莫洛克 傲特莫：奥瑞-艾尔、崔尼马克、玛格努斯、希拉班、伊’符瑞、沙克斯、玛拉、斯丹达尔、洛克汗、菲纳斯特 波斯莫：奥瑞-艾尔、伊’符瑞、阿尔凯、泽’恩、沙克斯、班·达尔、玛拉、斯丹达尔、洛克汗、赫玛-莫拉、琼恩、琼德 丹莫：阿玛莱西亚、维威克、索萨·希尔、波耶西亚、梅法拉、阿祖拉、洛克汗、尼瑞瓦、莫拉格·巴尔、玛拉凯斯、谢尔格拉、梅鲁涅斯·大衮 尤库达：撒塔卡尔、卢普特迦、图’瓦卡、泽特、莫尔瓦、塔瓦、马洛可、蒂亚戈纳、塞普、洪丁、蕾姬、昂斯 布莱顿：阿卡托什、玛格努斯、伊’符瑞、迪贝拉、阿尔凯、泽尼萨尔、玛拉、斯丹达尔、凯娜瑞丝、尤里安诺斯、舒奥尔、菲纳斯特 艾斯维尔：阿尔科什、肯娜希、瑞德'塔尔、贾-卡'贾伊、玛拉、斯'仁达尔、洛卡吉、拉钦、班·达尔、阿祖菈、谢格拉斯 众神系神灵注记 阿卡托什（时间龙神）：阿卡托什是九圣灵（希罗帝尔人及其省份的主要宗教信仰）的主神，也是每个泰姆瑞尔宗教中都会出现的二神之一（另一个是洛克汗）。他通常被视为初始之地形成的第一位神，在其建构后，其他精魂发现了更加轻易的过程，世上各种各样的神系才得以出现。他是希罗帝尔帝国的终极神，体现了帝国的忍耐、无敌与永恒法统。 奥杜因（世界吞噬者）：奥杜因是阿卡托什在诺德的变体，只在表现上与九圣灵具有对应关系。例如，奥杜因在神话中描绘成了可怕肆虐的火焰风暴，摧毁了上一个世界，并开启了此时，故而被称作“世界吞噬者”。因此，诺德人把时间神视为世界末日的创造者与先兆。他并非诺德神系的主神（事实上，这一神系根本没有主神，参见下文的舒尔），但他确实是这一神系的源头，只不过可怕骇人。 阿尔科什（猫龙王）：安内奎那的前里’达塔王朝的神。是傲特莫的奥瑞-艾尔的变体，因此在早期卡吉特文化中将之视若阿卡托什般的英雄。他的信仰在瑞德'塔尔的确立过程中被吸纳合并，但仍在艾斯维尔的荒漠地区享有极高的声望。 阿玛莱西亚（晨风之母）：在古代凯莫所谓的“出走”神话中，阿卡托什的大部分痕迹都消失了，主要是由于该神在傲特莫中的联系与尊重。然而对于凡人种族来说，阿卡托什的大多数重要方面，即不朽性、历史性与谱系性，都在晨风广受欢迎的神圣审判席中顺理成章地在阿玛莱西亚身上得以体现。 阿尔凯（生死轮回之神）：九圣灵的一员，在其他地方也大受欢迎。在阿尔凯之父阿卡托什与时间联系不紧密或者很难理解其时间性的文化中，阿尔凯往往变得更为重要。他是埋葬与丧仪之神，有时也与四季有关。他的祭司们坚定地反对死灵法术和一切亡灵生物。据说洛克汗监督/催促/欺骗众神创世之前，阿尔凯并不存在。因此，他有时也被称作“凡人之神”。 奥瑞-艾尔（奥德莫之王）：奥瑞-艾尔就是精灵版的阿卡托什。奥瑞-艾尔是阿努-艾尔的灵魂，而阿努-艾尔则是“万物”阿努的灵魂。他是绝大多数奥德莫神系的主神。大多数傲特莫和波斯莫都自称奥瑞-艾尔的直系后裔。他唯一展现了软弱的时刻，他同意参与创造凡世，这一举动将精灵从永恒的精魂世界中永远分离出来。为了弥补这一点，奥瑞-艾尔在神话时代领导古奥德莫对抗洛克汗的大军，击败了暴君，建立了奥德莫、奥尔特莫拉、古伊诺菲的第一个王国。随后，他在追随者们的完全遵循下登天，以便他们可以学习到逃离尘世所需的步骤。 阿祖拉（黄昏与黎明女神）：阿祖拉是教导凯莫需要与傲特莫不同之秘的神祖。她的某些更传统的教义被视为来自波耶西亚。在故事中，阿祖拉通常更像是整个种族的集体精神力量，而非祖先或神灵。她也被称为索萨·希尔的预塑。在艾斯维尔，阿祖菈几乎是一个完全独立的实体，但仍然与卡吉特脱离傲特莫世系的起源联系在一起。 班·达尔（强盗之神）：在大多数地区，班·达尔都只是个无关紧要的神，是个盗贼与乞丐的骗子精魂。但在艾斯维尔，他的地位却更加重要，被视为下层人的守护神。在这方面，班·达尔成为了长期受苦的卡吉特的聪慧或绝境的天才，他最后一刻施展的计划总是能打乱他们的（精灵或人类）敌人的阴谋。 波耶西亚（阴谋君王）：根据先知维洛斯的预示，波耶西亚乃暗精灵神祖。在他的启发下，“变民”凯莫放弃了与奥德莫的所有联系，并根据迪德拉的法则建立了新的国度。暗精灵文化中从哲学到魔法再到“负责任”的建筑，一切“进步”都归功于波耶西亚。凯莫奋斗的原型故事，古维洛锡寓言中波耶西亚战胜各类敌人的英雄事迹比比皆是。他也被称为阿玛莱西亚的“预塑”。 蒂亚戈纳（侧刃之山铜神）：红卫人古老而凶残的信仰起源与尤库达二十七蛇族屠杀时期。蒂亚戈纳是洪丁（尤库达的开路之神，见下文）的法相，并获得永恒。他在打败左撇子精灵的战斗中发挥了至关重要的作用，在其中给尤库达人带来了山铜武器，并带领他们赢得了战争的胜利。在泰姆瑞尔，他领导着一群紧紧追随之人，在奥辛纽姆的兽人强盛之时与他们对抗，但后来却逐渐淡出了人们的生活。现在他不过是龙尾山脉地区一个地方性的强大精魂。 迪贝拉（美之女神）：九圣灵中颇受欢迎的神。在希罗帝尔，她坐拥十多个不同教派，一些致力于女性，一些致力于艺术家与美学，还有一些致力于情欲教育。 赫玛-莫拉（林中人）：远古阿特莫拉的恶魔，一度险些引诱诺德人变成奥德莫。大多数伊斯格拉默神话都是关于逃离老赫玛-莫拉诡计的故事。他也被称为知识恶魔，如果他与其兄弟/姐妹梅法拉有关，那么他与莫拉格堂（森人公会）的邪教起源也有模糊的关系。 洪丁（开路之神）：“勇毅面对异教徒”的尤库达精魂。从历史上来看，每当红卫人需要为族人“开路”，洪丁就会出现。在泰姆瑞尔历史上，这种情况只发生过三次——两次是在第一纪元拉·格达入侵期间，一次是泰伯战争期间。在最后的化身中，据说洪丁或是一把剑，或是一顶王冠，或者两者兼而有之。 尤纳尔（符文之神）：尘封已久的诺德神。在失去同神系其他神的青睐后，成为了九圣灵中的尤里安诺斯。在现代的天际神系中是缺席者。 琼德（大月神）：奥德莫的大月神。也称马瑟尔或玛拉之泪。在卡吉特宗教中，琼德只是月之障或贾-卡'贾伊的一个方面。 琼恩（小月神）：奥德莫的小月神。也称瑟坤达或斯丹达尔之哀。在卡吉特宗教中，琼恩是月之障或贾-卡'贾伊的一个方面。 尤里安诺斯（智慧与逻辑之神）：尤里安诺斯通常与诺德的语言与数学之父尤纳尔相联系，是文学、法律、历史和矛盾之神。由泰伯·塞普汀建立的尤里安诺斯修道会是上古卷轴的守护者。 凯娜（终末之吻）：诺德风暴女神。舒尔的遗孀，战士们钟爱之神。时常被称作“人类之母”。她的女儿们教会了第一批诺德人使用吐’目,风暴之声。 凯娜瑞丝（空气女神）：凯娜瑞丝是九圣灵之一，是最强大的天空精魂。在一些传说中，她是首肯洛克汗凡界计划之人，并在虚空中为其创造之举提供了空间。她还与雨水有关，据说这种天象在洛克汗的神圣火花被移除前并不会发生。 蕾姬（精魂之剑的圣徒）：蕾姬是大老爹之女，千变万化剑术的女神。在神话纪元，尤库达的纳-托塔姆布陷入停滞，以决定谁来领导对左撇子精灵的冲锋。然而，众位剑术大师在各路著名剑招上皆十分熟练，以至于势均力敌。蕾姬引入了“短暂佯攻”。后来胜者出现，与奥德莫的战争开始了。 洛克汗（缺位神）：这位创造、诡计、试炼之神存在于泰姆瑞尔每个神话体系之中。他最受欢迎的名字是奥德莫的“洛克汗”或“厄运之鼓”。他说服或策动了原灵们创造了凡人的天地，打破了现状，一如其父帕多梅在初始之地带来了不稳定一样。在世界始奠，洛克汗与他的神圣中心相分离，有时被描述为非自主之举，并在艾’阿达的造物间徘徊。他和他的“万物计划”的形上地位被以各种方式解释。例如，在晨风，他是与“赛伊克奋进”有关的存在，由此过程，凡人被赋予超越创造他们的神的责任。对于高精灵来说，他是所有高等力量中最邪恶的，正是他永远破坏了他们与精魂位面的联系。在传说中，他几乎总是奥德莫的敌人，因此，他也是早期人类的英雄。 洛卡吉（月兽）：安内奎那的前里’达塔王朝的神，很容易识别出其与缺位神洛克汗的关系。 玛格努斯（魔法）：魔法之神玛格努斯在最后一刻退出了创世，尽管他也为此付出了高昂的代价。他在这个世界所留之物被凡人视作魔法用来感知和控制。有故事说，虽然创世是洛克汗提出的，但玛格努斯创造了建造凡人位面所需的图纸。他有时被星盘、望远镜所代表，但更常见的象征则是法杖。希罗帝尔的传说称他可以寄宿在强大的法师体内，并把自己的力量借予他们。且与“幽域之主”祖林·阿克图斯有关。 玛拉凯斯（诅咒之神）：玛拉凯斯是曾为崔尼马克的复活粪便。暗精灵称之为“玛拉克”，是个有些软弱但复仇心极强的迪德拉，也是兽人的神王。他一直在试炼丹莫身体上的弱点。 马洛可（部落之王）：拉·格达的敌神。在第一纪元率领哥布林对抗红卫人。当洪丁大军追赶他的哥布林部落时，他逃亡东方。 莫洛克（玛拉凯斯）：兽人之神莫洛克长期困扰着哈拉尔德王的继承人们。在大约1E660年的龙墙之战中大败后，逃亡东方。据说他的愤怒包含恶毒的仇恨充斥了天空，后来此年被称为“夏之冬年”。 玛拉（爱之女神）：几乎普世而存的女神。起源于神话时代，是生育女神。在天际，她是凯娜的侍女。在帝国，她是母神。她有时和“阿努之歌”中的孕育万物的宇宙女性法则——尼尔相联系。根据宗教不同，她或是阿卡托什之妻，或是洛克汗之妻，或是二者的侍妾。 梅鲁涅斯·大衮（毁灭之神）：十分流行的迪德拉神灵。与火灾、地震和洪水等自然灾害联系在一起。然而在某些文化中，大衮只是流血与背叛之神。他在晨风地位重要，象征着那里近乎荒凉的地形。 梅法拉（雌雄同体者）：梅法拉是织网者或称蜘蛛神。在晨风，他/她是教导凯莫躲避敌人或秘密谋杀敌人所需技能的先祖。昔日，凯莫势单力薄，树敌众多。他/她与波耶西亚一起，组织了最终成为大家族之基的氏族制度。他/她还创立了莫拉格堂。也被称为维威克的预塑。 莫拉格·巴尔（计划之神、强暴之王）：在晨风是十分重要的迪德拉神。在那里，他总是被视为阴谋君王波耶西亚的劲敌。他是阻碍丹莫（过去的凯莫）的主要来源。传说中，莫拉格·巴尔总是试图破坏家族的血脉，或者破坏丹莫的“纯洁性”。据说有个居住在莫拉格·阿穆尔的超级怪物种族，就是他在上一个时代诱惑维威克的结果。 莫里豪斯（人类初息）：希罗-诺德古代文化中的英雄神。传说他是大本营的接管者，这一神话时代之举，建立了人类对谷中腹地的控制。他经常与诺德的吐'目之力相联系，因此也与凯娜瑞丝有关。 莫尔瓦（乳神）：尤库达的繁衍女神。尤库达神系中基础性的神灵，也是大老爹妻子中最受钟爱者。在落锤的很多地方仍有信仰，其中包括斯卓·米'凯。莫尔瓦总被描述成四只手，这样就能“抓住更多的丈夫”。 尼瑞瓦（弑神者）：旧时维洛斯黄金时代，瑞斯戴恩的凯莫之王。在红山之战中被杀的尼瑞瓦是三位一体之路的先驱，也是丹莫信仰中最重要的圣人。据说他杀了末代矮人王杜马克，并享用了他的心脏。 昂斯（剔骨者）：尤库达拉·格达人著名的战神，昂斯教导了人类如何将小刀变成了长剑。 奥凯（古老敲击者）：古诺德的借出神，似乎在奥德莫统治阿特莫拉时期就被信仰。诺德人们相信自己曾经与精灵一样长寿，直到奥凯出现。奥凯通过异教诡计，诱骗他们做了一笔交易，“把他们绑在了严冬计数上”。相传某时，诺德人因奥凯的邪恶魔法，只剩下六年寿命。然而舒尔出现，并通过未知方法解除了诅咒，把大部分诅咒丢给了附近的兽人。 菲纳斯特：夏暮岛的英雄神，教导傲特莫如何通过缩减走路的步幅来轻而易举地延寿百年。 拉钦（拦路贼）：卡吉特的贼神，成长在森丘的黑奇尔葛地区。拉钦是艾斯维尔史上最著名的窃贼，据说曾在金泰拉女皇睡觉时偷走了她脖子上的一个纹身。 雷曼（希罗帝尔）：第二帝国中的文化神英雄。雷曼是阿卡维尔入侵中最伟大的英雄。事实上，他说服了入侵者帮忙建立他自己的帝国，并征服了除晨风外的全泰姆瑞尔。他制定了称为皇帝的仪式，其中包括关于蕴含巨大力量的灵魂石--诸王护符的仪式。他的皇朝在第一纪元末期被丹莫的莫拉格堂所终结。他也被称为尘世神。 瑞德'塔尔（双月之舞）：卡吉特的宇宙秩序之神，瑞德'塔尔是由“鬃鬣”先知里德'塔尔-里'达塔启示给艾斯维尔的。瑞德'塔尔更像是一整套生活指南，而非单个实体，但他的一些化身喜欢以神灵的谦卑使者的身份出现。他也被称为“糖神”。 卢普特迦（大老爹）：尤库达神系的主神。卢普特迦（更多被叫做“大老爹”），是第一个明晰了如何在“撒塔卡尔之饥”中生存下来的神。在他的带领下，其他神也学会了“漫步”，或者说一种可以坚持一生的过程。大老爹将群星挂在天上，告诉那些次级精魂如何行动。然而，当太多精魂无法追踪时，卢普特迦从过去世界的死皮中创造了一个帮手。这个帮手就是塞普（见下文），塞普后来创造了凡人世界。 撒塔卡尔（世界之皮）：尤库达的万物之神。是阿努与帕多梅两个概念的融合。在基本面貌上，撒塔卡尔很像诺德的奥杜因，摧毁一世，开启一世。在尤库达神话中，撒塔卡尔已经如此多次（现在仍在继续），这个循环促使能够在过渡中生存下来的精魂的诞生。这些精魂最终构成了尤库达神系。他也是阿里克’尔牧民最喜欢的神。 谢尔格拉（疯神）：对谢尔格拉的可怕信仰很是普遍，在泰姆瑞尔大多数地方都能找到。当代资料已经整理出他在奥德莫创造故事中的起源，彼时，洛克汗的神圣火花被移除时，他就“诞生”了。一个重要的神话把他称为世界的“西帝斯型之洞”。 舒奥尔（恶徒）：在布莱顿，“恶徒”是一切冲突的根源。一开始他似乎是农业歉收之神，但大多数现代神学家都认为他是诺德的舒尔被妖魔化的产物，诞生于萨瑟尔陷落后的黑暗岁月。 塞普（蛇）：尤库达版的洛克汗。大老爹需要创造一个人来帮助他管理精魂的交易，塞普就此诞生。然而，塞普被“撒塔卡尔之饥”逼疯，说服了一些神来帮助他制造一个更容易的替代品来取代“漫步”。当然这就是我们所熟知的世界，追随塞普的精魂被困于其中，以凡人身份度过了一生。塞普因过错受到大老爹的惩罚，群星中试图阻止凡人进入“远岸”的“非空间”——虚空，正是他仍旧存在的饥饿。 舍扎尔（人类之神）：希罗帝尔版的洛克汗，当阿卡托什出现在帝国（实际上是阿莱西亚）宗教的前列时，他的重要性受到了影响。舍扎尔是一切人类事业背后的精神，尤其是在面对奥德莫的侵略时。他有时被视为第一批希罗帝尔战斗法师的创始人。而在这个种族宽容的时代，舍扎尔几乎被遗忘。 舒尔（幽域之神）：诺德版的洛克汗，在创世之后站在了人类一边。外邦神（即精灵神）密谋反对他，导致他失败，注定堕入幽域。阿特莫拉神话把他描述成一个嗜血的战士王，领导诺德人一次又一次战士压迫他们的奥德莫。舒尔在被毁灭前曾是众神之首。有时他也被称作“孩童之神”（见上文奥凯）。 索萨·希尔（晨风之秘）：丹莫神索萨·希尔是神圣审判席中最不为人所知的。据说他正在那隐秘的发条城中重塑世界。 斯丹达尔（仁慈之神）：斯丹达尔身为九圣灵之一已经从诺德原型演变成了一位富有同情心的神，有时，还象征着正义的统治。据说泰伯·塞普汀在晚年时就有他的陪伴。在早期的傲特莫传说中，斯丹达尔是为人类辩护者。 斯图恩（赎金之神）：斯丹达尔的诺德前身，苏恩的兄弟。舒尔的盾牌武卫。斯图恩是与奥德莫众神作战的战士神。他向人类展示了如何俘获战俘，并从中获益。 希拉班（术士之神）：奥德莫的魔法神祖，在攻陷斯洛德之战中帮助了本笃·奥罗。希拉班通过明智的使用他的魔法戒指，在瑟拉斯瘟疫中救了许多人。又因广受法师公会年轻成员的喜爱，也被称作“学徒之神”。 塔瓦（鸟神）：尤库达的空之精魂。塔瓦最著名的事迹就是带领尤库达人在家园被毁后抵达了赫恩岛。自那以后，她就融入了凯娜瑞丝神话。她在落锤的水手当中仍然很受欢迎，大多数口岸城市都能找到她的祭坛。 泰伯·塞普汀（塔洛斯，龙裔）：分裂诸王的继承人。泰伯·塞普汀是人类最重要的英雄神。他征服了整个泰姆瑞尔，开创了第三纪元（与第三帝国）。有时也被称为“北方巨龙”伊斯米尔。 崔尼马克：早期奥德莫的强大之神，在某些地方甚至比奥瑞-艾尔更受欢迎。他是原始精灵诸部的战士精魂，率领军队对抗人类。据说波耶西亚变作他的样貌（有些故事中甚至是将崔尼马克吃掉），说服力一大群奥德莫聆听他的教诲，导致他们最终皈依变成了凯莫。自此之后，他从神话舞台上消失，以可怕的玛拉凯斯身份回归（傲特莫的宣传中将其描绘成丹莫的影响之危险）。 苏恩：已死的反抗逆境的诺德试炼之神。为了保护舒尔免受异族神的攻击而死。 图’瓦卡（狡猾神）：尤库达的灵魂之神。图’瓦卡在创世之前无人在乎。当大老爹着手开始“漫步”之旅的建造时，图’瓦卡找到了自己的目标，他成为了远岸的守护者，并继续帮助红卫人找到通往来世的道路。他的教派在落锤较为国际化的地区会与阿尔凯联系在一起。 维威克（晨风之主）：丹莫的战诗人神。维威克是圣地的隐形守护者，永远警惕着火山内的黑暗之神。他/她在许多情况拯救丹莫于死亡，其中在约2E572年，他/她教会了丹莫如何水下呼吸一天，以便他/她可以淹没晨风并杀死阿卡维尔入侵者。 沙克斯：沙克斯是祖先与秘密知识之神。他最初是奥瑞-艾尔的书吏，从时间之始记录着奥德莫大大小小的成就。他根据自己最喜欢的历史时刻创造了自己的妻子奥格玛。 伊’符瑞（森林之神）：波斯莫信仰中最重要的神。虽然时间之龙奥瑞-艾尔可能是诸神之王，但波斯莫将伊’符瑞视为“当下”的精魂。根据木精灵的说法，在凡世初创，一切陷入混乱。第一批凡人在动物植物之间往复变换其形。伊’符瑞随即将之变成了第一个伊诺菲（或“地骨”）。在这些自然法则确立以后，新世界终于拥有了安全的表象，因为凡人终于可以理解它了。伊’符瑞有时被称作“叙事者”，因为他教导了第一批波斯莫。有些波斯莫仍然具备混沌时代的知识，他们可以利用这些知识产生巨大的影响（即狂猎）。 伊斯米尔（北方巨龙）：塔洛斯的诺德法相。他顶住了灰胡子的吼声之力，聆听了他们的预言。后来许多诺德人每目视于他都能看见一条龙。 泽’恩（辛劳之神）：波斯莫的实物交易之神。研究表明其起源于阿尔贡和阿卡维尔神话，可能是由柯斯林吉人水手引入威伦森林。表面上是农业之神，有时却被证明是一个更高级别的宇宙秩序实体。他的信仰在纳哈腾流感后不久就消失了。 泽特（农场之神）：尤库达的农业之神。在创世之后即与其父断绝关系，因此大老爹让种植粮食变得十分艰难。 泽尼萨尔（工作与商业之神、商人神）：九圣灵之一的泽尼萨尔与泽’恩相联系是不难理解的。然而在帝国，他是更有教养的商人与中层贵族之神。他的信徒们说，尽管他的起源神秘，但他确实“常胜”神。

String.Source: Sapphire + Salt Pile + Albino Spider Pod
String.Dest: 蓝宝石+盐堆+白化蜘蛛卵囊

String.Source: I agreed to fund Ralis Sedarys' expedition to excavate Kolbjorn Barrow. He told me to return in a few days to see how work was progressing.
String.Dest: 我同意了资助拉里斯·瑟达瑞斯对寇泊琼古坟的发掘。他告诉我过几天来看看进展。

String.Source: Adril Arano has requested my help with what he believes is an imminent threat on Councilor Morvayn's life. He believes members of the Ulen family are present on the island and seek revenge upon Morvayn for executing their ancestor. He's requested that I investigate the identity of any possible assassins, hoping to catch them unaware. Adril suggests that I start by speaking to Geldis Sadri, the innkeeper at the Retching Netch Cornerclub. He's hoping that Geldis, who has quite the reputation as a gossip, might have some useful leads.
String.Dest: 阿德尔·阿拉诺请我帮忙解决莫尔凡议员遭受的生命威胁。他认为乌伦家的某些成员就在岛上，是为报莫尔凡议员处决其先祖之仇而来。他请我调查刺客的身份，想要不走漏风声地抓捕他们。他建议我先去和呕吐气母街角吧老板吉迪斯·萨德利交流一下。他希望以八卦闻名的吉迪斯能派上用场。

String.Source: Flawless Sapphire + Damaged Albino Spider Pod
String.Dest: 无瑕蓝宝石+受损的白化蜘蛛卵囊

String.Source:
<font face='$HandwrittenFont'><font size='18'> I am stronger now. The heart stone kept me alive after Neloth's butchery. I can feel the bones in the ash calling to me. I can call back to them too. With the heart stone I can bind the spirits to bone and ash and raise a servant to do my bidding.
Tonight I will seize control. These miners and fortune seekers are pawns of Neloth. I can feel them staring at me. I'm sure they are sending him messages, reporting on my every move. The only ones I can trust are the voices. They've never lied to me. They've shown me that these fools plan to betray me, just like Neloth.
When they are all asleep, I will raise my ash spawn. Their brute of a leader will die first. I can see the lust in his heart. He may act all kind and generous, but I know what he wants, what they all want. They want the heart stone.
I'll keep a few prisoners. I need test subjects for my experiments. There is more that the heart stone can do. I just need to try out a few ideas.
String.Dest:
<font face='$HandwrittenFont'><font size='18'> 我现在更强了。心石让我在内洛斯的杀戮下活了下来。我能感受到灰烬中的骸骨在呼唤我。我也要回应他们。有了这颗心石，我就能把精魂束缚在骨与灰烬中，还能让奴仆听我号令。
今晚我将掌握控制权。这些矿工和寻宝者都是内洛斯的爪牙。我能感觉到他们正在盯着我。他们肯定会给他报信，监视我的一举一动。我唯一能相信的就是那些声音。它们从不骗我。它们让我知道了这些傻子打算和内洛斯一样背叛我。
等他们都睡着，我唤醒了我的灰烬怪。他们的野蛮头子会第一个死。我能看得到他内心的欲望。他可能表现得很善良慷慨，但我知道他想要什么，他们都想要什么。他们都想要心石。
我要留几个俘虏做实验。心石还能做更多事。我需要尝试一些想法。

String.Source: I agreed to fund Ralis Sedarys' expedition to excavate Kolbjorn Barrow, but in order to continue, he needs more gold to hire replacements for the workers that have been killed.
String.Dest: 我同意了资助拉里斯·瑟达瑞斯对寇泊琼古坟的发掘，但为了复工，他需要更多金币来招募人手，换下那些被杀的工人。

String.Source:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont"> Dear Wulf,
I'm writing to you now as I already feel my mind going. I fear that soon I will not be able to write at all. I came to the wilderness to better know the All-Maker. But in the wilds I found a force greater than any we've known.
It is seductive, this power I've been granted. I know you would not approve, so I don't dare show my face to you. But know that I realized the greatest potential of my strength before the last.
I hope to one day meet you before the All-Maker, brother.
- Torkild </font>
String.Dest:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont"> 亲爱的沃弗：
我现在给你写信，因为我已经能感到思维不济了。恐怕很快我就无法写字了。我到荒野里，希望能够更好地了解造物主。但我在野外发现了一种前所未有的力量。
太诱人了，我被赋予的力量太诱人了。我知道你不会同意，所以我不敢在你面前露面。但你要知道，我在最后一刻到来前发现了自己最大的潜能。
希望有一天，在造物主面前与你相见，兄弟。
--托基尔德 </font>

String.Source: I agreed to fund Ralis Sedarys' expedition to excavate Kolbjorn Barrow, but work on the site has stopped. Most of the miners were killed by another wave of Draugr attacks, and two more are unaccounted for. I dealt with the Draugr, but still need to locate the missing miners.
String.Dest: 我同意了资助拉里斯·瑟达瑞斯对寇泊琼古坟的发掘，但现场施工已经中止。大多数矿工死于又一波尸鬼袭击，还有两名矿工下落不明。我打败了尸鬼，但还得去找消失的矿工们。

String.Source: You can't make out the writing on this tablet.

Mora's Agony: Summons a field of writhing tentacles that poison foes.
You may only accept one ability from this book at a time.
String.Dest:
莫拉的痛苦：召唤一片扭曲的触手，毒害敌人。
你每次只能接受书中的一种能力。

String.Source:
[pagebreak]

Lives of the Saints

[pagebreak]

f you would be wise, model your lives on the lives of the saints. If you would learn valor, follow St. Nerevar the Captain, patron of Warriors and Statesmen. Lord Nerevar helped to unite the barbarian Dunmer tribes into a great nation, culminating in his martyrdom when leading the Dunmer to victory against the evil Dwemer and the traitorous House Dagoth in the Battle of Red Mountain. If you would learn daring, follow Saint Veloth the Pilgrim, Patron of Outcasts and Spiritual Seekers. Saint Veloth, prophet and mystic, led the Dunmer out of the decadent home country of the Summerset Isles and into the promised land of Morrowind. Saint Veloth also taught the difference between the Good and Bad Daedra, and won the aid of the Good Daedra for his people while teaching how to carefully negotiate with the Bad Daedra. If you would learn generosity, follow Saint Rilms the Barefooted, Patron of Pilgrims and Beggars. Saint Rilms gave away her shoes, then dressed and appeared as a beggar to better acquaint herself with the poor. If you would learn self-respect and respect for others, follow Saint Aralor the Penitent, Patron of Tanners and Miners. This foul criminal repented his sins and traveled a circuit of the great pilgrimages on his knees. If you would learn mercy and its fruits, follow Saint Seryn the Merciful, Patron of Brewers, Bakers, Distillers. This pure virgin of modest aspect could heal all diseases at the price of taking the disease upon herself. Tough-minded and fearless, she took on the burdens of others, and bore those burdens to an honored old age. If you would learn fierce justice, follow Saint Felms the Bold, Patron of Butchers and Fishmongers. This brave warlord slew the Nord invaders and drove them from our lands. He could neither read nor write, receiving inspiration directly from the lips of Almsivi. If you would learn pride of race and tribe, follow Saint Roris the Martyr, Patron of Furnishers and Caravaners. Captured by Argonians just before the Arnesian War, Roris proudly refused to renounce the Tribunal faith, and withstood the cruel tortures of Argonian sorcerers. Vengeance and justice for the martyred Saint Roris was the rallying cry of the Arnesian War. If you would learn the rule of law and justice, follow Saint Olms the Just, Patron of Chandlers and Clerks. Founder of the Ordinators, Saint Olms conceived and articulated the fundamental principles of testing, ordeal, and repentance. If you would learn benevolence, follow Saint Delyn the Wise, Patron of Potters and Glassmakers. Saint Delyn was head of House Indoril, a skilled lawyer, and author of many learned treatises on Tribunal law and custom. If you would learn the love of peace, follow Saint Meris the Peacemaker, Patron of Farmers and Laborers. As a little girl, Saint Meris showed healing gifts, and trained as a Healer. She ended a long and bloody House War, intervening on the battlefield in her white robe to heal warriors and spellcrafters without regard to faction. The troops of all House adopted white robes as her standard, and refused to shed the blood of their brethren. If you would learn reverence, follow Saint Llothis the Pious, Patron of Tailors and Dyers. Contemporary and companion of the Tribunals, and the best-loved Alma Rula of the Tribunal Temple, he formulated the central rituals and principles of the New Temple Faith. Saint Llothis is the symbolic mortal bridge between the gods and the faithful, and the archetypal priest.
String.Dest:
[pagebreak]

圣人之道

[pagebreak]

如果你想拥有智慧，那便追寻圣者们的脚步生活吧。 若你想了解勇敢，追寻领袖圣尼瑞瓦的脚步吧。他是战士与政治家们的守护者。尼瑞瓦大人统一了尚未开化的诸丹莫部落，将其建设为一个伟大的国家。红山之战中，他率领丹莫击败了邪恶的锻莫，以及背叛了丹莫的达格斯家族，但圣尼瑞瓦本人也在此战中殒命。 若你想了解开拓，追寻朝圣者圣维洛斯的脚步吧。他是放逐者与寻求心灵者们的守护者。圣维洛斯，先知与秘智者，带领丹莫从颓废堕落之乡夏暮岛离开，来到这应允的晨风之地。圣维洛斯还教导了丹莫如何分辨迪德拉的好坏。在为他的人民争取到好迪德拉援助的同时，又教导他们如何小心地与坏迪德拉谈判。 若你想了解慷慨，追寻赤脚圣瑞姆斯的脚步吧。她是朝圣者与乞丐们的守护者。圣瑞姆斯将她的鞋子送给穷人，又以乞丐的身份生活，只为了更深切地体会劳苦大众们的痛楚。 若你想了解尊严与尊重，追寻悔罪者圣阿拉诺的脚步吧。他是皮革匠与矿工的守护者。阿拉诺曾是一名肮脏的罪犯，为表悔罪之心，他长途环绕，膝行朝圣。 若你想了解仁慈及其恩惠，追寻慈悲的圣瑟琳的脚步吧。她是酿酒师、面包师、酒厂工人们的守护者。这位纯洁而端庄的处女可以治愈一切疾病，但代价却是将疾病转移到她自己身上。她以坚定无畏的意志接过他人的负担，并将其背负终生。 若你想了解严厉的正义，追寻勇者圣费姆斯的脚步吧。他是屠夫与渔夫们的守护者。这位骁勇的军阀奋力杀敌，将诺德侵略者赶出了我们的土地。他目不识丁，却直接从阿玛希维处得到神启。 若你想了解民族与部落的骄傲，追寻殉道者圣洛瑞斯的脚步吧。他是供应商与旅行商人们的守护者。洛瑞斯在阿尼西亚战争前被阿尔贡俘虏，但是他以凛然之姿拒绝放弃自己对审判席的信仰，无论阿尔贡巫师如何以酷刑折磨他，他依旧虔诚不改。为殉道者圣洛瑞斯复仇和讨回公道成为了阿尼西亚战争中的战斗口号。 若你想了解公正与律法的规则，追寻公正者圣奥姆斯的脚步吧。他是货商与文员的守护者，也是协调使的创始人。圣奥姆斯构想并阐释了测试、磨炼与悔罪的基本原则。 若你想了解仁爱，追寻智者圣德林的脚步吧。他是陶工和玻璃工们的守护者。圣德林曾是因多瑞尔家族的首领，也是一位经验丰富的律师，著有许多审判席法律与习俗的论文。 若你想了解和平之爱，追寻止战者圣梅里斯的脚步吧。她是农民与劳动者们的守护者。圣梅里斯从小便展露出了治愈的天赋，并在日后成为了一名治疗师。她终结了一场漫长而血腥的家族斗争。在战场上，她着一袭白袍穿梭于阵间，不分派别地治疗战士与法师们。最后，所有家族的军队都以她白袍为旗帜，并立誓再不自相残杀。 若你想了解敬畏，追寻虔信者圣里洛菲斯的脚步吧。他是裁缝与染匠的守护者。他是审判席的同代人和伙伴，也是最受神殿所爱的阿玛·鲁拉。他制定了新神殿信仰的主要仪式和原则。圣里洛菲斯也是神明与信徒之间象征性的凡俗桥梁，更是所有神职人员的典范。

String.Source: I met a group of Nords from Thirsk Mead Hall, which has recently been overrun by Rieklings. They're camped on the eastern shore of the island, waiting for an opportunity to attack and take back their home.
String.Dest: 我遇到了一群被瑞克灵赶出瑟斯科蜜酒大厅的诺德人。他们驻扎在岛的东岸，等待着进攻时机，夺回他们的家园。

String.Source:
[pagebreak]

The Poison Song Book IV By Bristin Xel

[pagebreak]

cra sat by the hearth in Tay's room, reading her book by the fire. It concerned some minutiae of theosophy that she did not believe in, but nevertheless found morbidly compelling. When the door opened and she heard Tay enter, she finished the paragraph she was reading before looking up. "I've been here for hours, darling. If I knew you were going to be so late, I would have brought more books," she giggled. When she saw Tay's face and the state of his clothing, her manner lost all frivolity. "What happened to you? Are you all right?" "I've been to see my old childhood nursemaid, Edebah," he said in a strange voice. "It was a sudden change of plans. I hadn't realized she was in Mournhold." "I wish I had known where you were going," she said, rising slowly from her chair. "I would have loved to have met her." "Well, it's too late now. I've killed her." Acra inhaled deeply, studying Tay's frozen face. She took his hand. "Perhaps you ought to tell me everything." Tay let his beloved lead him to the hearth, where he sat blinking at the fire. He looked down at the silver ring on his finger. "Before I killed her, she gave me this. It's the sealing ring of the House Dagoth. She told me I was the bearer of the inheritance, and the Song I hear all the time in my head, the one that called me to kill another boy when I was young, and then Edebah herself, is the Song of my ancestors." Tay fell silent. Acra knelt by his side, stroking his ringed hand. "Tell me more." "My tutor Kena Gafrisi taught us that the House Dagoth was a curse on Morrowind. He said that when they were all destroyed at the end of the War, the very earth itself breathed in relief," Tay closed his eyes. "I can see the obliteration. I can even hear it in the Song. Edebah told me that the five Houses adopted the orphan children of Dagoth, raising them in their own traditions. I thought she was mad or a liar, but the real lie was all those years I thought my family was House Indoril." "What are you going to do?" Acra whispered. "Well, Edebah told me to follow the Song to my destiny," Tay laughed bitterly. "But the Song led me to kill her, so I don't know if she'd still give me that recommendation now. I know that I need to leave Mournhold. Before I knew what I was doing, I set a fire in her tenement. The guards were called. I just don't know where I'd go." "You have many friends to shield you if you prove yourself to be the new leader of the return of the Sixth House," Acra kissed the ring. "I will help you find them." Tay stared at her. "Why would you help me?" "When you thought I was your cousin of the House Indoril, you did not mind having me though it might well have been incestuous," Acra replied, meeting his eyes. "I have heard the Song too. It is not as strong with me as it was with you, but I never chose to ignore it. It taught me more than the ridiculous Temple priests and priestesses ever could. I knew that my true name was Dagoth-Acra, and I knew that I had a brother." "No," Tay said through gritted teeth. "You're lying." "You are Dagoth-Tython." Tay shoved Acra hard against the wall and ran from the room. As he fled through the hall, he heard the sound of Kalkorith's footfall on the stairs behind him, a percussive instrument in the Song that was rising in his heart and head "Cousin," the senior initiate was saying. "Have you heard about the fire--" Tay unsheathed his dagger and turned, burying it to the hilt in Kalkorith's throat. "Cousin," he hissed. "I am not your cousin." The streets of Mournhold were lit by the red glow of the tenement fire, spreading through the tight alleyways by a steady and intense gust of wind. It was as if Dagoth-Ur himself was looming over the city, fanning the flames his heir had struck. A House guard, running toward the blaze, stopped at the sight of Tay, standing uncertainly, swaying, before the front door of Kalkorith's house, a bloodied blade in his hand. "What you done, serjo?" Tay ran for the forest, his cape whipping behind him by the force of the howling wind. The guard clambered after him, sword drawn. He had no need to investigate the house to see the murder. He knew. For hours, Tay raced through the wilderness, the Song pushing him onward. The sound of his pursuer faded away. At last, the trees thinned, and he saw nothing before him but air and water. A cliff, a hundred foot long plunge into the Inner Sea. The Song told him no. It pulled him north, sweetly promising a place to rest among friends. More than friends -- people who would worship him as the heir of Dagoth. As he slowly walked toward the edge of the cliff, the Song became more threatening, warning him not to seek to avoid his fate. There was no escape in death. Tay spat a curse upon his House and threw himself head first over the cliff. It was another glorious day on the island of Gorne, the first one in weeks that Baynarah could truly enjoy. Uncle Triffith had important company, Housemen from far away, and she had been required to attend every dinner, every meeting, every ceremony. As a child, she remembered, she had hoped for some attention. Now nothing was more blissful than time away from her duties. There was only one thing she wanted to do that she had to do indoors, and that was writing a letter to her cousin. But that could wait until the evening, she told herself. After all, he had not written her in many days. It was the influence of that girl, Acra. Not that she seemed disagreeable, but Baynarah knew how one's first love can be all-consuming. At least, she had read about it. As she walked idly through the wildflower meadow, Baynarah was so distracted with her thoughts that she did not hear her maid Hillima calling. She was quite startled when she turned to see the young servant running up. "Serjo," she said, breathlessly. "Please come! Someone has washed up on the shore! It's your cousin, Serjo Indoril-Tay!"

毒之歌 卷四 布里斯汀·泽尔 著

[pagebreak]

阿克拉坐在忒伊房间的壁炉旁，就着炉火读她的书。这本书涉及一些她不相信的神学的细枝末节，但仍然发觉其中有一种病态的说服力。门打开时她听到忒伊进来了，她读完正在看的那一段，然后抬起头来。 “我已经在这里呆了几个小时了，亲爱的。如果早知道你这么晚才回来，我会多带几本书。”她咯咯地笑着说。等她看到忒伊的面色和衣服，神态不复轻佻。“你怎么了？你还好吧？” “我见到了我童年时的老保姆，艾德芭，”他用一种奇怪的嗓音说。“不是我的安排。我本来不知道到她在哀伤之城。” “我要是早知道你会去哪儿就好了，”她说着从椅子上慢慢站起来。“我也很想见见她。” “唉，现在已经太晚了。我杀了她。” 阿克拉深吸了口气，打量着忒伊冷若冰霜的面孔。她握住他的手。“也许你应该告诉我一切。” 忒伊任由他的爱人牵他到壁炉前，他坐下来目光迷离地看着火光。他又低头看了看自己手指上的银戒指。“在我杀她之前，她给了我这个。这是达格斯家族的印戒。她告诉我，我是这份遗产的继承人，而我脑子里一直听到的那首歌，那首在我幼时叫我去杀另一个男孩的歌，那首后来又让我杀死艾德芭本人的歌，是我祖先的歌。” 忒伊陷入了沉默。阿克拉跪在他身边，抚摸着他戴戒指的手。“告诉我更多。” “我的老师凯勒·加弗里斯教导我们说，达格斯家族是对晨风的诅咒。他说，当他们在战争结束时被赶尽杀绝时，大地本身也松了一口气。”忒伊闭上了眼睛。“我能看到那场毁灭。我甚至能在歌声中听到它。艾德芭告诉我，五个家族收养了达格斯家的孤儿，用他们自己的传统抚养他们。我本来以为她疯了或者是个骗子，这些年里我一直相信自己的家人是因多瑞尔一族，没想到这才是真正的谎言。” “你打算怎么做？”阿克拉低声说。 “好吧，艾德芭告诉我跟着歌声去追寻自己的命运，”忒伊苦笑道。“但那首歌让我杀了她，所以我不知道换现在她是否还会这么建议。我知道，我得离开哀伤之城。在我意识到自己在做什么之前，我放火烧了她的住处。人们叫了守卫。我只是不知道我该去哪里。” “如果你能证明自己是第六家族复兴的新领袖，会有很多朋友保护你。”阿克拉吻了一下戒指。“我会帮你找到他们。” 忒伊盯着她。“你为什么要帮我？” “当你觉得我是你因多瑞尔家族的堂亲时，你并不介意要我，尽管这很有可能是在乱伦，”阿克拉直视他的双眼。“我也能听见那首歌。它在我身上不如在你身上那样强烈，但我从未选择忽视它。它教给我的东西比那些可笑的神殿祭司教给我的要多得多。我知道我的真名是达格斯-阿克拉，我知道我有一个兄弟。” “不，”泰勒咬牙切齿。“你在撒谎。” “你是达格斯-忒伊松。” 忒伊将阿克拉一把推到墙上，跑出房间。穿过大厅时，他听到卡寇斯在他身后楼梯的脚步击出为他心中与脑海里腾起歌声伴奏的节拍。 “堂弟，”这位资深门徒说道。“你有没有听说火灾......” 忒伊拔出匕首转过身来，一刀连柄插入卡寇斯的喉咙。“堂弟，”他嘶吼道。“我不是你的堂弟。” 哀伤之城的街道被旧屋火灾的红光照亮，在强劲不息的阵风中从逼仄的巷子里蔓延开来。仿佛达格斯·乌尔本人就在城市上空徘徊，煽动他继承人点燃的火焰。一个向火场跑去的家族卫兵看到忒伊时停了下来，他摇摇晃晃地站在卡寇斯家门前，手里拿着一把带血的刀。 “你做了什么，塞乔？” 忒伊跑向森林，身后的斗篷被狂风卷起。守卫拔出剑来紧追其后。没必要进屋察看到底有没有发生谋杀了。情况已经了然。 忒伊在旷野上飞奔了好几个小时，歌声催逼着他往前。追捕者声音逐渐消失。最后，树木渐渐稀疏，眼前只剩天空和水域。百尺高的悬崖矗立于内海。 歌声阻止他。催促他朝北走，甜蜜地许诺一处地方能让他在朋友中间休憩。不仅仅是朋友——那些人会把他当作达格斯的后裔来尊崇。当他慢慢走向悬崖的边缘时，歌声恐吓之意更甚，警告他不要试图逃避他的命运。死亡避无可避。 忒伊对他的家族吐出一句诅咒，然后一头扎下悬崖。 这是戈尼岛上又一个灿烂的日子，几周以来第一天贝娜拉能真正享受一下。远道而来的家族成员是特里菲斯叔叔的贵宾，她被要求参加每一次晚宴，每一次会议，每一场仪式。她记得在孩提时代，她曾希望受人瞩目。现在却没有什么比远离职责的时光更令人幸福了。 她现在想的只有一件事，不过必须回室内才行，那就是给她的堂弟写封信。但是这可以等到晚上，她告诉自己。毕竟，他已经很多天没有给她写信了。这是那个女孩阿克拉的缘故。并不是说她讨厌这女孩，但贝娜拉知道一个人的初恋会让人多么投入。至少，她在书上读到过。 当她在野芳纷发的草地间闲庭信步时，贝娜拉沉在自己的思绪里，以至于没有听到她的女仆希利玛的呼喊。当她转身看到那个年轻的仆人跑过来时一脸愕然。 “塞乔，”她喘着粗气说。“快来! 有人被冲上了岸!是你的堂弟，塞乔因多瑞尔-忒伊！”

String.Source: <font face="$HandwrittenFont"> Black slimy fingers. Black slimy words. Black slimy book. <alias=Dungeon> Black like the back of my eyes. But darker. Get these things out of my head!
String.Dest: <font face="$HandwrittenFont"> 黑乎乎黏糊糊的手指。黑乎乎黏糊糊的文字。黑乎乎黏糊糊的书。在<alias=Dungeon>。黑得像我的眼窝。但太黑暗了。快把这些东西从我脑袋里拿走！
不能在这里召唤卡斯塔格。
