String.Source:
[pagebreak]

The Five Far Stars

[pagebreak]

his is a volume of verse collected from wise women of the Urshilaku Ashlanders. It consists of verses composed by Ashlander warriors, champions, and ashkhans, committed to memory by the wise women and transmitted down the generations. 'May I shrink to dust' is attributed to the long-dead poet and warrior Zershishi Mus-Manul. Rise from darkness, Red Mountain! Spread your dark clouds and green vapors! Birth earthquakes, shatter stones! Feed the winds with fire! Flay the tents of the tribes from the land! Feed the burned earth with our souls! Yet never shall you have your rule over me. Never shall I tremble or flinch from your power. Never shall I yield my home and hearth. And from my tears shall spring forth The flowers of grassland springs.
String.Dest:
[pagebreak]

遥远五星

[pagebreak]

这是一本从灰地民乌什拉库部落的智妪处收集的诗歌集。是讴歌灰地民战士、勇者及灰可汗们的诗句，由智妪们背诵并代代相传。“我可否缩成尘埃？”就是出自已故的诗人与勇士——泽尔什什·姆斯-玛努尔 从黑暗中崛起吧，红山！ 把乌云与绿气散开！ 震动大地，粉碎岩石！ 以烈火滋润狂风！ 把部落的帐篷从大地上拔起！ 用我们的灵魂哺育燃烧的大地！ 但你永远也无法统治我。 我也绝不会在你的力量面前战栗退缩。 我永远不会放弃我的家园。 草原春日的花朵， 将会从我的泪水中绽放。

String.Source:
The Doors of the Spirit

[pagebreak]

he Ancestors are among us. They are never farther away than the Waiting Door. The Ancestors are not departed. The dead are not under the earth. Their spirits are in the restless wind, in the fire's voice, in the foot-smoothed step. Pay heed to these things, and you will know your absent kin. Pay reverence through gift and prayer. Acquaint the Ancestors with your affairs, with your comings and goings, with your blessings and trials. From the Waiting Door comes your protection. Heed the spirits, who are the guardians of your hearth, teachers of wisdom, counselors of fortune, seers of fate. Each bone is a door through the wall of the world. Each bone is the road, with Wisdom and Power the travelers. Each bone is the ghost fence that guards us from evil. Honor the Ancestors upon your hearths, within your halls, in the community of your temples, in the solitude of your tombs. Guard your Ancestors from beasts, from thieves, from profane priest and sorcerers. Let no creature steal your spirits, for the plundered hearth is diminished, and the plundered tomb is shamed. Live in One World with your spirits. Honor the spirits within and without you. Do not grieve for the dead. Take shelter in their arms, and pay heed to their words.
String.Dest:
精魂之门

[pagebreak]

祖先就在我们之间。他们永远不会比“等待之门”更远。 祖先从未离开。逝者不在地下。他们的精魂存于不息的风中，存于火炎的声中，存于平稳的脚步中。留心这些事物，你就能感知到你逝去的亲族。 用礼品和祈祷向他们表达敬意。要让祖先知晓你的事情，你的来去，你的祝福与试炼。 等待之门会保护你。留心于那些精魂吧，他们是你心灵的守护者、智慧的导师、幸运的顾问、命运的预见者。 每一根骨都是穿越世界之墙的门。每一根骨都是引领智勇兼备的旅者的路。每一根骨都是保护我们免遭邪恶侵害的幽魂之栅。 在你们的壁炉前，在你们的大厅中，在你们的神庙区中，在你们孤寂的坟墓中，都要供奉祖先。 要保护你们的祖先免受野兽、盗贼、渎神祭司和术士的伤害。不要让任何人偷走你的祖先精魂，否则会致使你家宅倒灶不宁，祖墓遭掠蒙羞。 你与祖先精魂生活在同一世界。尊重你内外的一切精魂。不要为逝者哀伤。投向他们的臂弯，留心聆听他们的话。

String.Source:
[pagebreak]

Bone, Part II By Tavi Dromio

[pagebreak] "What do you mean the story gets more appalling?" Garaz was incredulous. "How in Boethiah's name could it get more appalling?" "It's a ruse," Xiomara scoffed, ordering two more mugs of greef and a glass of flin for Garaz. "How much worse can a tale get which prominently features cannibalism, abuse of slaves, and the regular placement of rotting animal carcasses?" "Don't you dare dare me," growled Hallgerd, annoyed by his listeners' lack of appreciation of his prose styling. "Remind me where we were?" "Arslic Oan is the owner of a stronghold under siege by savage, cannibalistic Nords," said Xiomara, keeping a straight face. "After a lot of deaths and several unsuccessful attempts to get water, he had his armorer with the unlikely name of Gorkith outfit his slaves with the first ever bonemold armor. One of them finally makes it back with some water." It was only one jarful of water (said Hallgerd, pulling back in his chair and continuing the tale), and Arslic Oan drank most of it, passing the remains to his dear armorer Gorkith and the last dribbles to the few dozen slaves who still lived. It was hardly enough to sustain health and well-being. Another expedition was necessary, but they had only one suit of bonemold left, as there was only one survivor of the trip. "One out of eighteen slaves made it through the gauntlet of Nords wearing that marvelous bonemold armor of yours," said Arslic Oan to Gorkith. "And one can only carry back enough water for one. Therefore, mathematically, as we have, counting you and me, fifty-six remaining people at the stronghold, we need armor for fifty-four. Since we already have one, you only need to make fifty-three to make the total. That way, three will make it back, with enough water for you and me and whoever's in the best condition to partake. I don't know what we'll do after that, but if we wait, we won't have enough slaves to fetch even a couple days' worth of water." "I understand," whimpered Gorkith. "But how am I going to make the armor? I used all the livestock bones to make the first batch of bonemold." Arslic Oan gave an order which Gorkith fearfully complied with. In eighteen hours - "What do you mean 'Arslic Oan gave an order which Gorkith fearfully complied with'?" asked Xiomara. "What was the order?" "All will be clear," smiled Hallgerd. "I have to choose what to reveal and what to conceal. Such is the way of the tale teller." In eighteen hours, Gorkith had fifty-three suits of bonemail (said Hallgerd, continuing, not really minding the interruption) prepared for the slaves. Without prompting, he ordered the slaves to practice using the armor, and even allowed them more training time than their predecessors. They not only learned how to move and stop quickly in bonemold, but how to adjust their peripheral vision to see a blow before it came, and to sway to dodge, and where the sturdiest reinforcement points on the arm were -- the center of the chest and the abdomen -- and how to position themselves to take blows there, against their natural instincts. The slaves even had time for a mock battle before being sent out among the cannibals. The slaves handled themselves admirably. Very few, just fifteen slaves, were killed and eaten out right. Only ten were killed and eaten when they reached the river. That was when things did not go according to Arslic Oan's plans. Twenty-one slaves with jars of water took off for the hills. Only eight returned to the castle, largely because they were blocked by the cannibal Nords. It was a larger percentage than he had anticipated surviving, but Arslic Oan felt righteous indignation at the paucity of loyalty. "Are you absolutely certain you wouldn't rather flee?" he hollered from the battlements. Finally, he allowed the survivors in. Three had been killed waiting for the gate to open. Two more died almost upon stepping into the courtyard. One was delirious, walking around in circles, laughing and dancing before suddenly collapsing. That meant five jars of water for four people, the two surviving slaves, Arslic Oan, and Gorkith. As the lord of the manor, Arslic Oan took the extra jar, but he was democratic with the others. "You're quite correct," frowned Garaz. "This story is getting more and more appalling." "Just wait," smiled Hallgerd. The next morning (Hallgerd continued) Arslic Oan awoke to a perfectly still and quiet stronghold. There was no murmuring in the corridors, no sound of hard labor in the courtyard. He dressed and surveyed the scene. It appeared that the fortress was utterly deserted. Arslic Oan walked down to the armorer's quarters, but the door was locked. "Open up," said Arslic Oan, patiently. "We need to speak. Thirty out of fifty-four slaves successfully made it to the river and gathered water. Admittedly, some then fled, and a couple didn't survive because I needed to correct their fickleness, but mathematically, that's a fifty-five percent survival rate. If you and I and the two remaining slaves made the next run to the river, we two should survive." "Zilian and Gelo left last night with their armor," cried Gorklith through the door. "Who are Zilian and Gelo?" "The two remaining slaves! They don't remain anymore!" "Well, that's vexing," said Arslic Oan. "Still we must continue on. Mathematically--" "I heard something last night," whimpered Gorklith in a funny voice. "Like footsteps, only different, and they were moving through the walls. And there were voices too. They sounded strange, like they couldn't move their jaws very well, but I knew one." Arslic Oan sighed, humoring his poor armorer: "And who was it?" "Ponik." "And who is Ponik?" "One of the slaves that died when the Nords poisoned our water. One of the many, many slaves that died, and we made use of. He was always a nice, uncomplaining fellow, that's why I noticed his voice above all the others," Gorklith began to sob. "I understood what he was saying." "Which was what?" asked Arslic Oan with a sigh. "'Give me back my bones!'" Gorklith's voice shrieked. There was silence for a moment, and then more hysterical sobbing. "I saw that coming," laughed Xiomara. There was nothing more to be done with the armorer for the time being (said Hallgerd, a trifle annoyed at the regular interruptions), so Arslic Oan stripped one of the dead slaves of his suit of bonemold and put it on. He practiced in the courtyard, impressing himself with his natural comfortably with medium weight armor. For hours, he boxed, feinted, dodged, sprinted, skipped, jumped, and generally cavorted about. When he felt tired, he retired to the shade and took a nap. The sound of the king's trumpet woke him with a start. Night had fallen, and for a moment, he thought he had been dreaming. Then the alarum sounded again, far in the distance, but clear. Arslic Oan leapt to his feet and ran to the ramparts. Several miles away, he could see the emissaries and their vast and well-armed escort approach. They were there early! The cannibal Nords below looked at one another with consternation. Savages they might be, but they knew when a superior force was approaching. Arslic Oan joyously dashed down the stairs to Gorklith's chamber. The door was still locked. He beat on it, cajoling, demanding, threatening. Finally, he found a key, one of the few scraps of metal that had not been smelted days before. Gorklith appeared to be sleeping, but as Arslic Oan approached, he noticed that the armorer's mouth and eyes were wide open and his arms were folded unnaturally behind his back. On closer inspection, the armorer was obviously dead. What was more, his face and whole body were sunken, like an empty pig's bladder. Something moved through the walls, like a footfall only... squishy. Arslic Oan expertly and gracefully turned to face it, completely in balance. At first, it seemed like nothing more than a bubble expanding through one of the cracks in the stone. As more of the flesh-colored gelatinous matter emerged, it more clearly resembled part of a face. A flaccid, almost shapeless face with a low brow and a slack, toothless jaw. The rest of the body oozed out of the crack, a soft bag of muscle and blood. Behind Arslic Oan and to the side, there was more movement, more slaves welling up through the cracks in the stone. They were all around him, reaching out. "Give us," moaned Ponik, his tongue rolling about his hanging jaw. "Give us back our bones." Arslic Oan began to rip off his bonemold, throwing it to the floor. A hundred figures, more, pooled into the small chamber. "That's not enough." The cannibals had cleared away by the time the king's emissaries arrived at Arslic Oan's gates. They had not been looking forward to this visit. It was best, they thought philosophically, to begin with the worst of the king's noblemen, so to end their trip well. They sounded the alarum once again, but the gates did not open. There was no sound from Arslic Oan's stronghold. It took a few hours to gain access. If the emissaries had not brought a professional acrobat with them for entertainment, it might have taken longer. The place seemed to be abandoned. They searched every room, until finally they came to the armorer's. There they found the master of the manor, folded neatly, legs behind his head, arms behind the legs, like a fine gown. Not a bone in his body. "The first part of your story was complete nonsense," cried Xiomara. "But now it doesn't hold true on any level. How could bonemold be made again if the armorer who invented it died before he could tell anyone how he did it?" "I said that this was the first time it was created, not the first time people learned the craft." "And when did someone first teach someone else the craft?" asked Garaz. "That, my friends," replied Hallgerd with a sinister smile. "Is a tale for another night."
String.Dest:
[pagebreak]

骨 卷二 塔维·德罗米奥 著

[pagebreak] “你这么说可怕的还在后头？”加拉兹难以置信，“波耶西亚在上，还有更可怕的？” “听他胡扯吧，”肖玛拉嘲笑道，又给加拉兹点了两杯格里夫和一杯飞令，“一个包含了食人、虐待奴隶、放置腐烂动物的故事，还能糟糕到哪儿去？” “别招我啊，”哈尔杰德怒道，他对听众不喜欢自己的故事风格感到恼火，“我们刚才讲到哪儿了？” “要塞主人阿斯里克·奥安被野蛮的诺德食人族包围了，”肖玛拉板着脸说道，“几次取水不成，死了好几拨人，他让护甲师高尔基斯，这名字肯定是假的，给他的奴隶们穿上了有史以来第一批骨模护甲。终于有个人带着水回来了。” 只有一罐水（哈尔杰德说着把椅子往后稍了稍，继续开讲），阿斯里克·奥安自己喝了大半罐，把剩下的分给了他最爱的护甲师高尔基斯，最后几滴分给了剩下几十个奴隶。这点水根本不足以维持健康和情绪。再出去冒一次险是必要的，但他们只剩下最后一套骨模护甲，因为这趟出去就活着回来了一个。 “每十八个奴隶，就有一个能穿着你那个神奇的骨模护甲，从诺德人手里逃回来。”阿斯里克·奥安对高尔基斯说，：“每个人只能带回一人份的水。所以，从数学上来讲，算上你我，要塞还剩五十六人，我们还需要五十四套护甲。既然我们已经有一套了，你只需要再加造五十三套就行了。这样就能有三个人回来，带着足够的水给你和我还有其他状态足够好的人喝。我不知道以后该怎么办，但如果再等下去，我们的奴隶连两天的水都打不回来。” “我明白，”高尔基斯呜咽道，“但我们没有原材料了。第一批骨模已经把所有牲畜的骨头都用光了。” 阿斯里克·奥安下达了一道命令，高尔基斯胆怯地服从。十八个小时后—— “你说，阿斯里克·奥安下达了一道命令，高尔基斯胆怯地服从。是什么意思？”肖玛拉问道，“到底是什么命令。” “一切都会揭晓，”哈尔杰德微笑，“我必须选择好什么该说，什么不该说。这才叫讲故事。” 十八个小时，高尔基斯为奴隶们准备了五十三套骨模护甲（哈尔杰德继续说，没有在意打断他的话）。在没有任何提醒的情况下，他开始让奴隶们练习使用护甲，甚至给了他们更多的训练时间。他们不仅学会了如何穿着骨模护甲快速移动，还学会了如何调整视角，在攻击到来前加以察觉，并如何摇晃躲避，还有手臂最坚固的地方在哪里，如何用胸腹的中心迎接攻击，这显然违背人的本能。奴隶们在被送到食人族中间之前，甚至还有时间进行一场模拟战斗。 奴隶们很让人钦佩。死的人不多，只有十五个被诺德人杀了吃掉。到了河边又折损了十人。事情没有按照阿斯里克·奥安的计划进行。有二十一个奴隶拿着水罐向山里跑去。只有八个人回到了城堡，但也只是因为他们被诺德食人族挡了回来。这个比例已经超出他的预期了，但阿斯里克·奥安还是对他们的不忠感到义愤。 “你们是真的不愿意逃走吗？”他站在城垛上高喊。 最后，他放幸存者进来了。又有三个人在等待开门的时候被杀。还有俩人一进院子就死了。一个人神志不清，在周围来回转圈，又笑又跳，然后突然倒下。这就意味着还剩四个人，两个幸存奴隶，阿斯里克·奥安和高尔基斯，他们要分五罐水。作为庄园主，阿斯里克·奥安多拿了一罐，但他对其他人倒很公平。 “你说得对，”加拉兹皱起眉头，“这故事已经越来越骇人听闻了。” “再等等。”哈尔杰德笑道。 第二天早上（哈尔杰德继续），阿斯里克·奥安醒来，发现要塞静的出奇。走廊里没有窃窃私语，院子里没有工作声响。他穿好衣服，打量着。看来要塞被完全遗弃了。阿斯里克·奥安来到护甲工的住处，但门是锁着的。 “开门，”阿斯里克·奥安耐心地说，“我们得商量商量。五十四个奴隶有三十个成功打到水了。不可否认，有些人逃走了，还有一些人没能活下来，因为我需要纠正他们的反复无常，但从数学上说，存活率有百分之五十五。如果你和我，还有剩下两个奴隶跑去河边，咱们俩就能活下来。” “齐连和基罗昨晚带着护甲跑了。”高尔基斯隔着门喊道。 “齐连和基罗是谁？” “剩下两个奴隶！他们不会留下来了！” “嗯，确实很烦人，”阿斯里克·奥安说，“但我们必须继续。数学——” “昨晚我听到了什么。”高尔基斯用一种滑稽的声音呜咽着说。“就像脚步声，只是不同，它们是穿过墙壁移动的。还有声音。听起来很奇怪，好像它们的下巴不太灵光，但我听出其中一个。” 阿斯里克·奥安叹气，迁就着自己那可怜的护甲师：“那又是谁？” “波尼克。” “波尼克是谁？” “喝了诺德人毒水死掉的奴隶里的一个。我们用的很多很多死掉的奴隶之一。他挺和蔼的，从来不抱怨，所以我注意到他的声音比其他人都响亮。我知道他在说什么。” 阿斯里克·奥安又叹了口气：“那他说什么了。” “还我骨来！”高尔基斯尖叫着。一阵沉默之后，随即就是更加歇斯底里地抽泣。 “我早就料到了。”肖玛拉乐了。 眼下算是指望不上那个护甲师了（哈尔杰德继续说，对于经常被打断感到有些恼火），于是，阿斯里克·奥安把一个死去奴隶身上的骨模甲扒下来，穿上了。他在院子里练习，中甲的重量确实令人感觉自然舒适。一连几个小时，他拳打脚踢、闪转腾挪，来回跳跃。当他感到疲劳，就退到阴凉处小憩一会儿。 国王的号角声把他惊醒。夜幕降临了，他还以为自己是在做梦。但警报声又一次响起，虽然很远，但也很清晰。阿斯里克·奥安一跃而起，向着城墙跑去。数里外，他看到使者和大批装备精良的护卫正在逼近。他们来得够早！下面的诺德食人族惊恐地面面相觑。他们虽然野蛮，却也知道力量对比。 阿斯里克·奥安兴高采烈冲下楼梯，来到高尔基斯房间。门还是锁着。他拍着门，哄骗、命令、威胁。最后，他找来钥匙，这是这短短几天里，为数不多没被熔化的金属之一了。 高尔基斯似乎是睡着了，但当阿斯里克·奥安走近却发现，护甲师的嘴巴眼睛张得老大，双臂不自然地交叉在背后。仔细一看，已经是死透了。而且，脸和整个身体都塌陷了，像个空掉的猪尿泡。 墙壁里有什么东西在东，好像只有脚步声……听起来黏糊糊的。阿斯里克·奥安熟练而优雅地转身面对它，完美地保持着平衡。 起初，它看起来只不过是一个气泡在石头缝中膨胀起来。随着更多肉色胶质物出现，显然像是脸的一部分。一张松弛到几乎没有形状的脸，眉毛低垂，下颌没有牙齿。身体的其他部分也从裂缝中渗出来，像一袋柔软的肌肉和血液。在他身周，动静越来越多，更多奴隶从石头的裂缝里涌出来，围着他伸出手来。 “还来，”波尼克呻吟着，舌头在他垂下的下巴上滚动，“还我们骨来。” 阿斯里克·奥安扯下他的骨模护甲，扔到地板上。一百多，乃至更多人涌进了小房间。 “不够。” 当国王的使者来到阿斯里克·奥安的城门时，食人族已经作鸟兽散。他们并不期待此次访问。他们从哲学的角度出发，觉得最好从国王最坏的贵族当第一站，这趟形成才能圆满结束。他们再次拉响警报，大门却没开。阿斯里克·奥安的要塞里寂静无声。 花了几个小时，他们才成功进去。还好使者们带了个专业的杂技演员以供娱乐，不然时间会拖得更久。这地方似乎是被遗弃了。他们搜遍了房间，最后来到了护甲师房间。 他们在那里找到了庄园主，被折叠得整整齐齐，腿在头后，胳膊在腿后，像一件漂亮的长袍，身上一根骨头都没有了。 “你这故事第一部分顶多叫胡说八道。”肖玛拉嚷道，“但现在已经是离了大谱了。如果发明骨模的工匠还没来得及告诉别人该怎么做就死掉了，怎么可能接着制造骨模呢？” “我说的是第一次被创造出来，而不是人们第一次学这门手艺。” “那什么时候有人第一次教别人这门手艺呢？”加拉兹追问道。 “好了，哥儿几个。”哈尔杰德带着阴险的微笑答道，“这就是另一个晚上的故事了。”

String.Source: Amethyst Gem Damaged Albino Spider Pod
String.Dest: 紫水晶 受损的白化蜘蛛卵囊

String.Source: Flawless Sapphire Gem Salt Pile Albino Spider Pod
String.Dest: 无瑕蓝宝石 盐堆 白化蜘蛛卵囊

String.Source: I agreed to fund Ralis Sedarys' expedition to excavate Kolbjorn Barrow, but work on the dig has come to a halt. The excavation had reached a dead end when Draugr within the tomb awoke, killing several miners and causing the rest to flee. I found a way past the dead end, but must deal with the Draugr before work can continue.
String.Dest: 我同意了资助拉里斯·瑟达瑞斯对寇泊琼古坟的发掘，但挖掘工程停滞了。当挖到了一处死胡同时，墓中的一头尸鬼苏醒了，杀死了几个矿工，其他人都跑了。我找到了一条绕开死胡同的路，但要复工还得先处理那头尸鬼。

String.Source:
<font face='$HandwrittenFont'> <font size='17'>She's finally done it. I knew she'd eventually crack. I probably should have left when I had the chance. The untapped power within these spiders has finally gone to Merilar's head. Who would have thought these tiny albino creatures had the ability to harness such magic?
Being locked in this cage is frustrating, of course, but it is keeping me even more focused on my work. What did she think I was going to do, anyway? As my sister, she must have known how devoted I am to this work. Although, I can't fault her for her actions right now. Who knows what kind of fumes these experiments have been giving off, or what effect they have on the human brain. At least I'm all right. Or maybe I'm not but I think I am? Could these experiments be having the same effect on me that they are on her? [pagebreak] Magnificent! It seems as though you can combine any one of the base spiders with a modifier to tweak its behavior. For instance, just imbuing an Albino Pod with a Ruby seems to create a spider that jumps at its victims and proceeds to explode. But, by simply adding a Salt Pile to the mix it creates the same manner of spider, but instead of jumping and exploding it emits flames from its body. I'd experiment with more of these behaviors, but it seems the bandits we tested the mind control spiders on are all still locked away, Merilar doesn't want me to let them out. Maybe there are too many in there for her to handle?
I heard her muttering to herself earlier today. She was saying things like, "The spiders are mine. They'll listen to me!" What exactly is she planning? I hope she's not attempting to enter the blocked off room in the main chamber. She knows we specifically sealed it after hearing odd chanting coming from that direction. Then again, what she used to "know" may not matter right now, considering the state she's been in. I hope she'll be all right.
String.Dest:
<font face='$HandwrittenFont'> <font size='17'>她还是走到这一步了。我知道她最后会崩溃的。也许我本该找机会离开。这些蜘蛛体内未开发的力量最终进入了梅莉拉的脑海。谁能想到区区白化蜘蛛竟有如此魔力？
被关在这笼子里确实令人懊丧，但也能更加专注于工作了。她会以为我要做什么呢？身为妹妹，她一定明白我对这份工作有多投入。不过我也不能责怪她现在的行为。谁知道这些实验释放了什么气体，又会对人脑有什么影响。至少我没事。也许已经有事了，但我自己还没觉得？这些实验对我的效果和对她一样吗？ [pagebreak] 太棒了！看来可以将任意的基底蜘蛛进行更改，以调整其行动模式。比如，在白化卵囊中植入红宝石似乎能创造一种跳向目标并爆炸的蜘蛛。通过简单地在混合中加入盐堆，则创建了相同形态却不会跳跃爆炸的蜘蛛，反而从身体中发射出火焰。我还想尝试更多办法，但我们用来测试精神控制蜘蛛的强盗都还被锁着，梅莉拉不想让我把他们放出来。也许是里面东西太多她处理不了？
今天早些时候，我听见她自言自语。她说：“蜘蛛是我的。它们会听我的！”她到底在计划什么？希望她没有打算进入主室的封闭房间。她明白，我们在听到那个方向传来奇怪吟唱后特意把它封了起来。话又说回来，考虑到她现在的状态，她过去“明白”的东西现在可能并不重要了。希望她没事。

String.Source: Amethyst Gem Albino Spider Pod
String.Dest: 紫水晶 白化蜘蛛卵囊

String.Source:
<font face='$HandwrittenFont'> <font size='17'>I know what he's planning. Does he really think he can take all the credit for discovering these spiders? Next time he goes into the safety cage to do whatever it is he does with those spiders, I'll lock the door. He'll have no choice but to listen to me then.
What does he mean about me not being right in the head? There's nothing wrong with me. He's the one trying to steal my discovery! Does he think I don't see what he's doing? There's nothing wrong with me. Nothing! [pagebreak] He keeps talking to me like I'm insane. I'm not insane! Who said I'm insane? Did I say I was insane? I'll show him insane!
It doesn't matter. He'll see what real power is. The chanting we heard just outside the main chamber must mean there's something extremely powerful there. If I can get my hands on that energy and bring it back, who knows what kind of discoveries we can make from it.
String.Dest:
<font face='$HandwrittenFont'> <font size='17'>我知道他在盘算什么。他真以为发现这些蜘蛛是自己的功劳了？下次他再进安全笼对付蜘蛛，我就把门锁上。那样他就别无选择，只能乖乖听我的了。
他说我脑子不正常？我能有什么事儿。明明是他想偷我的发现！他以为我不知道他在干什么？我什么事儿都没有。没有！ [pagebreak] 他一直跟我说话，好像我疯了一样。我没疯！谁说我疯了？我说过我疯了吗？我现在想让他疯！
没关系。他会明白什么叫真正的力量。我们在主厅外听到的吟唱一定意味着什么，那里有非常强大的东西。我要得到这种能量并带回来，谁知道我们能发现什么呢。

String.Source: I agreed to continue funding Ralis Sedarys' excavation of Kolbjorn Barrow. I should return and see how the work is going.
String.Dest: 我同意了资助拉里斯·瑟达瑞斯对寇泊琼古坟的发掘，该回去看看工程怎么样了。

String.Source: Captain Veleth told me that Adril Arano, Second Councilor of Raven Rock, wanted to speak to me immediately. I should find him and discover the reason for his urgent message.
String.Dest: 维黎斯队长告诉我，鸦石镇副议员阿德尔·阿拉诺想尽快和我谈话。我该去找他问问急着找我的原因。

String.Source:
[pagebreak]

Trap by Anonymous

[pagebreak]

saw the gold, and I took it. A different man might not have, I know that, and from time to time, I think back on the hour when I saw the gold and took it. You see, I was hungry. Isn't it ironic. I don't remember much else about that night but the gold and the hunger. I don't remember the name of the tavern, or even the village, but I believe it was somewhere in southern Vvardenfell. I can't really be certain. For some time, I sat dumbly in my chair, my mind occupied with nothing but the pain in my stomach. If you've never been truly hungry from days of no food, you can't know what it's like. You can't concentrate on anything. It wasn't until a figure to my left got up from the table to get a drink and left a stack of gold marks behind that I snapped to awareness. From this moment on, my memory is crystalline. My eyes to the gold. My eyes to the stranger's back, walking calmly toward the barmaid. My hand to the gold. The gold in my pocket. I'm up from the table, and out the door. For just a moment, I look back. The stranger has turned to look my way. He wears a hood, but I can feel his eyes meet mine. I swear, I can scent a smile. Out into the street, and behind some barrels I crouched down, waiting for my pursuer. One benefit of a lifetime running from guards, I know how to disappear. For nearly an hour, I waited there, suffering even more from hunger. You see, I was awake now and I had the means to buy myself a feast. This knowledge tortured me. When I finally got to my feet, I very nearly fainted. I had only enough energy to walk to the other edge of the village to a run-down tavern before collapsing at a table. I think I must have fallen unconscious for a moment before I heard the barmaid's voice. "Can I get you something to eat, sera?" I gorged myself on roasts and pies and huge frothing mugs of greef. As the fog of near fatal starvation began to lift, I looked up from my plate to see a gold-masked stranger looking at me, his vizard glowing by the blinding light of the moon through the window. He wore black leather armor and was a different physique and size from the man I had burgled, but I could tell he knew. I paid for my meal quickly and left. I skirted the edge of the village, through a tiled central courtyard surrounded by the squalid peasant's cottages. There was not a light shining from any window or door. No one was on the streets. I could find no place to hide, so I took the road out of town, heading for the wilderness. Hunger had pushed me on in the days before, but now I felt what I imagined to be the whip of guilt. Or perhaps, even then, it was fear. I fell twice, rushing down the dark path, unused to the slopes and pebbled texture. The sounds of animal life, which I had numbed to, were suddenly very loud in my ears. And there was something else out there in the night, something chasing me. On the side of the road, there was a low wall, and I scrambled over it and hid. I knew enough about concealment to pick a spot where the bulwark sunk slightly so even if someone saw the outline of my figure, he would assume it to be part of the wall. It wasn't long before I heard the sound of running footsteps from more than one person pass me by and then stop. There was a moment of whispered conversation, and one of the people ran back on the path toward the village. Then, silence. After a few more minutes, I peered out from behind the wall. A female figure in a dun gown, wimple, and veil stood in the road. On the other end of the road, blocking the way back to town, was a knight, coated in dark mail. I could see neither of their faces. For a moment, I froze, unsure whether either or both had seen me. "Run," said the woman in a dead voice. The hill behind me was too steep, so I leapt over the wall and across the road in two bounds. Into the night forest I ran, the maddening jingle of the accursed gold in my pocket. I knew I was making so much noise my pursuers could not help but hear me, but now I cared more for putting distance between us than in stealth. Clouds of ash filtered through the moonlight, but I still knew it was too bright to hide. I ran and ran until I felt all my blood pumping in my head and heart, begging me to stop. I was at the edge of the wood, on the other side of a shallow stream from a vast, crumbling house encircled by a rail fence. Behind me, running footfall in the broken, dusty earth. To the south, downstream, a distinct sodden splashing of someone moving nearer. There was no choice. I half jumped and half fell into the mud and dragged myself up the bank on the other side. I rolled under the fence and ran through the open field toward the house. Jerking my head around, I saw seven shadowy figures by the fence posts. The cloaked man I had robbed. The man in the gold mask. The veiled woman. The dark knight. Three others too who had pursued me, but I had never seen. And I thought I was the stealthy one. The moon was entirely hidden in a swarm of ash. Only the stars offered their meager illumination as I reached the open door of the ruin. I slammed and bolted the door behind me, but I knew there could be no protection for very long. As I looked about the ravaged interior of broken furniture, I searched for someone to hide. A corner, a niche where if I stayed very still, no one would see me. A splintered table lying against the wall looked perfect for my purposes. I crawled under it, and jumped when something moved and I heard a frightened old man's voice. "Who's there?" "It's all right," I whispered. "I'm not one of them." His puckered, gnarled hand reached out from the shadow and gripped my arm. Instantly, I felt sleep fall upon me, resist it as I might. The old man's horrible face, the face of the hungry dead, emerged as the moon came out and shone through the broken window. His talon still gripping me, I fell back, smelling his death surround me. The table was thrown back. There stood the seven hunters and a dozen more. No, hunters they weren't. They were harriers who had chased me out of every hiding place, expertly pushing me to the lair of the real predator. He was weak with age, the old man was, not as good at the chase as once he was. A blunt, killing machine. "Please," I said. It was all I could muster. Having enjoyed the sport I offered, he granted me mercy, of sorts. I was not bled dry. I was not cursed by being made one of them, the Berne. I was kept with others, most of us mad with fear, to be aged and tasted at the vampires' whim. We are called cattle. I lost all hope months ago of ever leaving the dank cellar where they keep us. Even if this note finds its way to the outside world, I cannot give enough information about my whereabouts to be rescued, even if some champion were able to defeat the bloodsuckers. I only write this to keep my own sanity, and to warn others. There is something worse than being hungry. Being food.
String.Dest:
[pagebreak]

圈套 佚名 著

[pagebreak]

我看到了金币，就拿走了。我知道，换二一个人可能不会，但我时不时会想起我看到金币然后把它拿走的那一刻。你知道么，我当时很饿。是不是很讽刺。 那天晚上的一切，除了饥饿和金币我已经什么都不记得了。我不记得酒馆的名字，甚至是那个村子，但我想应该是在南瓦登费尔的某个地方吧。我实在是不能确定。那时候，我呆呆地坐在椅子上，脑子里只有胃疼的感觉。如果你从来没有真正饿过几天肚子，你是不可能与我感同身受的。饥饿会使你无法专心于任何事。直到我左手边的人拿着酒站起来，留下一叠金币就离开，我才有了意识。 从那一刻起，我的记忆清晰了。 我的眼直勾勾看着金币。我的眼直勾勾盯着谋生人的背，看着他平静地走向酒馆女侍。我的手伸向金币。把金币放进我的口袋，离开位子，走出门。我回头看了一会儿，那个陌生人也转头看我。他带着兜帽，但我能感觉到他与我目光相遇。我发誓，他在笑。 我跑到街上，蹲在几个木桶后面，等着追捕我的人离开。一辈子都在躲避卫兵的好处就是，我知道该如何消失。我在那里等了将近一个小时，饿的心发慌。你看，我现在清醒了，我有钱给自己买一顿大餐了。这些想法折磨着我。当我终于站起来的时候，差点晕了过去。我用仅存的力气走到村子的另一边，进了一家破旧的小酒馆，然后瘫倒在一张桌子旁。我一定是昏过去了，过了一会儿才听到酒馆女侍的声音。 “需要我给你拿点吃的吗，塞拉？” 我狼吞虎咽吃着烤肉、馅饼和一大杯满是泡沫的格里夫。几乎致命的饥饿迷雾开始消散，我从盘子里抬起头来，看到一个金色面具的陌生人在看着我，他的面具在窗外刺眼的月光下闪闪发光。他穿着黑色的皮甲，体格与我偷的那人不同，但我看得出，他知道我的所作所为。我很快就付了钱离开了。 我绕过村子的边缘，穿过一个铺的齐整的中央庭院，四周都是肮脏的农户小屋。没有一扇窗户或门透出亮光。街上也没有人。我找不到藏身之处，于是选择了出村，向荒野进发。过去都是饥饿感推动我继续前进，但现在我感受到一种我想象中的负罪感在鞭打我。或者，那就是恐惧。我跌到了两次，冲下黑暗的小径，踏上难行的斜坡与卵石。当我几近麻木的时候，动物的声音在我耳边变得响亮。黑夜里还有别的东西在追我。 路边有一堵矮墙，我爬过去藏了起来。我知道如何隐藏，所以选择了一个墙体微微下沉的位置，这样即便有人看到我的轮廓，也会觉得那是墙的一部分。没过多久，我听到不止一个人奔跑的脚步声从我身边经过，然后停下来。他们低声交谈了一会儿，其中一个人跑回了通往村庄的小路上。之后，一片寂静。 几分钟后，我从墙后向外窥视。路上站着一个身着黄褐色长袍，戴头巾面纱的女人。路的另一端，一个身穿暗色链甲的骑士挡住了进村的路。我看不清他俩的脸。有那么一会儿，我僵住了，不确定他们是否有人注意到我。 “跑。”那女人用死气沉沉的声音说道。 我身后的山太陡了，所以我跳过墙，分两步越过小路。我跑进黑夜的森林，口袋里那该死的金币令人发狂地叮当作响。我知道我发出的声音太大了，追我的人一定会听到，但现在我更关心的是尽快拉开我们之间的距离，而不是隐身。灰烬云雾飘过月光，但我知道还是太亮了，无法隐藏。我跑啊跑，直到我的血液在我的头和心脏里跳动，求我停下来。 我站在树林边缘，旁边是一条浅水小溪，对岸是一座被栏杆围起来的破败大屋。在我身后，尘土飞扬的土地上传来奔跑之声。南面的下游，有人溅起水花。 我别无选择，半跳半陷地到了泥里，拖着身子爬到河对岸。我从栏杆下滚过，穿过开阔的田野向房子跑去。我扭过头，看见栏杆柱旁有七个人影。我抢劫的那个斗篷人、金面具人、面纱女、黑暗骑士。还有三个人在追我，但我没看见。我还以为我才是那个鬼鬼祟祟的人。 月亮完全隐藏在一团灰烬之中。当我走到破屋敞开的门口时，只有星光提供了微弱的照明。我砰的一声关上身后的门，但我知道不可能坚持太久。我看着破败的屋内，想找个可以藏人的地方。或是角落，或是壁龛，只要我保持不动，就没人能看到我。 靠墙放着一张破桌子，对我来说再合适不过。我爬到下面，有什么东西动力一下，我跳起来，听到一个吓坏的老人声音。 “是谁？” “别害怕，”我低声说，“我不是他们的人。” 他那皱巴巴的粗糙手掌从阴影中伸来，抓住了我的胳膊。尽管竭力抗拒，但我还是立刻感到了睡意。当月光从破窗里照进来，老人那张饥饿如同死人般的可怕脸浮现出来。他的魔爪抓着我，我向后倒去，嗅到他那死亡气息包围了我。 桌子被放了回去。那里站着七个猎人和另外十几个人。不，他们不是猎人。他们是把我从每个藏身之处赶出来的猎犬，熟练地把我推到了真正的捕食者的巢穴。那老人因年长变得虚弱，不再像以前那样打猎了。但仍是一个迟钝的，杀人机器。 “求你。”我说道。这是我唯一能想到的了。 他很希望我提供的这次运动，所以给了我一些怜悯。我没有被直接榨干。也没有被诅咒成伯尔尼氏族的一员。我和其他人被关在一起，大多数都被恐惧逼疯，等待着变老，品尝那吸血鬼的心血来潮。我们被称为“牛”。 几个月前我就放弃了离开这个阴湿地窖。即便这张纸条能传到外面的世界，我也提供不了足够的信息来救我，哪怕真有勇士能打败那吸血鬼。我写这些只是为了保持自己的理智，并警告其他人。 有比挨饿更糟糕的事。 那就是变成食物。

String.Source:
Mora's Boon: Fully restores your Health, Magicka, and Stamina.
You may only accept one ability from this book at a time.
String.Dest:
莫拉的恩典：完全恢复你的生命、魔力、耐力。
你每次只能接受书中的一种能力。

String.Source: I've killed Vendil Ulen and stopped the threat against Councilor Morvayn. Vendil had hired assassins from Morrowind known as the Morag Tong to help him with his coup, but fortunately I was able to defeat them in the depths of Ashfallow Citadel. I should return to Adril Arano with the good news.
String.Dest: 我杀了文德尔·乌伦，解决了莫尔凡议员的生命威胁。文德尔从晨风的莫拉格堂招募了一批刺客来帮他发动政变，但不幸的是被我在灰落堡垒深处全部击败。我该带着这个好消息回去找阿德尔·阿拉诺。

String.Source: Adril Arano has requested my help with what he believes is an imminent threat on Councilor Morvayn's life. He believes members of the Ulen family are present on the island and seek revenge upon Morvayn for executing their ancestor. He's requested that I investigate the identity of any possible assassins, hoping to catch them unaware. For now, I've refused his request, but I should return to Adril when I'm ready to assist him.
String.Dest: 阿德尔·阿拉诺请我帮忙解决莫尔凡议员遭受的生命威胁。他认为乌伦家的某些成员就在岛上，是为报莫尔凡议员处决其先祖之仇而来。他请我调查刺客的身份，想要不走漏风声地抓捕他们。我暂时拒绝了他的请求，等我准备好帮他时会回来找他的。

String.Source: Bone Meal Albino Spider Pod
String.Dest: 骨粉 白化蜘蛛卵囊

String.Source: Your Thu'um enchants your nearby allies' weapons, allowing them to attack faster.
String.Dest: 你的吐目能强化附近盟友的武器，让他们得以快速攻击。

String.Source:
bjorn
do not come to this place
i have not much self left
the earth will take us both
- h
String.Dest:
布裘
别来这里
我已经不剩什么自我了
大地会带走我俩
-贺

String.Source: I've been attacked by a group of people claiming to work for someone named Miraak. I need to find out why they're trying to kill me, and so I should look for the boat in Windhelm they used to travel from Solstheim.
String.Dest: 我被一群自称听命于米拉克的人袭击了。我需要查出他们为什么试图杀了我，这样就得去风舵城找那条把他们载到这里来的船。

String.Source: The people of Raven Rock know the name Miraak, saying that he has a temple near the center of the island. I should seek him out and find out why people have attacked me on his behalf.
String.Dest: 鸦石镇的人听过米拉克这个名字，说他在岛中央建了一座神殿。我得去找他，查清楚为什么会有人打他的旗号来袭击我。

String.Source:
Hrodulf, light and strength,
I feel your words are not your own, and I worry dearly for your health.
I beg of you: please depart that blasted isle and return to Solitude at once! My nights have been almost impossible to bear without you; to lose you would be the death of me.
My love, if you do not return soon, I will come for you.
- Bjornolfr
String.Dest:
贺多夫，我的光明与力量：
我觉得你说的话不是自己想说的，我非常担心你的健康。
我求你了，赶快离开那该死的小岛，赶快回独孤城来！！没有你，我无法忍受夜晚。没有你，我就好像死了一样。
亲爱的，如果你不快点回来，那我就去找你。
--布裘诺弗

String.Source: The Skaal have been freed from Miraak's control, and his minion has been destroyed. Now that Storn no longer needs to focus on keeping his people safe, he may be able to tell me more about how to find and defeat Miraak.
String.Dest: 斯卡尔人不再受米拉克的控制了，他的爪牙也被毁灭了。既然斯托恩不再需要专注于保护他的族人，他也许能告诉我更多关于寻找和打败米拉克的办法。

String.Source: All spells cost <15>% less to cast, and magicka regenerates <100>% faster.
String.Dest: 所有法术减耗<15>%，魔力恢复速度提升<100>%。

String.Source:
<font face='$HandwrittenFont'> Delvin, Here's the formula for the Improved Bonemold I mentioned in my last letter. Take whatever gold you make from the sale and make sure our little sister is taken care of.
Ingredients: 4 cups of bone dust 1 cup void salts 1 bottle of netch jelly 1 cup of ground stahlrim chips 1 bucket of fresh water
Add the bone and void salts to the water and allow them to soak for at least a full day. Then add the netch jelly and stahlrim chips to the bucket. Stir over a hot flame until the mixture begins to harden. Pour the mixture into the desired mold and set the mold in the center of your forge. Fire the mixture in the forge for at least a half day. Let the mixture cool and then remove from the mold.
Glover Mallory
String.Dest:
<font face='$HandwrittenFont'> 戴尔文： 这是我上次信里提到的改良骨模装备的配方。把你赚的钱拿去好好照顾咱们的小姑娘。
原料： 骨粉 4杯 虚无盐 1杯 气母胶质 1瓶 魔冰碎屑 1杯 淡水 1桶
把骨粉和虚无盐加到水里，浸泡至少一天。然后把气母胶质和魔冰碎屑加到桶里。大火搅拌，直到混合物开始变硬。再把混合物倒进模具里，并把模具放到锻炉中。把混合物在锻炉里烧制至少半天。等混合物冷却，再脱模。
葛洛文·马洛里

String.Source: I have arrived on Solstheim in search of the "Miraak" mentioned in a note as being behind the attempt on my life. Perhaps someone in the town of Raven Rock will know about him.
String.Dest: 我来到索瑟海姆找字条中提到的，想要取我性命的“米拉克”。也许鸦石镇里有人了解他的情报。

String.Source: In order to free the people of the Skaal, and to thwart Miraak's plans, I have learned a new Word of Power. I must use this new knowledge to disrupt the activity at the Wind Stone, where the Skaal are working against their will.
String.Dest: 为了解放斯卡尔人，挫败米拉克的计划，我学会了一种新的力量之语。我必须用这个新知识来打断风石处的活动，斯卡尔人正被迫在此劳作。

String.Source: Storn, the Skaal shaman, has suggested that my experiences on Solstheim mean I am connected to Miraak. To follow the path Miraak has taken and learn what he knows, I must travel to Saering's Watch and learn the Word of Power written there.
String.Dest: 斯卡尔萨满斯托恩，认为我在索瑟海姆的经历意味着我与米拉克有联系。为了重走米拉克之路来了解他所知的一切，我必须前往瑟琳看台，学会那里铭刻的力量之语。

String.Source: After a strange experience with the Black Book in the Temple of Miraak, Frea says it is important to consult the Skaal Shaman. I should accompany her back to her village.
String.Dest: 在米拉克神殿体验了一番奇异经历后，芙瑞雅说很有必要去请教斯卡尔萨满。我应该陪她回村子里去。

String.Source: I've located Gratian Caerellius's remains and uncovered the truth regarding his untimely death deep inside Bloodskal Barrow.
String.Dest: 我在血颅古坟深处找到了格瑞希安·凯利乌斯的尸骨，揭开了他英年早逝的真相。

String.Source: After locating the remains of Gratian Caerellius, recovering his journal and escaping Bloodskal Barrow, I should bring the evidence of my discovery to Crescius Caerellius.
String.Dest: 在找到格瑞希安·凯利乌斯的尸骨、拿到他的日记然后逃出血颅古坟后，我应该把发现的证物交给克雷修斯·凯利乌斯。

String.Source: I've located the skeletal remains of Gratian Caerellius. In his satchel, I've discovered an old journal. In order to return it to Crescius, I'll have to discover a way to escape Bloodskal Barrow.
String.Dest: 我找到了格瑞希安的尸骨。他的背包里有一本古老的日记。为了把日记还给克雷修斯，我必须找到逃离血颅古坟的办法。

String.Source: I've stumbled across Crescius Caerellius inside of Raven Rock Mine. He's convinced that the remains of his great-grandfather, Gratian Caerellius, are at the bottom of the mine and that the East Empire Company has falsely designated his ancestor's death as an accident. He's tasked me with exploring the mine on his behalf to locate the remains, and perhaps discovering what killed Gratian Caerellius.
String.Dest: 我在鸦石镇矿场里偶然遇见了克雷修斯·凯利乌斯。他确信他曾祖父格瑞希安·凯利乌斯的遗体就在矿井底部，而东帝国公司将他祖先的死伪造成一场意外事故。他请我代他去探索矿井，找到祖先的遗骨，如果可能的话，找出格瑞希安·凯利乌斯的死因。

String.Source:
<font face='$HandwrittenFont'> My dearest daughter Sapphire,
It's with a heavy heart that I write this letter. For years I've thought about how I could tell you this, and each time I imagined it would be face-to-face. I'm ashamed for what I did, and I hope that someday you'll learn to forgive me. Whatever you do, don't blame Delvin for any of this. He didn't know.
Long after I joined the Guild, I was sent to rob a caravan that was stopping at a tiny farming village not far from the border of Skyrim. When I arrived there, I was a day early and decided to sleep for the night in the barn of a pig farm. I awoke the next morning when the most beautiful woman I'd ever laid eyes upon walked in to do her chores. She wasn't even frightened when she saw me... in an instant I think we had both fallen in love. I ended up living with that woman in that tiny little village for a year until she was with child. And then, like a coward... I ran away. I was a thief and I didn't want to end up a farmer. It was a terrible thing to do.
I didn't have the courage to return to that village for almost fifteen years. When I finally decided to visit, it was too late. It had been raided by bandits and burned to the ground. Everyone was either dead or gone. Including you... my only child.
Years later, when you were recruited by Brynjolf I couldn't believe my eyes. You're a spitting image of your mother and I knew it was you in an instant. Only the gods know how you survived the attack on that village and why you followed in my footsteps, but I have to assume it was meant to be. I've wanted to walk right up to you and embrace you, to tell you the truth, but I was still a coward. I decided to leave Riften instead of facing your anger. I said my goodbyes to Delvin and I headed for Solstheim... as far from the truth as I could get.
I'm sorry, my daughter. I'm sorry for never being there to hold you at night or to protect you from whatever hardships you endured. One day, I hope you'll make the journey to Solstheim and visit your father.
Glover Mallory
String.Dest:
<font face='$HandwrittenFont'> 我亲爱的女儿蓝宝石：
我写这封信时心情十分沉重。多年来我一直在想该如何告诉你这些，每次我都想着应该与你当面说清楚。但我为自己的行为而羞愧，希望有一天你能试着原谅我。不管你做什么，都不要责怪戴尔文。他并不知情。
在我加入公会后不久，就被派去抢劫一个停在离天际边界不远的小村庄里的商队。我比预计早到了一天，决定在一个养猪场的谷仓里睡一晚。第二天早上醒来，我见到了这辈子见过最美丽的女人，她走进来干活儿。甚至她看到我的时候也没有害怕……就在那一瞬间，我觉得我俩坠入爱河了。我和那女人在村里住了一年，直到她怀孕了。然后，我就像个懦夫一样……逃跑了。我是个贼，不想当农夫。那太可怕了。
整整十五年，我都没有勇气回那个村子去。等我最终下定决心回去时，已经太迟了。村子被强盗洗劫烧毁。所有人死走逃亡。包括你……我唯一的孩子。
多年后，当你被布林乔夫录用时，我简直难以相信自己的眼睛。你和你妈妈长得一模一样。我立马就知道是你。天知道你是如何从洗劫中幸存下来的，又为什么会追随我的脚步，但我必须想这就是命中注定。我想直接走过去，抱抱你，告诉你真相，但我是个懦夫。我决定离开裂谷城，我不想面对你的愤怒。我和戴尔文告别，去了索瑟海姆……离真相越远越好。
对不起，女儿。我很抱歉没能在夜晚陪伴你或是保护你免受苦难。希望有一天，你能来索瑟海姆看看你爸。
葛洛文·马洛里

String.Source: The people of the Skaal have been freed from the control they have been under, but in doing so a hideous creature has been released. It must be defeated before the Skaal are truly safe.
String.Dest: 斯卡尔人已经从精神控制中解放出来，但这过程也放出了一个可怕的怪物。必须打败它，确保斯卡尔人的安全。

String.Source: After searching the Temple of Miraak, Frea and I found a strange book. When I read it, I was transported to some other realm, where I learned that Miraak is still alive and plans to return to Solstheim. I need to tell Frea what I've seen.
String.Dest: 在探索米拉克神殿后，芙瑞雅和我发现了一本神秘书籍。我读了后就被传送到了另一个位面，在那里了解到米拉克还活着，还计划着回归索瑟海姆。我得告诉芙瑞雅我的所见所闻。

String.Source: After delving through the Temple of Miraak, Frea and I have discovered a strange book deep within secret chambers. Frea believes it has a connection to what's happening on Solstheim. I should read it to learn what secrets it holds.
String.Dest: 在探索米拉克神殿后，芙瑞雅和我在密室深处发现了一本神秘书籍。我该读一读看看其中有什么奥秘。

String.Source: I was successful in defeating Miraak after he attacked me, but he has disappeared. I should search his temple to learn more about what's going on here.
String.Dest: 在成功击退了现身攻击我的米拉克后，他却消失了。我得搜查他的神殿，深入了解这里发生了什么。

String.Source: I found a group of Rieklings who took over Thirsk Mead Hall, but chose not to help them.
String.Dest: 我发现了一群占据了瑟斯科蜜酒大厅的瑞克灵，但我没帮他们的忙。

String.Source: After freeing the Skaal from the magic controlling them, Storn, the shaman, has asked me to seek out a member of their tribe who is still missing. I need to look to the temple near the center of Solstheim to find Frea and make sure she is safe.
String.Dest: 在把斯卡尔人从控制他们的魔法中解放出来后，萨满斯托恩请我去找一名仍然下落不明的族人。我得去索瑟海姆中央的神殿去找芙瑞雅，确保她的安全。

String.Source: I found a group of Rieklings who took over Thirsk Mead Hall. I did a number of favors for them, and their Chief attacked me, thinking I was attempting to usurp him. After I defeated him, the rest of the tribe welcomed me, and they made me their chief.
String.Dest: 我发现了一群住进了瑟斯科蜜酒大厅的瑞克灵。在帮了他们很多忙后，他们的酋长认为我有意取代他，并袭击了我。在击败他后，剩下的部族欢迎我并推举我成为酋长。

String.Source: I've found a group of Rieklings who seem to have taken up residence in Thirsk Mead Hall. Somehow, their chief has learned how to speak intelligently, and after I did a number of small favors for the tribe, asked me to help them kill the group of Nords who formerly lived in the hall.
String.Dest: 我发现了一群住进了瑟斯科蜜酒大厅的瑞克灵。他们的酋长不知怎的学会了有灵性的对话。在帮了他们部族几个小忙后，他们又请我帮忙除掉以前住在大厅里的那群诺德人。

String.Source: After an attempt on my life, I've tracked the people responsible to the isle of Solstheim, where someone named Miraak has a temple near the center of the island. It's time to find out what's going on.
String.Dest: 在有人试图取我性命后，我追踪嫌疑人来到了索瑟海姆岛，一个叫米拉克的人在岛中央有座神殿。是时候查清出什么事了。

String.Source: I've found a group of Rieklings who seem to have taken up residence in Thirsk Mead Hall. Somehow, their chief has learned how to speak intelligently, and has asked me to assist them with a number of small favors.
String.Dest: 我发现了一群住进了瑟斯科蜜酒大厅的瑞克灵。他们的酋长不知怎的学会了有灵性的对话，并请我帮他们几个小忙。

String.Source: After finding Frea, and searching the Temple of Miraak, we discovered a strange book. When I read it, I was transported to some other realm and learned that Miraak is still alive and claims he'll return soon. Frea has suggested we take this information back to her village, and see what can be done to stop Miraak.
String.Dest: 在探索米拉克神殿后，芙瑞雅和我发现了一本神秘书籍。我读了后就被传送到了另一个位面，在那里了解到米拉克还活着，他宣称很快就会回归。芙瑞雅建议我们把这个消息带回她村里去，看看能做什么来阻止米拉克。

String.Source:
[pagebreak]

The Lusty Argonian Maid Folio Edition By: Crassius Curio

[pagebreak]

To the Arano Family, When I showed up on your doorstep in Morrowind that day, I expected to be thrown back into the street and cast aside like some sort of vermin. Instead, you offered me into the warmth and safety of your home. For that, I will be eternally grateful. Please, accept this rare Folio Edition of my work as a sign of appreciation for your kindness. Crassius Curio [pagebreak]

Act IV, Scene III, continued Lifts-Her-Tail Certainly not, kind sir! I am here but to clean your chambers. Crantius Colto Is that all you have come here for, little one? My chambers? Lifts-Her-Tail I have no idea what it is you imply, master. I am but a poor Argonian maid. Crantius Colto So you are, my dumpling. And a good one at that. Such strong legs and shapely tail. Lifts-Her-Tail You embarrass me, sir! Crantius Colto Fear not. You are safe here with me. Lifts-Her-Tail I must finish my cleaning, sir. The mistress will have my head if I do not! Crantius Colto Cleaning, eh? I have something for you. Here, polish my spear. Lifts-Her-Tail But it is huge! It could take me all night! Crantius Colto Plenty of time, my sweet. Plenty of time. END OF ACT IV, SCENE III [pagebreak]

ACT VII, SCENE II, CONTINUED Lifts-Her-Tail My goodness, that's quite a loaf! But how ever shall it fit my oven? Crantius Colto This loaf isn't ready for baking, my sweet. It has yet to rise. Lifts-Her-Tail If only we could hurry that along. How would I accomplish such a task? Crantius Colto Oh, my foolish little Argonian maid, you must use your hands. Lifts-Her-Tail You wish me to knead the loaf? Here? Crantius Colto Of course. Lifts-Her-Tail But what if the mistress catches me? Your loaf was meant to satisfy her appetite. Crantius Colto Don't fret, my delicate flower. I'll satisfy the mistress's cravings later. Lifts-Her-Tail Very well, but I'm afraid my oven isn't hot enough. It could take hours! Crantius Colto Plenty of time, my sweet. Plenty of time. END OF ACT VII, SCENE II
String.Dest:
[pagebreak]

元气满满的阿尔贡女仆 对开本版 克拉修斯·库里奥 著

[pagebreak]

致阿拉诺家族： 想当初我在晨风驻足府前，本以为会被扔回街上，弃之如害虫。却承情迎入，免于寒冷不安。此情不忘。谨以此珍品对开本，以谢厚谊。 克拉修斯·库里奥 [pagebreak]

第四幕，第三场，接续前幕 尾巴翘翘：当然不是这样啊，亲爱的先生！我来这里只是要打扫您几位的房间呀。 克兰修斯·科尔托：你到我的房间来光是想着做这些而已吗，小可爱？ 尾巴翘翘：我真的不晓得您在暗示些什么呀，主人。我只是区区一介可怜兮兮的阿尔贡女仆哇。 克兰修斯·科尔托：你当然是啦，我的甜心。你在这方面可出落得个不得了呢。多健美的双腿，还有这匀称的尾巴。 尾巴翘翘：您让我好害羞呀，先生！ 克兰修斯·科尔托：怕什么。你和我在一起很安全。 尾巴翘翘：我必须得把清洁工作干完呀，先生。要不然太太可非把我的头拧下来不可啊！ 克兰修斯·科尔托：清洁工作啊，嗯哼？我有些活儿要给你干。来这儿，擦亮我的矛。 尾巴翘翘：可它真是太大了！这得花上我一整晚时间呢！ 克兰修斯·科尔托：时间有的是呢，我亲爱的。时间有的是。 第四幕，第三场终 [pagebreak]

第七幕，第二场，接续前幕 尾巴翘翘：老天啊，这真是好一条不得了的面包啊！但它要怎样才能塞进我的烤箱里呢？ 克兰修斯·科尔托：这条面包还没准备好进去烘培呢，亲爱的。它还没发酵呢。 尾巴翘翘：要是我们能一起弄快点儿就好啦，我该怎么完成这样的任务呢？ 克兰修斯·科尔托：哦，我的小笨蛋阿尔贡女仆啊，你必须要使上你的小手呀！ 尾巴翘翘：您要我揉发这条面包吗？就在这儿？ 克兰修斯·科尔托：当然啦。 尾巴翘翘：可万一太太逮着我得怎么办呢？您的面包本是要填满她的胃口噢。 克兰修斯·科尔托：别烦恼了，我的小娇花儿。稍后我会满足夫人的食欲啦。 尾巴翘翘：真好呢，但我恐怕烤箱不够烫，可能要花上好几个小时哟！ 克兰修斯·科尔托：时间有的是呢，我亲爱的。时间有的是。 第七幕，第二场终

String.Source: I've found Miraak's temple on Solstheim. Now I need to find out why people are trying to kill me on his behalf.
String.Dest: 我发现了索瑟海姆岛上的米拉克神殿。现在要查清为什么有人打他的旗号袭击我。

String.Source: I've found Frea, a warrior from the Skaal Village on Solstheim. She is also searching the Temple of Miraak, looking for answers. We've agreed that we need to find where Miraak's power is coming from.
String.Dest: 我找到了索瑟海姆斯卡尔村的战士，芙瑞雅。她也在调查米拉克神殿寻找答案。我们同意必须找出米拉克力量的源头。

String.Source:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont">My dearest Selina,
This is my last letter. I don't know if you'll ever get any of them, but I'll keep them on me in case I'm ever found. Something happened here, Selina. It was horrible. Something's happened at the Red Mountain but I can't describe it. It's as if hundreds of Oblivion gates opened at once at its summit and it's spitting fire and death in all directions. Fort Frostmoth has been completely destroyed. The walls crumbled like loose dirt and the land is on fire. Everything around me smells of ash and of death. I don't know where anyone is. I've been trapped in one of these lower sections of the fort and I don't expect to be rescued anytime soon. I miss you, Selina. I want to hold you and the children in my arms and tell you that everything is going to be fine, but I don't think that will ever happen. Give my love to Siricus and Atia for me. Tell them their father died bravely defending the Empire, so they can hold their heads high when they speak of me one day. And you my love, when you close your eyes at night, think of me so my spirit can finally come home.
Yours always and forever,
Maximian Axius 3 Sun's Dawn 4E 05
String.Dest:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont">我最亲爱的赛琳娜：
这是我最后一封信了。我不知道你能否拿到，但我会把它们带在身上，以防别人拿去。这里发生的一切太可怕了，赛琳娜。红山出事了，但我没法描述。它顶端似乎有数百个湮灭之门一起打开，向四面八方喷出火焰与死亡。霜蛾堡已经被毁了。墙壁像松软的泥土一般倒塌，遍地着火。我周围都是灰烬与死亡的味道。我找不到任何人。现在我被困在堡垒一个较低的区域，但恐怕没希望获救了。我很想你，赛琳娜。我想把你和孩子们抱在怀里，告诉你们一切都会好起来的，但已经不可能了。代我向西里古和阿提亚问好吧。告诉他们，他们的父亲是为保卫帝国英勇牺牲的，这样等他们有朝一日说起我时，能够昂首挺胸。还有你，我的爱人，当你晚上闭上眼睛，就想想我，这样我的灵魂也就能回家了。
你的挚爱，
迈席米安·阿克修斯 4E5年，日晓月3日

String.Source:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont">My dearest Selina,
It's been a difficult day. General Carius ordered us to help the laborers shore up the walls since they're starting to show their age. It was back-breaking work, but without the extra support, those walls wouldn't stand up to a siege. Some of the men are grumbling about the task, but I don't agree with them. The general knows what's best for Fort Frostmoth and I would follow him to Oblivion and back if he asked. There's a supply ship due on Solstheim in a few weeks and I hope to send you all these daily letters I've been writing. I hope you enjoy reading them as much as I enjoy writing them. I miss you Selina, and I can't wait to see you when my time on Solstheim is through.
Yours always,
Maximian Axius 20 Evening Star 4E 04
String.Dest:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont">我最亲爱的赛琳娜：
真是艰难的一天。卡里乌斯将军命令我们帮劳工加筑老化的城墙。工作很繁重，但如果不进行额外加固，这些城墙就无法抵御进攻了。有人开始抱怨这项任务，但我不同意。因为将军知道怎样才是对霜蛾堡最有利的做法，只要他一声令下，我愿追随他往返湮灭。几周后就会有补给船要到索瑟海姆来，希望能把我日常写给你的信捎回去。但愿你能如我爱写它们一样喜欢读。我很想念你，赛琳娜，真迫不及待想在从索瑟海姆退役之后回去找你。
你的挚爱
迈席米安·阿克修斯 4E4年，夜星月20日

String.Source:
You can ride a dragon by using the Bend Will Shout to tame it temporarily. When the dragon is landed, press [Activate] to mount. When you're riding the dragon, press [Activate] to dismount, or press it again to cancel a dismount command while the dragon is landing.
You can order the dragon to fly to anywhere you can fast travel - just bring up the map and fast travel to that marker.
You can lock onto a target while riding the dragon. Press [Jump] to toggle Target Lock mode on and off. While you're in Target Lock mode, your current target will appear on the interface below the compass.
While in Target Lock, you can cycle forward and backward through the list using [1] and [2]. If the current target dies or gets too far away, the Target Lock will automatically switch to the next target. If there are no more targets, you will automatically leave Target Lock.
You can also command the dragon to attack your current target by pressing [Sneak].
String.Dest:
你可以使用“意志屈服”来暂时驯服巨龙。龙落地后，按[Activate]骑乘。当你骑乘巨龙时，按[Activate]可以取消骑乘，或者在龙着陆时再次按下取消下来的命令。
你可以命令龙飞到任何你可以快速旅行到达的地方，只需打开世界地图，快速旅行到那个标记位置。
你可以在骑龙时锁定目标。按下[Jump]切换到目标锁定模式。当你处于该模式时，你当前的目标将显示在指南针下方的界面上。
在目标锁定模式中，你可以使用[1]和[2]来切换目标。如果当前目标已死亡或者距离目标太远，目标锁定会自动切换到下个目标。如果没有其他目标，将自动解除目标锁定模式。
你还可以按[Sneak]命令龙攻击你当前的目标。

String.Source: A warrior in ebony mail approached me in <alias=StartCity>. He has challenged me to fight him at his last vigil, wherever that is.
String.Dest: 一位身穿黑檀护甲的战士在<alias=StartCity>街头找上了我。他向我发起挑战，让我去他的终末守灵处与他决斗。

String.Source: I have defeated the Ebony Warrior. May his soul find rest in Sovngarde.
String.Dest: 我击败了黑檀战士。希望他的灵魂能在松加德安息。

String.Source:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont">My dearest Selina,
Euphemius was killed this morning by one of those awful Rieklings. We were escorting a supply wagon from Fort Frostmoth to Raven Rock when a war party of those bastards ambushed us from the cliffs. We fought them off, but poor Euphemius was impaled by one of their spears. The healers at the fort couldn't do anything for him, and I watched him slip away as I held his hand. I don't know how much longer I can stand being here. My loyalty to the Empire, and the strong words General Carius delivers to us at each morning muster are the only things keeping me going. The supply ship should arrive tomorrow, and I promise to give the quartermaster my letters so they can finally be sent home. Give my love to the children.
Yours always,
Maximian Axius 11 Morning Star 4E 05
String.Dest:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont">我最亲爱的赛琳娜：
尤菲米厄斯今早被一个可怕的瑞克灵杀死了。我们护送补给车从霜蛾堡去鸦石镇时遇到了那群混蛋从悬崖上伏击了我们。我们虽然打退了它们，但可怜的尤菲米厄斯被它们的长矛刺穿了。堡里的治疗师对此无能为力，我握着他的手，看着他就这么走了。我不知道自己还能再这里待多久。我对帝国的忠诚以及卡里乌斯将军每天早晨集合时对我们的训话是我坚持下去的唯一动力了。补给船明天就到了，我跟军需官说好把信送回家了。代我向孩子们问好。
你的挚爱，
迈席米安·阿克修斯 4E5年，晨星月11日

String.Source:
You can ride a dragon by using the Bend Will Shout to tame it temporarily. When the dragon is landed, press [Activate] to mount. When you're riding the dragon, press [Activate] to dismount, or press it again to cancel a dismount command while the dragon is landing.
You can order the dragon to fly to anywhere you can fast travel - just bring up the map and fast travel to that marker.
You can lock onto a target while riding the dragon. Press [Jump] to toggle Target Lock mode on and off. While you're in Target Lock mode, your current target will appear on the interface below the compass.
While in Target Lock, you can cycle forward and backward through the list using [Left] and [Right]. If the current target dies or gets too far away, the Target Lock will automatically switch to the next target. If there are no more targets, you will automatically leave Target Lock.
You can also command the dragon to attack your current target by pressing [Sneak].
String.Dest:
你可以使用“意志屈服”来暂时驯服巨龙。龙落地后，按[Activate]骑乘。当你骑乘巨龙时，按[Activate]可以取消骑乘，或者在龙着陆时再次按下取消下来的命令。
你可以命令龙飞到任何你可以快速旅行到达的地方，只需打开世界地图，快速旅行到那个标记位置。
你可以在骑龙时锁定目标。按下[Jump]切换到目标锁定模式。当你处于该模式时，你当前的目标将显示在指南针下方的界面上。
在目标锁定模式中，你可以使用[Left]和[Right]来切换目标。如果当前目标已死亡或者距离目标太远，目标锁定会自动切换到下个目标。如果没有其他目标，将自动解除目标锁定模式。
你还可以按[Sneak]命令龙攻击你当前的目标。

String.Source:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont">My dearest Selina,
The supply ship due in Solstheim hasn't arrived yet and no one knows what's happened to it. I'll keep writing these letters in hopes that they can be delivered to you one day. It's awful being isolated on Solstheim like this, but General Carius keeps telling us we need to maintain Fort Frostmoth for the good of the Empire. I believe what he's saying only because he's never led us down the wrong path in the past, but I'm wondering if anyone on the Imperial Council even gives a damn about this pile of rock. Four soldiers have died in the last two years at Fort Frostmoth. It almost seems as though the Empire takes us all for granted and expects us to sit out here and get chipped away at like the rock inside the mines. My posting here can't end soon enough.
Yours always,
Maximian Axius 1 Sun's Dawn 4E 05
String.Dest:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont">我最亲爱的赛琳娜：
原定抵达索瑟海姆的补给船并未到达，且原因至今不明。我将继续写信，希望有一天你能收到。像这样被孤立在索瑟海姆很可怕，但卡里乌斯将军一直在告诉我们，为了帝国的利益我们要保护好霜蛾堡。我愿意相信他只是因为他过去从未把我们引入歧途，但我也在想帝国议会是否真的有人在意过这堆石头。过去两年，四名士兵死于霜蛾堡。帝国似乎认为我们这样是理所当然的，希望我们就这样待在这里，就像矿场里的岩石一样被凿开。我在这里的服役恐怕不能很快结束了。
你的挚爱，
迈席米安·阿克修斯 4E5年，日晓月1日

String.Source: A warrior in Ebony tried to talk to me on the streets of <alias=StartCity>.
String.Dest: 一位身穿黑檀护甲的战士在<alias=StartCity>街头找我谈话。

String.Source: Continue along the path, don't tread where you've been.
All men must die, often by their own means.
Stay your course. To idle is to die.

Adril Arano has sent me to Ashfallow Citadel to destroy Vendil Ulen and whatever forces he's amassed there. I've found the two Redoran Guard I was supposed to meet there dead. I'll have to carry the fight into the ruins alone.
String.Dest: 阿德尔·阿拉诺派我去灰落堡垒消灭文德尔·乌伦还有他在那里聚集的武装力量。我发现原本该和我在那儿碰头的两个瑞多然卫兵死了。我只能自己一人迎接废墟里的战斗了。

String.Source: A sacrifice will bring you closer to that which you seek.
String.Dest: 牺牲会让你更接近所求。

String.Source: <font face="$HandwrittenFont"> Look Meryn I'm not arguing that these towers are falling to pieces, but I think you are exaggerating about the planks falling ou--------
String.Dest: <font face="$HandwrittenFont"> 听着，梅林，我不是说塔要塌了，但我觉得你说木板要倒的说法有点太夸弓……

String.Source: I read the Black Book called "Filament and Filigree", and found myself in Hermaeus Mora's realm of Apocrypha. I should uncover the knowledge hidden here, or read the book again to escape.
String.Dest: 我读了名为《金丝银线》的黑暗经书，发现自己来到了赫麦尤斯·莫拉的领域：异典。我应该揭开潜藏于此的知识，或者重读一遍经书逃出去。

String.Source: I read the Black Book called "The Sallow Regent", and found myself in Hermaeus Mora's realm of Apocrypha. I should uncover the knowledge hidden here, or read the book again to escape.
String.Dest: 我读了名为《蜡黄摄政》的黑暗经书，发现自己来到了赫麦尤斯·莫拉的领域：异典。我应该揭开潜藏于此的知识，或者重读一遍经书逃出去。

String.Source: I uncovered the hidden knowledge contained in the Black Book "The Hidden Twilight".
String.Dest: 我揭开了潜藏于黑暗经书《隐匿暮光》中的知识。

String.Source: I uncovered the hidden knowledge contained in the Black Book "Untold Legends".
String.Dest: 我揭开了潜藏于黑暗经书《未叙传说》中的知识。

String.Source: I uncovered the hidden knowledge contained in the Black Book "The Sallow Regent".
String.Dest: 我揭开了潜藏于黑暗经书《蜡黄摄政》中的知识。

String.Source: I read the Black Book called "The Winds of Change", and found myself in Hermaeus Mora's realm of Apocrypha. I should uncover the knowledge hidden here, or read the book again to escape.
String.Dest: 我读了名为《变幻之风》的黑暗经书，发现自己来到了赫麦尤斯·莫拉的领域：异典。我应该揭开潜藏于此的知识，或者重读一遍经书逃出去。

String.Dest: 我揭开了潜藏于黑暗经书《金丝银线》中的知识。

String.Dest: 我读了名为《隐匿暮光》的黑暗经书，发现自己来到了赫麦尤斯·莫拉的领域：异典。我应该揭开潜藏于此的知识，或者重读一遍经书逃出去。

String.Source: I read the Black Book called "Untold Legends", and found myself in Hermaeus Mora's realm of Apocrypha. I should uncover the knowledge hidden here, or read the book again to escape.
String.Dest: 我读了名为《未叙传说》的黑暗经书，发现自己来到了赫麦尤斯·莫拉的领域：异典。我应该揭开潜藏于此的知识，或者重读一遍经书逃出去。

String.Dest: 我揭开了潜藏于黑暗经书《变幻之风》中的知识。

String.Source: Hermaeus Mora taught me the final Word of Power of the "Bend Will" Shout, killing Storn in the process. With this Shout, I can tame Miraak's dragon and force him to fly me to Miraak's temple in Apocrypha.
String.Dest: 赫麦尤斯·莫拉教会了我“意志屈服”龙吼的最后一句力量之语，同时杀死了斯托恩。有了这个龙吼，我就能驯服米拉克的龙，强迫他载我去异典的米拉克神殿。

String.Source: Hermaeus Mora taught me the final Word of Power of the "Bend Will" Shout, killing Storn in the process. I now have what I need to return to Apocrypha and face Miraak.
String.Dest: 赫麦尤斯·莫拉教会了我“意志屈服”龙吼的最后一句力量之语，同时杀死了斯托恩。回异典去挑战米拉克所需的一切都已完备。

String.Source: Hermaeus Mora taught me the final Word of Power of the "Bend Will" Shout, killing Storn in the process. I used the Shout to tame Miraak's dragon and flew to his temple, where I fought and killed him. Miraak will never threaten Solstheim again.
String.Dest: 赫麦尤斯·莫拉教会了我“意志屈服”龙吼的最后一句力量之语，同时杀死了斯托恩。我使用了这个龙吼驯服了米拉克的龙，飞到了他的神殿，在此与他战斗并将其斩杀。米拉克再也不能威胁索瑟海姆了。

String.Source: Hermaeus Mora taught me the final Word of Power of the "Bend Will" Shout, killing Storn in the process. I used the Shout to tame Miraak's dragon and flew to his temple, where he was waiting for me.
String.Dest: 赫麦尤斯·莫拉教会了我“意志屈服”龙吼的最后一句力量之语，同时杀死了斯托恩。我使用了这个龙吼驯服了米拉克的龙，飞到了他的神殿，他正在此等我。

String.Source: I've stumbled across Crescius Caerellius inside of Raven Rock Mine. He's convinced that the mines of Raven Rock contain a secret important enough for him to risk his own life to discover. He appears to have more to tell, so I should listen to what he has to say if I want to learn more.
String.Dest: 我在鸦石镇矿场里偶然遇见了克雷修斯·凯利乌斯。他确信矿井里有个值得他冒生命危险去揭开的秘密。看来他还有很多事情没说，如果我想了解更多最好听听。

String.Source:
Board the vessel Northern Maiden docked at Raven Rock. Take it to Windhelm, then begin your search. Kill the False Dragonborn known as before reaches Solstheim.
Return with word of your success, and Miraak shall be most pleased.
String.Dest:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont"> 搭上停靠在鸦石镇的北方少女号，到风舵城去，然后开始搜查。在伪龙裔<Alias=Player>到达索瑟海姆前杀了<Alias.Pronoun=Player>。
带着成功的消息回来，米拉克会为此高兴的。

String.Source:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont"> I grow impatient with your lack of progress. If you cannot break the smith, I will be forced to find a more capable interrogator. I expect your next report to contain more encouraging results.
- A
String.Dest:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont"> 我对你毫无进展之举已不耐烦。如果你不能从铁匠那里套出口供，我就只能换一个更得力的审讯人员了。希望你下份报告能有更令人鼓舞的消息。
-A

String.Source: While assisting Captain Veleth at the Attius Farm, I've discovered a strange note. I should give this note to Captain Veleth in order to learn more about the origins of the ash spawn threat to Raven Rock.
String.Dest: 我在阿提乌斯农场帮助维黎斯队长时，找到了一个奇怪字条。我应该把它带给维黎斯队长，了解更多关于鸦石镇的灰烬怪威胁来源的信息。

String.Source: I met a group of Nords who had been exiled from their former home in Thirsk Mead Hall, but chose to help the Rieklings keep them permanently evicted.
String.Dest: 我遇到了一群被瑞克灵赶出瑟斯科蜜酒大厅的诺德人。但我选择了帮助瑞克灵，确保他们被永远的赶走了。

String.Source:
Eydis stayed back on the gears to hold off those metal monsters, but she hasn't shown up so I'm going to press on and hope for the best. Unfortunately, my progress has been blocked by a gate that I can't pry open. Following the pipes, it appears that the gate is controlled by the large boiler in the center of the room. I'm going to try shooting the resonators with an arrow to see if that opens the gate. [pagebreak] [pagebreak] My first attempt didn't work very well. Each of the resonators appeared to route steam when I shot them and things on the boiler started to glow blue, which seemed like a good sign. Unfortunately, once I shot a few of the resonators, the lights started turning orange, all the steam vented from the boiler, and several dwarven constructs came out and attacked me. Still, the Visage of Mzund must be here so I'm going to try again. [pagebreak]

I'm sure there has to be a trick to it. Perhaps if I can fill the central boiler with just the right amount of pressure. I seem to recall

[pagebreak]

that three parts lit up when I hit the resonator on the left. I'm going to try from the right this time and keep track as I go.
String.Dest:
爱迪斯留在齿轮上阻止那些金属怪物，但她始终没跟上来，所以我只能继续前进了，但愿有个好结果吧。可惜，我被一扇撬不开的门挡住了。顺着管道走，大门似乎是由房间中央的大锅炉控制的。我要试试用箭射击共振器，看看能不能把门打开。 [pagebreak] [pagebreak] 第一次尝试不太成功。射击时每个共振器似乎都在输送蒸汽，锅炉上的东西开始发出蓝光，似乎是个好兆头。不幸的是，有时击中某个共振器，灯就变橙色了，所有蒸汽从锅炉里一起释放，好几只矮人机械跑出来攻击我。不过，“姆祖德之容”一定在此，我必须再试试。 [pagebreak]

我确定这里有窍门。也许我能给中央锅炉适当加压。我记得

[pagebreak]

当我敲击左面的共振器，有三个亮了起来。这次我从右边开始，一直追踪下去。

String.Source:
[pagebreak]

Ahzidal's Descent by Halund Greycloak

[pagebreak]

n the days beyond memory, when men first walked the lands of Skyrim, there arose in the city of Saarthal a great enchanter. As a boy, his gift for magic and artifice had been evident to his tutors. As a man, his skill surpassed them all. And finding nothing more to learn among his kin, he left wife and child, and set out to train under the elven masters. A year became two, then three. And when finally his path led him back to Saarthal, he found only ruins: for the elves had sacked the city, and all that lived there were dead or gone. Amid the ashes, in the smoldering ruins of his home, he swore a terrible oath of vengeance. And from that comes the name the legends give him: Ahzidal, the embittered destroyer. Alone, he could do nothing. And so, he bided his time, delving deeper into his art than any before him. From the Dwemer, he learned the seven natures of metal and how to harmonize them. From the Ayleids, the ancient runes and dawn-magic even the elves had begun to forget. Among Falmer and Chimer and Altmer he traveled, taking what he could from each, and all the while plotting how he might turn that knowledge against them. Finally, word reached him of Ysgramor and his Companions, newly-arrived from Atmora. For three days and nights, he rode north, and met them as they made landfall on the icy coast near the ruins of Saarthal, which the elves had fortified against them. He offered the Companions his service, and all he had produced in his years of labor. And with Atmoran steel imbued with his enchantments, the elves fell before them, and at last he had his revenge. But he was not content. His craft had become his life, and his hunger for knowledge still gnawed at him, driving him to delve ever deeper. At long last, he exhausted the lore of the elves, but it was not enough. He sought the secrets of Dragon-runes, and won for himself a seat among their high priests, but it was not enough. And at length, he turned his gaze to the planes of Oblivion, and found there both power and madness. Some say he ventured there, never to return. Others, that he was betrayed by his fellow Dragon Priests, and killed, or driven into hiding in the ruins beneath his beloved Saarthal. Among the Skaal of Solstheim, it is said he fled to their island, and was sealed in the depths of Kolbjorn Barrow, together with the last of his relics. But that is the tale, as it was told among the bards of Winterhold. Whatever the truth, the legend of Ahzidal was intended as a warning: in pursuit of perfection, one must take care that the pursuit itself does not become all-consuming.
String.Dest:
[pagebreak]

阿兹达尔的堕落 “灰斗篷”哈伦德 著

[pagebreak]

在记忆之外的时代，当人们首次踏足天际，萨瑟尔出了一位伟大的附魔师。当他还是个孩子时，他在魔法和技艺方面的天赋就已经展现在一众导师面前。及至成人，技术已经超越了所有人。在亲族那里，他已经无甚可学，于是告别妻儿，到精灵大师们的门下学习。 一年两年，直至三年。当他重回萨瑟尔，他只看到一片废墟，精灵洗劫了城市，所有人死走逃亡。在自己家的灰烬中，他发出了可怕的复仇誓言。传说中他的名字就来源于此：阿兹达尔，意为痛苦的毁灭者。 但孤掌难鸣，他只能等待时机，比前人更加深入地钻研技艺。他从锻莫处学会了金属的七种性质，以及如何使之互相协调；从亚历德处学会了古老的符文还有连精灵都开始遗忘的黎明魔法。他旅行于伐莫、凯莫和傲特莫之间，尽可能地从每个人身上获取知识，同时又策划着如何以彼之道还施彼身。 最后，他得知了刚刚从阿特莫拉而来的伊斯格拉默与其一众战友的消息。他飞马北上，赶了三天三夜的路，在精灵筑起防御工事的萨瑟尔遗迹附近的冰封海岸与他们相遇。他向战友团提供服务，以及多年的辛劳所得。精灵们被灌注了他魔法的阿特莫拉钢铁征服，他终于完成了自己的复仇。 但他并不满足。他的技艺已经成为了他的生命，他对知识的渴望仍然折磨着他，驱使他更加深入地钻研。终于，他穷尽了精灵知识，却仍心有不足。他开始寻求龙族符文的奥秘，并获得了一个高级祭司的席位，可仍旧没能让他满足。最后，他把目光转向湮灭，发现那里既有力量也有疯狂。 有人说，他前往湮灭冒险，最后一去不返。也有人说，他被龙祭司同僚出卖害死，或者被驱赶到了他心爱的萨瑟尔下面的废墟中隐藏起来。在索瑟海姆的斯卡尔人口中，他逃到了这座岛上，并于他最后的遗物一同封印在寇泊琼古坟的深处。 但这些终究只是冬堡吟游诗人间流传的故事。无论真相如何，阿兹达尔的传说都是一个警告：在追求完美极致的过程中，必须注意不被追求本身耗尽自己的一切。

String.Source: <font face="$HandwrittenBold" size="18"> Meet me here at midnight. </font>
String.Dest: <font face="$HandwrittenFont" size="18"> 午夜时分这里碰面。 </font>

String.Source: I met Wulf Wild-Blood, who told me about his brother Torkild running off many years ago. Wulf died before I could help him learn the truth about his brother.
String.Dest: 我邂逅了“野血”沃弗，他告诉我他的兄弟托基尔德很多年前失踪了。在我帮他找出真相前，沃弗就死了。

String.Source: I met Wulf Wild-Blood, who told me about his brother Torkild running off many years ago. Wulf's worst fears of his brother proved true, unfortunately. I found Torkild wandering Solstheim, out of his mind. I killed him before he could hurt me, and should tell Wulf the sad news.
String.Dest: 我邂逅了“野血”沃弗，他告诉我他的兄弟托基尔德很多年前失踪了。不幸的是他对兄弟遭遇的担心成真了。我发现托基尔德在索瑟海姆上失去理智地游荡，并在他伤害我之前杀了他。我应该告诉沃弗这个悲伤的消息。
召唤探求者

String.Source: I cleansed the sacred Stones that Miraak had corrupted. Solstheim is free of his influence except for the Tree Stone, which remains imprisoned inside his new Temple.
String.Dest: 我净化了被米拉克腐化的圣石。除了仍被禁锢在他的新神殿中的树石外，索瑟海姆不再受他的影响了。

String.Source: After I destroyed Miraak's control of the Wind Stone, Storn has asked me to free the remaining sacred Stones using my "Bend Will" Shout.
String.Dest: 在我打破了米拉克对风石的控制后，斯托恩要我用“意志屈服”龙吼将剩下的圣石解放出来。

String.Source:
<font face="$HandwrittenBold"> My dear Usha, we can make it work! Do not worry about my father - he will grow to accept you in time. So will my mother. And my sister.
Oh Usha, I only ask that you write and let me know you are all right. I have convinced father to look past your lack of wealth and see you for who you really are.
Come back home. No need to worry. I'll be waiting for you, my love.
- Monesa </font>
String.Dest:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont"> 亲爱的乌夏，我们会成功的！不用担心我父亲，他迟早会接受你的。我母亲也会的。还有我妹妹。
哦，乌夏，我只求你写信给我报个平安。我已经说服了父亲不要在意你的贫穷，看到你真正的为人。
回家吧。不用担心。我会一直等你的，亲爱的。
--莫妮萨 </font>

String.Source: While in Beast Form, your attacks do <50>% more damage, but you also take <50>% more damage.
String.Dest: 在野兽形态下，你的攻击造成<50>%额外伤害，但也会受到<50>%额外伤害。

String.Source: While in Beast Form, your health regenerates.
String.Dest: 在野兽形态下，你的生命会不断恢复。

String.Source: Increases the duration of your Howls by <25>%.
String.Dest: 提升<25>%嚎叫效果持续时间。

String.Source:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont"> I hope this message finds you quickly, <Alias=Player>. We've completed the initial excavation, but have run into some unexpected difficulties. Come as soon as you can.
- Your partner, Ralis </font>
String.Dest:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont"> <Alias=Player>， 希望你能够尽快收到此信。我们已经完成了初步的挖掘工作，但遇到了预料外的困难。尽快来一趟吧。
--你的合伙人，拉里斯 </font>

String.Source: I've found a note on one of the cultists who attacked me. Perhaps it has some clue as to what prompted them to come after me.
String.Dest: 我在其中一个攻击我的教徒身上找到了一张字条。也许这上面说了是什么让他们冲我来的。

String.Source:
[pagebreak]

Deathbrand A Pirate's Tale

[pagebreak]

aknir Death-Brand was dying. For Garuk Windrime, ship's quartermaster, it was unthinkable. His grandfather had served under Haknir, nigh on sixty years before, and even then he was a legend among the pirates of the north. "The King of Ghosts," they called him, as eternal and pitiless as the sea he sailed. To Garuk, who had seen him charge into battle, clad in armor of gleaming Stalhrim like the kings of old, his twin swords scything men like grass, Haknir was practically a god. But none feared Haknir more than his own crew. They knew his rages, his fits of madness, how he delighted in torture and murder for its own sake. And there were even darker rumors: Some said he fed upon the blood and souls of those he killed to extend his unnatural life. Some thought him a Daedra, loosed upon the mortal world. And others said he owed his life and power, his armor and swords, to a pact with Dagon, prince of destruction. And the seal of that pact was the terrible wound that scarred his face, never to heal - the Deathbrand, which no man could look upon without flinching. All these things ran through Garuk's mind as he took his place on deck at the head of the crew, exchanging a curt nod with Thalin, the ship's helmsman and his chief rival. By sundown, he thought, one of them would be captain. The other would be dead. When Haknir finally emerged from his cabin, the crew fell silent. He looked frail, his voice raspy. But even so, he had a presence about him. As he looked over his men, the most brutal murderers ever to ply the northern seas, not one could meet his gaze. At last he sighed. "You wish to know who will be my successor, and how my share of the treasure shall be divided." That was the question, but even so, there were murmurs of protest. Haknir cut them off. "All these years, I have looked for one who was worthy to take my place, or strong enough to take it from me. Not one of you even comes close. And so none of you shall have it." He extended his hand. "In Dagon's name, I place a curse upon my armor, and my swords. This ship, and all it carries. Until the day when one of you can best me in combat, you shall have not a single coin." He looked up at them. "Be grateful I have left you with your lives." Garuk and Thalin shared a single glance. Had anyone else said such a thing, there would have been mutiny. A hundred treasure-mad pirates against one old man. But this was Haknir. The crew was silent. Haknir threw a map at Garuk's feet. "Garuk, take a longboat, and bury my armor in the places I have marked. Thalin, we sail to my tomb, where you shall leave me with my gold. Then burn your ships, and do as you will. I am your captain no more." And with that, he turned and stalked back to his quarters. At daybreak, Garuk took his leave, and set out in a longboat with three of his men. They landed on a shoal to the north of Solstheim, at the place Haknir had marked, made camp, and began to dig. But already, greed stirred in Garuk's heart. Time and again, he glanced at the iron-bound chest they had brought with them. The old man was gone, perhaps already dead. His orders, foolish. That night, Garuk pried open the chest and drew out the helm within. The Stalhrim shimmered in the moonlight. It was time. Time for a new King of Ghosts to rise. He placed the helmet on his head. And he screamed. And it is said you can hear that screaming still, on moonlit nights, on a rocky shoal off the northern coast of Solstheim. [pagebreak] Postscript- This story is one of the last in the "Haknir Saga," the tales surrounding the life and adventures of the legendary pirate king Haknir Death-Brand. How much of it is actually true, if indeed any of it is true, I leave to the reader's discretion.

- Artise Dralen House Redoran Scribe
String.Dest:
[pagebreak]

死印 海盗传说

[pagebreak]

“死印”海科尼尔快死了。 对于船上的军需官“风霜”戈鲁来说，这简直难以想象。大概60年前，他的祖父就跟随海科尼尔，即便那时，他也是北方海盗中的传奇人物。人送外号，“幽灵之王”，一如他航行的大海一样永恒而无情。戈鲁从目睹他如古代国王一样身穿闪光的魔冰盔甲、挥舞双剑如割草般杀伤敌人，简直就是神明。 但没有人比这些船员更害怕海科尼尔。他们知道他的暴怒、癫狂，以及是如何以折磨和杀戮为乐。甚至还有更黑暗的谣言说，他以自己所杀之人的血与灵魂为食，以延长他那不正常的寿命。有人觉得他是被释放到人间的迪德拉。还有人说他的生命与力量，还有盔甲和刀剑，都得益于他与毁灭之王大衮的契约。而这契约的印记，就是他脸上那道永远无法愈合的可怕伤疤—死亡烙印，无人能够毫无畏惧地直视于它。 所有这些都在戈鲁脑海中闪过，他站在船头，与舵手、也是他的主要竞争者沙凛简单地点了点头。他想，到了日落，他们之一就会成为船长。而另一个则会死。 当海科尼尔终于走出船舱时，船员们陷入了沉默。他看上去十分虚弱，声音嘶哑。但即便如此，他身上仍然带着一股气质。他俯视着自己的手下，这些北海之上最残忍的杀人犯们，却没有一个敢与他的目光相对。最后，他叹了口气。 “你们想知道谁能继承我的位子，而我又要如何分配那份财宝。” 这确实就是问题所在，但即便如此，仍有人议论纷纷。海科尼尔打断了他们。 “这些年来，我一直在寻找配得上我这位置的人，或者足够强大的人直接来取而代之。可你们甚至没人能够接近。所以你们谁也得不到它。” 他伸出手：“以大衮之名，我诅咒我之盔甲刀剑。还有这艘船，和它所承载的一切。除非有一天你们中有人能够在战斗中打败我，否则你们一分钱也拿不到。”他抬头看向他们。“感激我留了你们一命吧。” 戈鲁和沙凛对视一眼。要是换别人说这话，马上就得火并哗变。一百个疯狂寻宝的海盗对付一个老人简直轻而易举。可这是海科尼尔。船员们都沉默了。 海科尼尔把一张地图扔到戈鲁脚边。“戈鲁，找个小艇，把我的盔甲埋到我标记的地方。沙凛，船往我的坟墓开，把我和我的金子都留在那儿。然后你们就把船烧了，之后就随你们的便吧。我不再是你们的船长了。”说完，他转身大步走回自己的房间。 拂晓，戈鲁告辞，带着三个人坐着小艇出发了。他们在索瑟海姆北部的浅滩登陆，在海科尼尔标记的位置扎营挖掘。 但贪婪已经在戈鲁心中激起怒火。他一次次瞥向带来的铁皮箱子。老头子走了，或许已经死了。还听他的，蠢。 当晚，戈鲁撬开箱子，取出了头盔。魔冰在月光下闪光。时机已到。是时候让新的幽灵之王崛起了。他把头盔戴在了头上。 随后尖叫起来。 据说，每当月光之夜，索瑟海姆北部海岸的岩石浅滩上，仍能听到尖叫声。 [pagebreak] 后记： 本故事是“海科尼尔传奇”的最后一章，整个系列围绕传奇的海盗王“死印”海科尼尔的生活与冒险展开。至于有多少是真，只能留给读者自己判断。

--阿迪斯·德拉伦 瑞多然家族抄事

String.Source: I've stumbled across a dark elf in combat for his life with strange, ashen beings at the Attius Farm outside of Raven Rock. He appears outnumbered, and he's requested I lend him a helping hand.
String.Dest: 我在鸦石镇外的阿提乌斯农场遇到了一个暗精灵，他正为人身安全和一群奇怪的灰烬生物作战。看来他寡不敌众，并请求我出手帮助他。

String.Source: <font face="$HandwrittenFont"> I hope you're not angry, but I may need some more resources. Come around when you have time. </font>
String.Dest: <font face="$HandwrittenFont"> 希望你不会生气，但我可能需要更多资金。有时间就来一趟吧。 </font>

String.Source: I've been attacked by a group of cultists. I need to see if they have any evidence of what prompted this assault.
String.Dest: 我被一群教徒攻击了，得看看他们身上有没有引发了这次袭击的证据。

String.Source:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont"> I don't want you to panic, but I need to see you at the barrow. Quickly.
- Ralis </font>
String.Dest:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont"> 我本不打算惊动你，但你还是得来古坟一趟。快点。
-拉里斯 </font>

String.Source:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont"> Find <alias=player> and get my money. I don't care how you do it, or what you do with <alias.PronounObj=player>. Just get my gold!
--M.
String.Dest:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont"> 找到<alias=player>把我的钱拿回来。我不关心你怎么做，或者把<alias.PronounObj=player>怎么样。赶快把我的钱拿回来！
--M.

String.Source: I helped Elynea repair the withered tower of Tel Mithryn by planting taproots in the wall that had been soaked in the headwaters of Harstrad river.
String.Dest: 我把浸泡过哈斯垂德河源水的直立根种在了墙里，帮艾琳娜修好了泰尔·米斯瑞恩的枯萎塔楼。

String.Source: Targets take <15> points of frost damage to Health and Stamina, and have a small chance of being frozen solid.
String.Dest: 目标的生命与耐力受到<15>点寒霜伤害，并有小几率被冻住。

String.Source:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont"> I am closer to solving the mystery of these ruins! I have found references in ancient Dwemer texts that call Kagrumez the site of a set of trials, a training ground of sorts. This is thus far unprecedented in my research.
This could explain why that gate is so unmovable - the proper trial has not been completed. It is possible nobody has opened this gate since the days of the Dwemer! What treasures of the ancient world lie in wait behind these doors?
The pattern on the gate seems to hold the solution to unlocking it. It bears resemblance to the console in the center of the chamber, but what does it mean? There are nine holes that I assume are to be filled, but with what? If only I had the means to fund more expeditions to the other Dwemer ruins of Solstheim. They surely hold the keys to opening the locked gate. Even if I had the means to start the trials, I surely have not the means to complete them.
I can't wait for more funding! I am going to have to recruit those who will act on the promise of treasure alone. Surely there are those as excited about this find as I am.
-Raleth Eldri </font>
String.Dest:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont"> 距离解开这遗迹的谜题又近了一步！我从古代的锻莫文本里发现，所谓卡格鲁梅兹是一系列试炼地点，是某种意义上的训练场。这一发现在我的研究中是前所未有的。
这就可以解释那扇门为什么不能移动了，因为适当的试炼还没有完成。很可能自从锻莫时代以来就没有人打开过这扇门！这些门后会有什么古代世界的宝藏？
门上的图案似乎是解开它的方法。和房间中心的控制台有些相似之处，但到底是什么含义？我猜这九个洞应该填上，但要用什么？如果我有足够的钱去索瑟海姆的其他锻莫遗迹探险就好了。那里肯定保存着打开那扇锁住的门的钥匙。即使我有办法开启试炼，也肯定没办法完成。
我等不及要更多钱了！我不得不招募那些只凭宝藏承诺就行动的人。肯定有人和我一样对这个发现感到兴奋。
——瑞莱斯·艾德利 </font>

String.Source:
<font face="$HandwrittenBold" size="19"> "The single richest treasure trove in all of Solstheim" they said. Bah. Looks like this place was cleaned out centuries ago. The Stalhrim might be worth something, but my pickaxe ain't even good enough to chip it.
Still, I can't shake the feeling that there's something I'm missing. There's an odd draft in this room - secret passage, maybe?
I've locked myself in until those bandits are good and gone. I suppose I'll keep looking. Not much else I can do. </font>
String.Dest:
<font face="$HandwrittenFont" size="19"> 他们说什么：“这是索瑟海姆最富的宝藏。”我呸。几百年前就被洗劫一空了。魔冰倒是值钱，可我的鹤嘴锄又凿不动。
不过，我还是总有一种错过了什么东西的感觉。这房间里有股奇怪的过堂风，难道是有秘道？
我把自己锁在里面，等那些强盗先走。我会继续寻找。我能做的事不多了。 </font>

String.Source: When wielded with Bloodscythe, absorbs <15> magicka and and has a chance to dispel magical defenses.
String.Dest: 与血镰一起使用时，吸收<15>点魔力，并有几率驱散魔法防御。

String.Source: By following an ancient treasure map, I found the armor of Haknir Death-Brand, the legendary pirate king. A key contained in one of the chests led me to Gyldenhul Barrow, where I defeated Haknir's ghost and claimed his swords and gold as well.
String.Dest: 照着一张古代藏宝图的指引，我找到了传奇海盗之王“死印”海科尼尔的护甲。其中一个宝箱里有打开吉尔登胡尔古坟的钥匙，我在那里击败了海科尼尔的鬼魂，并且夺走了他的剑和金币。

String.Source: When wielded with Bloodscythe, absorbs <15> magicka and and has a chance to dispel magical defenses.
String.Dest: 与血镰一起使用时，吸收<15>点魔力，并有几率驱散魔法防御。

String.Source: When wielded with Soulrender, absorbs <15> health and has a chance to weaken enemy armor.
String.Dest: 与魂引一起使用时，吸收<15>点生命，并有几率削弱敌方护甲。

String.Source: A band of pirates followed an ancient treasure map to Haknir's Shoal, where they discovered a chest at the location marked on the map. By following the map, perhaps I can find the rest of the treasure they sought.
String.Dest: 一群海盗参照一张古代藏宝图来到了海科尼尔的浅滩。他们在地图上标记之处发现了一个宝箱。照这张地图的指示，或许我能找齐他们在追寻的其他宝物。

String.Source: At Haknir's Shoal, a band of pirates uncovered an ancient treasure chest containing a single piece of Stalhrim armor. How did they know to dig in this remote spot?
String.Dest: 在海科尼尔的浅滩上，一群海盗挖出了一个装有一件魔冰护甲的远古宝箱。但他们怎么知道要跑到这么偏僻的地方来挖宝？

String.Source: When wielded with Soulrender, absorbs <15> health and has a chance to weaken enemy armor.
String.Dest: 与魂引一起使用时，吸收<15>点生命，并有几率削弱敌方护甲。

String.Source: By following an ancient treasure map, I found the armor of Haknir Death-Brand, the legendary pirate king. A key contained in one of the chests led me to Gyldenhul Barrow, where I found a room filled with Haknir's cursed gold. But touching it triggered the curse: the doors behind me have sealed.
String.Dest: 照着一张古代藏宝图的指引，我找到了传奇海盗之王“死印”海科尼尔的护甲。其中一个宝箱里有打开吉尔登胡尔古坟的钥匙，我在那里发现了一个房间，装满了海科尼尔那些受过诅咒的黄金，当我触碰时引发了诅咒：身后的门封死了。

String.Source: By following an ancient treasure map, I found the armor of Haknir Death-Brand, the legendary pirate king. In addition to his armor, one chest also contained the key to Gyldenhul Barrow. Could it be connected to Haknir's treasure?
String.Dest: 照着一张古代藏宝图的指引，我找到了传奇海盗之王“死印”海科尼尔的护甲。除此之外，宝箱里还有一把打开吉尔登胡尔古坟的钥匙。这会与海科尼尔的宝藏有关吗？

String.Source: By following an ancient treasure map, I found the armor of Haknir Death-Brand, the legendary pirate king. A key contained in one of the chests led me to Gyldenhul Barrow, where I found his tomb and one of his cursed swords. But when I touched the sword, his ghost appeared and attacked.
String.Dest: 照着一张古代藏宝图的指引，我找到了传奇海盗之王“死印”海科尼尔的护甲。其中一个宝箱里有打开吉尔登胡尔古坟的钥匙，我在那里发现了他的坟墓，还有一把受过诅咒的剑。当我触碰那把剑时，他的鬼魂现身并袭击了我。

String.Source: ;stage set when all fights are completed
String.Dest: ;stage set when all fights are completed

String.Source: You need an Ancient Nordic Pickaxe to use this.
String.Dest: 你需要一把古诺德鹤嘴锄才能使用它。

String.Source: Once a day, take on the mighty aspect of a dragon, delivering colossal blows, with an armored hide, and more powerful shouts.
String.Dest: 每天一次，显现巨龙的强力化身，给予更大伤害、提升护甲、赋予自身更强大的龙吼。

String.Source: Talvas has asked me to destroy an Ash Guardian that is running amok on the Tel Mithryn grounds.
String.Dest: 塔瓦斯让我去摧毁一个在泰尔·米斯瑞恩地界上发狂的灰烬守卫。

String.Source: All spells cost <15>% less to cast, and magicka regenerates <100>% faster. Chance on hit to spawn a tentacle explosion.
String.Dest: 所有法术消耗降低<15>%，魔力恢复速度提升<100>%。命中时有几率产生触手爆炸。

String.Source: I destroyed the Ash Guardian that Talvas had accidentally summoned. He was quite grateful.
String.Dest: 我摧毁了塔瓦斯意外召唤的灰烬守卫。他很感激我。

String.Source: I've encountered an Ash Guardian that is running amok on the Tel Mithryn grounds.
String.Dest: 我碰上了一个在泰尔·米斯瑞恩地界上发狂的灰烬守卫。

String.Source: Your Voice power is corrupted by the influence of Hermaeus Mora.
String.Dest: 你的龙吼力量被赫麦尤斯·莫拉腐化了。
%.0f 点额外能力点数已返还。

String.Source: Spend one Dragon Soul to remove your Two-Handed perks and regain %.0f perk point?
String.Dest: 是否花费一个龙魂移除双手武器的额外能力，并返还%.0f额外能力点数？
